what: Learning 85 — A “docfix sweep” under strict TDD: turn a single named example bug into an evidence-based scope via a read-only audit FIRST; the discriminating RED test for a roxygen @examples is STRUCTURAL (“the example for F invokes F()”, extracted from man/<F>.Rd via tools::Rd2ex) — NOT “the example runs” (broken examples often still run); the build-equivalent is actually RUNNING the corrected examples; and new tests for already-correct functions are an honest degenerate cycle (hand-derive expected values + teeth-check by perturbation) (S87, fix 3 @examples defects + backfill tests for kinshipMatrixToKValues/getAncestors)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: () [scoping “sweep”, AUDIT whole surface read-scope AskUserQuestion evidence-based, blind — classify false positives] handoff named ONE example bug (getPedDirectRelatives), “sweep” implies breadth. cheap read-audit (awk @examples→def block every @exported function; flag whose example never calls <fn>() found 5 suspects → 3 real defects (getPedDirectRelatives severe: called getLkDirectRelatives + omitted required ped; cumulateSimKinships called createSimKinships; getIdsWithOneParent called sibling helpers) 2 FALSE positives (summary.nprcgenekeeprErr / print.summary.nprcgenekeeprErr — S3 methods correctly demonstrated via summary()/auto-print GENERIC; heuristic can’t see dispatch). Also confirmed firsthand “±” candidates kinshipMatrixToKValues/getAncestors zero direct test references (grep -rln tests/testthat/). Lesson: candidate framed “sweep” optional “±” tail, run breadth audit PRE-RED (read-) present findings — real defects, false positives, coverage gaps — scope AskUserQuestion options (per phase-gate contract, scope SEPARATE pre-RED question). audit converts “fix one named bug” deliberate owner choice across true surface; recompute-don’t-inherit applies scope . (b) [discriminating RED test documentation/example defect STRUCTURAL, behavioral — “example F invokes F()” — broken examples frequently still RUN] “documented example runs without error” RED driver : getPedDirectRelatives’s buggy example called getLkDirectRelatives(ids=...) fails SOFT NULL (getDemographics tryCatch’d — LabKey, error), two called real sibling functions — three “ran” HEAD. property actually encodes defect “@examples F contains call F(”. Test extracting example GENERATED man/<F>.Rd via tools::Rd2ex(rd, =tmp) grepl(paste0(fn,"("), code, fixed=TRUE) (use fixed=TRUE — \( “unrecognized escape” needless ; require ( mere mention comment doesn’t satisfy ). Add NEGATIVE assertion severe case (getPedDirectRelatives must call getLkDirectRelatives(). Locate .Rd testthat::test_path("..","..","man",paste0(fn,".Rd")) skip_if(!file.exists, ...) runs load_all/dev (man/ exists ’s RED→GREEN) skips gracefully installed package (man/) — project’s accepted context-gated-test idiom. Lesson: roxygen-example fix TDD, meaningful RED test structural (example-invokes---function), read generated .Rd; “example runs” weak guard wrong examples routinely run. real “run?” check build-equivalent . (c) [docs build-equivalent RUNNING corrected examples; document() must scope-checked; coverage tests correct functions honest degenerate cycle; user-facing help defect NEWS-worthy] GREEN roxygen edits + devtools::document(), SAFEGUARDS “build-equivalent” documentation actually execute fixed example (tools::Rd2ex→source(, local=new.env()), assert error) — R CMD check ’s proof new examples work, complementing structural test. Guard document() scope: git status must show intended man/*.Rd (exactly 3) — restore unrelated roxygen-version drift, confirm NAMESPACE untouched (new exports example-change). two NEW test files (test_kinshipMatrixToKValues.R, test_getAncestors.R) exercise already-correct shipped functions, pass start — declare honest degenerate RED→GREEN: hand-derive every expected value independently (symmetric kinship matrix’s coefficients; recursive sire--dam lineage c("C","","B","D","","B") explicit ptree), TEETH-CHECK perturbing value throwaway run confirm fails — proving assertions bite. Build small deterministic fixtures -test (hand-built ptree, 3×3 named + 2×2 unnamed matrix) rather leaning dataset structure (note: lacy1989Ped, E FOUNDER → use F createPedTree integration check). news-vs-changelog: getPedDirectRelatives example user-facing HELP defect (?getPedDirectRelatives showed wrong function) → — NEWS Documentation bullet (edit NEWS.Rmd, rmarkdown::render NEWS.md, verify diff EXACTLY new bullet, reflow churn) plus CHANGELOG entry (NEWS historically lists example/roxygen fixes). Right-size note: correctly SOLO even ultracode “” — docs+tests sweep gated interactive TDD breadth fan-; Workflow theater (project methodology + Learning 80c/84 govern right-size call). Reflexes: [audit whole @export surface read-scoping “sweep” — awk @examples→def block fn whose example never calls ][classify S3-method false positives (example calls GENERIC → dispatch)][confirm “±” coverage gaps firsthand via grep -rln tests/][scope SEPARATE pre-RED AskUserQuestion built audit findings][structural RED test = example-invokes---fn, extracted man/.Rd via tools::Rd2ex][grepl(…, fixed=TRUE) — avoid \( escape, require (][add NEGATIVE assertion wrong-fn call][skip_if(!file.exists(man/…)) runs load_all, skips installed pkg][docs build-equivalent = actually RUN fixed examples (Rd2ex→source, expect error)][scope-check document(): intended .Rd change, NAMESPACE untouched, restore roxygen-version drift][tests already-correct fns = honest degenerate cycle: hand-derive expected values + teeth-check perturbation][build deterministic -test fixtures, dataset-shape-dependent ones][lacy1989Ped E founder — use F ancestors][user-facing help defect → NEWS+CHANGELOG; render NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md, diff = new bullet][declare-phase-every-response][gate-every-transition-via-AskUserQuestion][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][Phase-3E N/docs+tests — state , don’t silently skip][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: “docfix sweep ± tests” candidate strict TDD — first run read-audit across exported functions turn one named bug evidence-based scope (real defects vs dispatch false positives vs coverage gaps) pose pre-RED scope question; write RED test STRUCTURAL “example-invokes---function” check extracted generated man/*.Rd (“example runs”, broken examples pass); make docs build-equivalent actual execution corrected examples; treat new tests already-correct functions honest degenerate cycle hand-derived, teeth-checked expectations; scope-check document(); route user-facing help-example correction NEWS CHANGELOG.

what: Learning 86 — A version-string HOUSEKEEPING fix is still a real RED→GREEN under strict TDD, and the load-bearing insight is RED-test SCOPE: an un-scoped grepl over as.character(appUI()) was a FALSE PASS at HEAD because the app already renders the dynamic version elsewhere (the title bar via getVersion()), so a positive assertion MUST be scoped to the specific element (the About-panel region); choose the root-cause DYNAMIC fix (reuse the existing exported getVersion() helper, which reads the version from DESCRIPTION) over a hard-coded literal so it can’t drift again (S88, replace stale Version 1.0.8 in R/appUI.R:230 + CLAUDE.md)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text:
trap fix. deliverable looked trivial (stale Version 1.0.8 string carried since S56), two non-obvious facts made genuine TDD slice. (1) rendered-UI assertion needs element-scope lies. first RED rendered whole appUI() HTML asserted grepl("Version ", html) (positive) plus !grepl("Version 1.0.8", html) (negative). positive PASSED HEAD — FALSE PASS — appUI.R:47 ALREADY shows getVersion() (dynamic, build date) title bar, "Version 1.1.0.9000" present regardless broken tab; negative teeth. Fix: scope assertions panel slicing rendered HTML GeneKeepR heading forward (regexpr + substring(html, , + 200L)) guard heading exists; assertions fail right reason HEAD. Lesson: asserting rendered page contains token one place, scope assertion element test, RED passes wrong reason — false-positive RED dangerous missing test. (2) Reuse existing source--truth helper. grep getVersion( showed package already exported getVersion(date=) wrapping packageVersion("nprcgenekeepr"); dynamic GREEN p(paste("Version", getVersion(date = FALSE))) (reuse, DRY) — fresh inline utils::packageVersion(...). owner chose dynamic hard-coded via separate pre-RED AskUserQuestion (permanent--recurring: kill drift class root). Phase-3E build-time UI string: version set UI CONSTRUCTION, via server reactivity, AppDriver boot INSTALLED app + body-HTML check (

Version 1.1.0.9000

present, 1.0.8 absent) sufficient — tab navigation needed (tabPanels render DOM load, just hidden). news-vs-changelog: displayed app version user-facing → NEWS (Shiny-application bullet; render NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md, diff = new bullet) CHANGELOG. Right-size: correctly SOLO even ultracode “” — two-edit fix gated interactive TDD breadth fan-(project methodology + Learning 80c/84/85 govern). Reflexes: [housekeeping ≠ trivial — scope real RED→GREEN][scope rendered-UI assertion element test — whole-page grepl FALSE-PASSES token appears elsewhere (title bar’s getVersion())][slice element heading: regexpr(“GeneKeepR”) + substring(…,,+200L), guard heading exists][grep existing source--truth helper writing inline one — getVersion(date=FALSE) reads packageVersion][prefer dynamic root-cause fix literal re-drifts (permanent--recurring)][body-edit UI fn → document()/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION change; utils already imported, helper already exported][Phase-3E build-time UI string = AppDriver body-HTML check INSTALLED app; tabPanels DOM load, navigation needed][news-vs-changelog: displayed version user-facing → ][declare-phase-every-response][separate pre-RED approach AskUserQuestion choice changes test+impl (dynamic vs hard-coded)][gate-every-transition-via-AskUserQuestion][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: stale-string / version-display housekeeping fix Shiny UI strict TDD — write RED test renders real UI function SCOPES positive+negative assertions specific element (don’t grep whole page; duplicate token elsewhere false-pass), reuse existing exported version/source--truth helper dynamic root-cause fix instead literal, verify live string via AppDriver body-HTML check installed app, route user-facing displayed version NEWS CHANGELOG.

what: Learning 87 — Fixing a “noisy warning” bug under strict TDD: REPRODUCE firsthand FIRST to establish it is noise-not-data-loss (which decides the whole fix shape); suppress it SURGICALLY with withCallingHandlers + message-matched invokeRestart("muffleWarning") (never blanket suppressWarnings), and prove the surgery with teeth that an UNRELATED warning still propagates; consolidate across all readers via a shared @noRd internal helper (the package’s non-exported convention → document() no-op); and test a server-internal closure structurally while putting behavioral teeth on the exported readers (S89, fix #4 — “incomplete final line found by readTableHeader” on files with no trailing newline)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: shape fix decided reproduction, bug report. Issue #4 reported "incomplete final line found readTableHeader '...0.txt'" Shiny text upload. naive reading (“final line incomplete → row dropped”) lead file-normalization fix. Reproducing firsthand (write CSV trailing newline, read , capture warnings + nrow) showed opposite: read.table/read.csv read every row correctly (nrow == 3, last row intact) merely emit cosmetic warning. single fact decided everything — fix suppress warning, recover row — recompute-don’t-inherit applies BUG’S ACTUAL BEHAVIOR designing anything. (1) Surgical, blanket. mechanism withCallingHandlers(expr, warning = function(w) (grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning")) — muffles one warning lets every warning (genuinely malformed file, config problem) reach caller. suppressWarnings() wrong: hides real problems . discriminating RED teeth “surgical” test UNRELATED warning("...") STILL propagates (expect_warning) — strongest RUNTIME proof, Phase-3E, getFocalAnimalPed’s unrelated “configuration file missing” warning still fires incomplete-final-line one gone. “warnings ” assertion untestable reader (config warning legitimately fires without DB) semantically wrong (mean blanket suppression). (2) Lazy-eval makes wrapper trivial. muffleIncompleteFinalLine(expr) takes expr unevaluated promise; withCallingHandlers(expr, ...) forces handler registered (idiom suppressWarnings uses) — substitute/eval gymnastics needed. (3) Consolidate via shared @noRd internal helper. owner chose (separate pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion: -readers-via-helper vs named-readers vs app-) root-cause scope — wrap read four sites (getPedigree, getGenotypes → read.table; getFocalAnimalPed → read.csv; modInput.R’s readDataFile text + CSV branches) one helper, fixing reported upload path. @noRd package’s established convention non-exported internals (confirmed via getRecordStatusIndex/readExcelPOSIXToCharacter — man/*.Rd, referenced tests nprcgenekeepr:::), document() -op (NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION churn) — scope-check git status confirm. (4) Test server-internal closure structurally; put behavioral teeth exported readers. readDataFile closure defined inside modInputServer — callable without standing Shiny session — practical seam deparse(body(modInputServer)) grep body references helper (honest structural lock, weaker behavioral). behavioral teeth live functions directly callable: helper unit tests getPedigree/getGenotypes (external deps, return data + assert warning + rows preserved). Phase-3E exported-reader fix = call readers INSTALLED package -newline input (warning gone, rows preserved) PLUS control file trailing newline (still reads cleanly — regression) PLUS surgical proof (unrelated warning still propagates). Issue left OPEN pending owner confirmation (standing close---owner-confirmation rule — owner picked #4 fix yet accepted fix). Right-size: correctly SOLO even ultracode “” — one-bug fix gated interactive TDD breadth fan-; Workflow theater (project methodology + Learning 80c/84/85/86 govern). Reflexes: [REPRODUCE “noisy warning” bug firsthand FIRST — noise-vs-data-loss decides whole fix shape (suppress warning vs recover row)][recompute-don’t-inherit applies bug’s actual behavior, just report’s wording][surgical muffle = withCallingHandlers + message-matched invokeRestart(“muffleWarning”), grepl(…, fixed=TRUE)][NEVER blanket suppressWarnings — hides real problems][teeth “surgical”: UNRELATED warning must STILL propagate (RED test + Phase-3E runtime proof)][DB/config-dependent reader assert ABSENCE SPECIFIC warning, zero warnings — config-missing warning legitimately fires without DB][withCallingHandlers(expr,…) lazy-eval promise idiom — handler registered force, substitute/eval needed][consolidate across readers via ONE shared @noRd internal helper — package’s non-exported convention → document() -op, scope-check git status][server-internal closure (readDataFile modInputServer) → structural deparse-grep seam; behavioral teeth directly-callable exported readers + helper][non-exported internals referenced tests nprcgenekeepr:::][scope SEPARATE pre-RED AskUserQuestion: -readers-via-helper vs named-readers vs app-; owner picks root-cause symptom][Phase-3E = call INSTALLED readers -newline + control -newline file (regression) + surgical proof][leave issue OPEN pending owner confirmation][declare-phase-every-response][gate-every-transition-via-AskUserQuestion][news-vs-changelog: file-reading bug fix user-facing → (render NEWS.Rmd→NEWS.md, diff = new bullet; curly-quote/en-dash conversion expected, churn)][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: fix “noisy harmless warning” bug strict TDD — first reproduce firsthand confirm noise (data preserved) fix suppression recovery; suppress surgically withCallingHandlers + message-matched invokeRestart("muffleWarning") (never blanket suppressWarnings), write RED teeth unrelated warning still propagates; consolidate across every affected reader one shared @noRd internal helper (document() stays -op); test server-internal closure structurally (deparse-grep) putting behavioral teeth directly-callable exported functions; Phase-3E exercise installed readers bad input, clean control input, confirm surgery left warnings intact.

what: Learning 88 — Writing a BEHAVIORAL test for an already-shipped fix: DE-RISK THE FIXTURE before the mechanism — validate firsthand that your fixture actually reproduces the bug condition in plain code, or you burn cycles blaming the app for a fixture artifact; the "incomplete final line" warning only fires for SMALL files (the readTableHeader scan must reach the unterminated line) AND is a console-only artifact unobservable in the assembled Shiny app, so the warning-suppression teeth live in an in-process shiny::testServer test (tiny fixture, withCallingHandlers) while the browser E2E owns end-to-end data integrity; the un-muffle teeth round-trip is what exposes a toothless assertion (S90, behavioral upload-path tests for #4 + close #4)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: cycle-burning mistake, lesson prevents . deliverable “behavioral E2E modInput upload path” #4 (S89 covered structurally). built browser E2E testServer test convenient fixture — -trailing-newline copy ExamplePedigree.csv (3694 rows) — un-muffle teeth round-trip kept coming back GREEN (warning-absence assertion passed even muffle removed). spent SIX browser diagnostics + multiple install/load_all round-trips hypothesizing assembled Shiny app swallows deferred warning (doesn’t), finally checking FOUNDATIONAL assumption never validated: -trailing-newline ExamplePedigree even emit warning plain read.csv? . Root cause: read.table/read.csv emit "incomplete final line found readTableHeader" header / type-detection scan (~first lines) actually REACHES unterminated final line — .e. SMALL files (≤ ~5 lines, condition user hit 0.txt); 3694-row file’s scan never reaches end, warning. Every “app swallows ” conclusion FIXTURE ARTIFACT. Swapping TINY 4-line fixture (header + 3 founder rows ExamplePedigree) made warning fire teeth appear instantly. Lesson: building teeth (browser get_logs, testServer withCallingHandlers) fixture, prove FIRST — plain code, Shiny — fixture reproduces exact failure condition (: withCallingHandlers around plain read.csv(fixture) capturing message). De-risk fixture, just observation mechanism. teeth round-trip won’t go RED means EITHER assertion toothless fixture doesn’t trigger bug — check fixture first; cheaper, common culprit. (1) [muffle’s effect browser/log-observable assembled app → warning-suppression teeth belong -process testServer test, browser E2E] Two independent reasons browser can’t teeth-test muffle: "incomplete final line" warning console-artifact (never reaches DOM, app$get_logs() surface modInput getData path), realistic-size fixture doesn’t even fire . browser expect_false(grepl("incomplete final line", logs_blob)) TAUTOLOGY (green without muffle — un-muffle round-trip proved ). vehicle CAN observe shiny::testServer (-process, warn reaches R-level handler): drive modInputServer’s getData simulated upload — session$setInputs(fileType=, fileContent="pedFile", minParentAge="2.0", pedigreeFileOne=data.frame(name=, size=, type=, datapath=fixture)) withCallingHandlers(session$setInputs(getData=1), warning=\(w){ capture; invokeRestart("muffleWarning") }) — assert captured warnings exclude "incomplete final line" (read module’s session$returned$qcSummary()$records/$errors data-integrity half). Teeth PROVEN round-trip: un-muffle read.table+read.csv sites modInput.R, load_all, run → blocks RED (warned=TRUE); git checkout → green. Lesson: fix suppresses R-process-internal signal (console warning) never reaches browser, browser E2E give teeth — put suppression teeth testServer (pure-helper) test signal observable, let browser E2E carry end--end user-visible behavior. **(2) [browser E2E owns end--end data integrity, read via TOP-LEVEL consumer modInput’s QC outputs SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs] modInput QC outputs (qcSummaryUI, cleanedDataTable, downloadCleaned) live non-active nested tabs dataInput-mainTabs, Shiny suspendWhenHidden=TRUE leaves empty / download link disabled href="" — neither set_inputs(\dataInput-mainTabs`=…)JS anchor-click reliably un-suspends app's bslib BS4 + (broken) shinyBS JS.** robust count signal TOP-LEVEL navbar consumer: upload+ready,navigate_to_tab(app, “Pedigree Browser”, “Pedigree”)(proven sibling mechanism) materializes pedigree, #pedigree-pedigreeTablerendersof 3,694 entrieson ACTIVE pane (DataTables formats count comma — match“3,694 entries”, not3694).data-ready=trueis set server-side imply hidden-tab outputs rendered. **Lesson:** read count/state Shiny module whose outputs sit hidden (suspended) nested tabs, fight tab-suspension — assert via top-level navbar tab CONSUMES data active pane (samenavigate_to_tab/assert_active_paneidiom e2e suite already trusts). **Right-size:** correctly SOLO even **ultracode ""** — two-file test backfill gated interactive TDD breadth fan-; Workflow theater (project methodology + Learning 80c/84/85/86/87 govern), background Workflow pause theAskUserQuestionphase gates project requires. honest division labor (testServer = warning-suppression teeth; browser = end--end) surfaced owner via mid-GREEN reframeAskUserQuestionrather silently shipping weaker--described test. **Reflexes:** [DE-RISK FIXTURE FIRST — prove plain code (Shiny) fixture reproduces exact bug condition building teeth ][teeth round-trip go RED ⇒ toothless assertion fixture trigger bug — check fixture first, cheaper culprit][read.table/read.csv emit "incomplete final line" SMALL files — readTableHeader scan must reach unterminated final line; large file never warns][console-warning browser/get_logs-observable assembled app → suppression teeth-tested browser][put suppression teeth shiny::testServer (-process, warn reaches withCallingHandlers) TINY fixture; simulate fileInput value 1-row data.frame(name,size,type,datapath)][read module return via session$returned$<reactive>()][prove teeth un-muffling sites → blocks RED → git checkout → green (load_all, install needed)][browser E2E owns end--end: modInput QC outputs SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs (suspendWhenHidden) reliably un-suspend via set_inputs/JS-click bslib+shinyBS — read TOP-LEVEL consumer (Pedigree Browser #pedigree-pedigreeTable) instead][DataTables formats entry count comma — match "3,694 entries"][data-ready=true server-side, imply hidden-tab outputs rendered][surface mid-GREEN scope/teeth reframe owner via AskUserQuestion — silently ship weaker test][close issue owner confirmation][test-→ CHANGELOG ][declare-phase-every-response][gate-every-transition][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. **Apply:** write BEHAVIORAL test already-shipped fix suppresses console-internal signal — first prove plain code chosen fixture actually reproduces failure (de-risk fixture, observation mechanism); recognize console-warning browser-observable, put suppression teeth -processtestServer/pure-helper test (tiny fixture,withCallingHandlers`) let browser E2E carry end--end user-visible behavior read top-level data consumer (module’s suspended nested-tab outputs); ALWAYS run un-muffle teeth round-trip — round-trip won’t go RED alarm catches toothless assertions non-triggering fixtures.

what: Learning 89 — Strict TDD on a DOCS-ONLY fix to a @noRd internal: the only thing RED at HEAD is the documentation TEXT, so the RED driver is a doc-completeness test that reads R/<fn>.R (the S87 tools::Rd2ex(man/<fn>.Rd) pattern is UNAVAILABLE — @noRd generates no man/*.Rd) via test_path("..","..","R","<fn>.R") + skip_if(!file.exists) for the installed context, and SCOPES its assertions to the extracted @return block (not the whole file) so a pre-existing mention in the title/\code{} can’t false-pass; pair it with a behavioral return-contract guard (green at HEAD, honestly classified) so the docs are verified against real behavior (S92, complete fillBins() @return docs — #33)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: doc-text deliverable RED surface — test doc text, scoped. Issue #33 single unfinished roxygen line: #' @return  list two TODO: RMS provide description. strict TDD question “fails fix passes ?” — docs-change answer behavioral test (function already returns right thing; return-contract assertion GREEN HEAD) doc-completeness test. (1) S87 Rd2ex pattern apply @noRd. test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R reads man/<fn>.Rd via tools::Rd2ex — @noRd internal generates man/*.Rd (confirmed: document() -op , scope-check git status). artifact carrying doc SOURCE file R/fillBins.R; read directly via testthat::test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R") (../.. root-relative idiom S87 uses man/) skip_if(!file.exists(src), "R/ source available (installed package)") ’s RED dev (fix lives) inert R CMD check (R/*.R source isn’t shipped). (2) SCOPE assertion @return block, false-passes. fillBins.R’s TITLE line already says list two lists \code{males} \code{females}, naive grepl("males", whole_file) GREEN even TODO still present. discriminating test extracts @return block — find @return line, take following lines next #' @ tag — asserts within : "TODO", \bmales\b/\bfemales\b (word-boundaries males inside females doesn’t satisfy males assertion). 3 assertions go RED HEAD right reason, RED failure output literally prints isolated @return line — proof extractor scoped correctly. (3) Pair behavioral contract guard, honestly classified. test_that asserting expect_named(res, c("males","females")) + expect_type "integer" + expect_length == length(lowerAges) GREEN HEAD — RED driver, contract-LOCK ties now-complete docs real behavior catches future drift (honest-degenerate-cycle classification S87’s backfill tests S89’s nrow guards: declare green--HEAD, don’t pretend drove change). (4) Verification appropriate docs-. document() → NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION churn (@noRd proof); lintr 0; full clean-regression 0/0; Phase-3E genuinely N/— roxygen comment @noRd internal changes runtime behavior, man page even renders — STATE (FM #24 doesn’t apply: ’s build-vs-runtime gap mistake, test suite verification). Style: match package’s existing \describe{\item{name}{...}} @return convention (4 files use , e.g. getPossibleCols.R). Issue left OPEN pending owner confirmation (standing rule). Right-size: correctly SOLO even ultracode “” — one-line doc fix gated interactive TDD breadth fan-; Workflow theater pause AskUserQuestion gates (Learning 80c/84/85/86/87/88 govern). Reflexes: [docs-fix strict TDD ⇒ RED driver doc-COMPLETENESS test, behavioral one (behavior already correct → green HEAD)][@noRd internal ⇒ man/*.Rd ⇒ S87 tools::Rd2ex pattern unavailable; read SOURCE R/<fn>.R via test_path("..","..","R","<fn>.R") + skip_if(!file.exists)][SCOPE doc assertions extracted @return block — pre-existing title/\code{} mention false-passes whole-file grep][extract block: @return line next #' @ tag][use \bword\b word-boundaries males inside females doesn’t satisfy males assertion][pair behavioral contract guard (named/type/length) classify honestly green--HEAD contract-lock, RED driver][document() -op @noRd — scope-check git status zero NAMESPACE/man//DESCRIPTION churn][Phase-3E N/@noRd doc edit — STATE , FM #24 doesn’t apply (build-vs-runtime gap)][match existing \describe{\item{}} @return convention][news-vs-changelog: @noRd internal docs never render user-facing page → CHANGELOG , NEWS][leave issue OPEN pending owner confirmation][declare-phase-every-response][gate-every-transition-via-AskUserQuestion][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: complete/repair documentation @noRd (non-exported) function strict TDD — drive doc-completeness test reads SOURCE file (man/*.Rd, doesn’t exist) scoped relevant roxygen block word-boundary assertions skip_if-installed guard, pair honestly-classified behavioral contract guard, verify via document() -op + lint + full regression, state Phase-3E N/@noRd comment changes runtime behavior.

what: Learning 91 — Before running OR proposing a recurring-shaped task (audit / sweep / inventory), CHECK THE PROJECT’S OWN PROCESS-HISTORY ARTIFACTS AT SCOPING TIME — CHANGELOG.md, docs/audits/, and the recent SESSION_NOTES.md handoffs are cheap and authoritative and tell you whether it was just done: a recurring audit is a DIFF against the last baseline, not a full re-run. S95 ran a full 14-agent / ~483k-token sweep classifying all 14 open issues for “implemented-but-open” when S62 (docs/audits/BACKLOG_STALENESS_AUDIT_2026-06-12.md, 4 days prior) had already classified all 21 then-open issues with the identical question, AND the CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entries (S89/S90/S92) + the S94 handoff already documented the implemented-but-open backlog being drained issue-by-issue (#4/#33/#49). The overlap surfaced only MID-EXECUTION (agents cited S62) and at report-writing (looking up the audits-dir naming convention) — never at scoping. The owner flagged it twice (“why was this in the backlog if already done?”, “you should have been able to check changelog also”). (S95, Learning 90 follow-through audit — 0 new closeable candidates found; the backlog was already drained)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: audit designed catch done--still-listed WORK done--re-listed TASK — tracker-lags-reality failure, one meta-level . decisive miss: AUDIT_WORKSTREAM’s Step 4 “Review Prior Audits” fired late — report-writing, produced nice S62 comparison table — instead SCOPING/Phase 1, collapsed whole sweep 7-issue delta (closures since S62) plus new #49 data point, owner “want full re-run just diff?” fan-. ultracode, token cost explicitly constraint — proposing/running REDUNDANT work judgment defect independent budget (FM #5 helpfulness--volume / FM #21 greenfield-assumption applied process artifacts: acted backlog never audited). (1) cheap pre-checks available orientation. docs/audits/ one ls; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] section top file; recent handoffs already read Phase 0. one three shown work recent -progress. (2) Recurring tasks need baseline check, fresh start. task audit/sweep/inventory-SHAPED (whole-backlog, whole-codebase, whole-corpus), first scoping move “done recently, ’s smallest delta updates ?” — grep process-history artifacts prior run deciding scope. (3) handoff-chain corollary (mirrors Learning 90). Just session implements--leaves-OPEN must carry “impl SXX, OPEN pending close” forward, handoff PROPOSES audit/sweep candidate must cite recent prior run shape (state “none exists”) next session scopes delta rather re-proposing novel. S94’s candidate (2) framed audit “cheap, high-value” without referencing S62 — proximate cause redundancy. (4) Honest recovery beats defended sunk cost. owner questioned overlap, right move plainly (yes, S62 ; yes, checked CHANGELOG docs/audits/ Phase 1), give honest marginal-value accounting (zero — 7 closures + #49 data point + firsthand “second #49” confirmation since S62 — delta paid full sweep), let owner decide keep-vs-trim, rather rationalizing spend. Reflexes: [task audit/sweep/inventory-SHAPED ⇒ SCOPING (Phase 1), ls docs/audits/ + scan CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] + recent handoffs prior run fanning ][recent prior run exists ⇒ scope DELTA since baseline; offer owner diff-option spending full sweep][AUDIT_WORKSTREAM Step 4 “Review Prior Audits” belongs scoping, report-writing][handoff proposes recurring-task candidate must cite recent prior run shape, “none”][redundant work judgment defect even token budget unconstrained — FM #5/#21][overlap owner honest marginal-value accounting; don’t defend sunk cost]. Apply: whenever ’re run — propose handoff — anything classifies/reviews whole set (open issues, files module, citations), first check project’s process-history (docs/audits/, CHANGELOG.md, recent SESSION_NOTES.md) last done; recent, delta say , full sweep baseline stale owner asks . “issue open must need work” assumption negative claim — verify way ’d verify claim: search disproof first. session’s near-miss: asked “#49 open,” (previous turn) answered “real implementation remains / implementation-ready TDD candidate” checking issue surface — sibling-issue states (#47 closed) S86 commit message — concluded “commit implements ” wasn’t last 8 commits 0 comments. unsearched negative. owner said “work closing #49,” recompute-don’t-inherit reflex (applied declaring RED) caught : 2-second grep -rn "ORIP\|orip" R/ tests/ revealed R/shouldShowOripTab.R, conditional appUI.R:177-183 mount, appServer.R:281-292 gate, three test files; git log --diff-filter=-- R/shouldShowOripTab.R dated whole feature S84 b980f998 (“gate ORIP Reporting tab ONPRC-(#49)”). feature DONE — including issue’s flagged “open product fork” (-config case), resolved shouldShowOripTab(center, hasConfigFile) <- isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC") (getSiteInfo() default ONPRC fallback show tab). (1) Implemented--unclosed repo’s default, exception. #4, #33, #49 followed impl--session-X → left-OPEN → administratively-closed--session-Y owner confirmation. deliverable “close #N,” FIRST move recompute: grep/git log implementation, read code firsthand, run tests — decide whether “close” means admin close (common case) real build. Treating “close #N” “build #N” risks duplicating shipped work (scope + volume violation, FM #5/#8). (2) Verify firsthand PUBLIC assertion completeness. close comment asserts world gate correct, re-read predicate + UI gate + server gate re-ran test_shouldShowOripTab.R (5/5), test_modORIPReporting.R (9/9, behavioral — renders real appUI(siteInfo=) HTML ONPRC/SNPRC/-config), test_modSiteConfig.R (30/30) green HEAD — trust S84’s green claim blindly. (3) changed premise owner’s ratify — don’t improvise pivot. owner directed close premise mis-stated (needed implementing), surfaced corrected finding confirmed close path via AskUserQuestion rather silently switching “build” “close.” Owning prior error explicitly part recovery, optional. (4) handoff chain dropped #49. Unlike #4/#33 (whose “left OPEN pending confirmation” carried every subsequent handoff), #49 implemented S84 without recording “done--open,” ~10 handoffs never surfaced — metadata-read looked plausible. Fix: session implements issue leaves OPEN, handoff’s gotchas/suggested-next MUST list “implemented SXX, OPEN pending close,” line must propagate issue closes. Reflexes: [deliverable “close #N” ⇒ recompute FIRST: grep/git log --diff-filter=-- <likely files> implementation assuming needs building][negative claim (“done”, “caller”, “doesn’t exist”) requires positive search, inference metadata (0 comments / recent creation / absent recent git log)][implemented--unclosed repo default (#4/#33/#49) — “close” usually = admin close owner confirmation][verify firsthand (re-read code + re-run tests green) asserting completeness PUBLIC close comment — recompute-don’t-inherit applied prior session’s green claim][mis-statement owner explicitly; confirm changed-premise pivot via AskUserQuestion, don’t improvise ][session implements--leaves-OPEN must carry “impl SXX, OPEN pending close” handoff close][closing issue adds new software work ⇒ new CHANGELOG/NEWS (logged impl time) — [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]][Phase-3E N/pure issue-close — STATE , verify issue transitioned CLOSED][right-size-SOLO operational close even ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: whenever task “close issue #N” ’re claim open issue still needs work — grep + git log implementation FIRST; already shipped (repo’s common case), recompute-verify firsthand (read code, run tests green), confirm close owner premise changed, gh issue close evidence comment citing impl commit + verification, carry implemented--open issues forward handoff.

what: Learning 92 — Re-verifying #37’s “exported-but-app-unused” inventory: (a) compute reachability WITHOUT loading the package — source R/*.R into a throwaway env + codetools::findGlobals (base lib), because the renv project library isn’t materialized in this checkout (pkgload::load_all bootstraps an empty renv and dies); and (b) use findGlobals(f, merge = TRUE), NOT $functions-only — a function passed AS A VALUE to a higher-order call (Map(chooseDate, …) at setExit.R:54) lands in $variables, so a call-position-only graph FALSE-FLAGS it unused. The genuine S97 delta vs S78: both wire-ins discharged (#47 ORIP + #48 getPotentialParents/modPotentialParents, both CLOSED + mounted), the surfaced docfix fixed (S87 2a64770f), the logging island still 0 live callers → 39 unused = 0 wire-in · 39 keep-as-public-API · 0 retire; #37’s actionable surface is fully drained (S97, audit of #37)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: recompute caught method bug — whole point recompute-don’t-inherit. First pass used findGlobals(merge = FALSE)$functions flagged chooseDate unused, contradicting S78’s “chooseDate longer unused.” Firsthand check found Map(chooseDate, ped$death, ped$departure) setExit.R:54 — chooseDate argument, call, ’s $variables. merge = TRUE (global reference, position — correct conservative “app uses ” test) reconciled moved exactly one function reached set. (1) Environment gotcha: checkout renv.lock + renv/activate.R renv/library, .Rprofile’s source("renv/activate.R") re-bootstraps renv empty lib pkgload/devtools call fails — findGlobals pure static parse-tree analysis needing base codetools, Rscript --vanilla + sys.source R/*.R reproduces issue’s documented method zero deps zero install. (2) Scoping discipline (Learning 91 applied correctly time): fan-read issue body + two triage comments (S65, S78), prior audit reports (BACKLOG_STALENESS, IMPLEMENTED_BUT_OPEN), CHANGELOG — establishing S78 already verified per-function disposition (2 wire-· 37 keep · 0 retire) adversarial completeness critic two days prior. deliverable delta re-verification (recompute set, diff S78, confirm wire-/docfix status), re-run S78’s triage. (3) Right-size SOLO even ultracode: 37 keep--public-API dispositions already verified S78; re-investigating exact Learning-91 redundancy. Workflow fan-theater — genuinely-new work one deterministic recompute + handful targeted firsthand grep/gh checks, adversarial check mattered (recompute correct?) inline catching merge bug. (4) disposition finding: #37’s ever-actionable items (#47, #48, getPedDirectRelatives docfix) shipped/fixed CLOSED; remaining 39 intended public API repeated owner decision (0 retire, safeExecute lone conditional future-candidate). #37 now pure standing inventory — close (work left) keep + update now-stale body (predates wire-ins; lists 3 S3 methods, 4) owner judgment call, auto-close. Reflexes: [#37 reachability recompute ⇒ source R/*.R + codetools::findGlobals Rscript --vanilla; rely pkgload::load_all (renv lib unmaterialized checkout)][use findGlobals(f, merge = TRUE) — call-position-$functions false-flags functions passed Map/apply/.call unused (e.g. chooseDate via Map setExit.R)][recurring inventory ⇒ delta last triage (S78), fresh per-function re-run — read issue comments + docs/audits/ + CHANGELOG scoping (Learning 91)][prior session verified dispositions adversarial critic ⇒ re-verifying redundant; DELTA right-size SOLO even ultracode][confirm “wire-shipped” claim grepping mount site (appUI.R/appServer.R) tracking issue’s CLOSED state — issue list alone][S3 methods can’t proven unused statically — flag “-prove-used,” “dead”][read-audit ⇒ Phase-3E N/, R//test/issue-state change, CHANGELOG-][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: re-verifying #37 (“exported--app-unused” reachability inventory) — recompute statically codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE) sourced R/*.R (package load needed, merge = TRUE avoids higher-order-argument false-unused), first read recent prior triage audit reports scope delta rather re-running verified per-function dispositions; disposition issue (close vs keep--update) owner’s judgment call actionable wire-/docfix surface confirmed drained.

what: Learning 93 — Updating a STALE inventory/catalog (e.g. an issue body) ⇒ reconcile against the FULL recomputed truth, NOT the handoff’s delta-list: a handoff’s “delta” is anchored to the handoff’s OWN baseline, but the artifact you’re editing may sit at an OLDER baseline, so the handoff’s recommendation list is necessary-but-not-sufficient. S97’s handoff listed the delta-since-S78 for #37’s body (strike modORIPReporting*/getPotentialParents, mark removeAutoGenIds transitive, add the 4th S3 summary.nprcgenekeeprGV, note the getPedDirectRelatives docfix) — but #37’s BODY was last re-verified at S65 (2026-06-12), older than S78, so two MORE entries were stale that a delta-since-S78 cannot name: chooseDate (left the unused set AT S78 → already-gone from S78’s frame, so absent from “what changed since S78”) and setAutoIdFormat (a newer #44/#38 export never catalogued at all). Recomputing the authoritative 39-set firsthand and reconciling the WHOLE body against it (a programmatic exact-match check) caught both, plus a pre-existing arithmetic error (the body’s own breakdown summed to 42, not 39) and a grep false-negative (NAMESPACE declares S3 methods as S3method(print,summary.x) — comma form — not print.summary.x). (S98, keep #37 open + update its stale body)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: handoff delta relative handoff’s baseline; artifact may different one — recompute full current truth reconcile whole artifact, don’t hand-apply delta list. deliverable owner’s choice “keep #37 open + update stale body” “close #37.” trap: treat S97’s tidy recommendation list complete change-set. wasn’t — S97 erred, “delta since S78” silent anything changed S78, body lagged S65. (1) recompute-don’t-inherit reflex applies PUBLICATION, just “close #N” / RED-gating. Editing public issue body asserts inventory world, right basis fresh authoritative computation, inherited list. re-ran issue’s documented method (codetools::findGlobals(merge = TRUE) 202 sourced R/*.R Rscript --vanilla, per Learning 92) got 166 / 127 / 39 unused (35 fn + 4 S3) — independently reproducing S97 — reconciled every non-struck body entry set tiny R check asserting identical(body_nonstruck, authoritative). exact-match gate (eyeballing) surfaced chooseDate (body said unused; recompute said REACHED via higher-order Map(chooseDate,…) setExit.R:54) setAutoIdFormat (truth-set, absent body tables). (2) Verify load-bearing claim firsthand goes public — even “trivial” edit. grep S3 exports returned EMPTY first (regex assumed print.summary.x; NAMESPACE uses S3method(print,summary.x)), falsely “confirmed” 4th S3 didn’t exist — re-grepping real form confirmed four. Confirmed 5 strikes (mount sites appUI.R/appServer.R, transitive removeAutoGenIds getPotentialParents.R:42, Map site) 2 additions (setAutoIdFormat direct callers; app reads format via getAutoIdFormat addUIds.R) writing . (3) “Update stale body” includes fixing body’s internal inconsistencies, named items. body claimed 39 unused origin-breakdown summed 42; rebuilt Summary 22 (original 70) + 17 (added since) = 39 holds matches 35-fn/4-S3 split. updated doc wrong arithmetic still stale. (4) Leave dated thread marker silent body edit. GitHub issue-body edits don’t appear timeline, long-lived catalog issue needs short dated comment (“body re-verified & updated 2026-06-16 (S98): struck 5, added 2, see audit doc”) change invisible anyone reading thread. (5) news-vs-changelog + right-size: issue-body edit dev-process bookkeeping (underlying audit already logged S97) → CHANGELOG/NEWS entry ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]); ’s correctly SOLO even ultracode — one recompute + targeted firsthand checks + body rewrite breadth fan-(audit fan-already happened S95/S97; Workflow theater). Phase-3E N/— editing issue body changes runtime behavior (R//test change); FM #24 inapplicable (build step). Reflexes: [updating stale catalog/inventory ⇒ recompute FULL authoritative set reconcile WHOLE artifact (programmatic exact-match), don’t hand-apply handoff’s delta list][handoff “delta” anchored HANDOFF’s baseline — artifact lags OLDER baseline, delta necessary---sufficient; compute diff ARTIFACT’s baseline now][recompute-don’t-inherit applies PUBLICATION (editing public body), just “close #N” RED-gating — fresh authoritative compute, inherited list, basis][verify load-bearing claim firsthand goes public — failed grep may regex artifact (NAMESPACE S3 form S3method(print,summary.x), comma dot), true negative][gate edit identical(body_entries, authoritative_set) — eyeballing 70-row table misses entries changed handoff’s baseline (chooseDate) never catalogued (setAutoIdFormat)][fix artifact’s internal inconsistencies (body’s breakdown summed 42≠39) — “update stale body” ⊇ “make self-consistent”][leave dated comment silent issue-body edit — GitHub body edits don’t show timeline][keep issue OPEN per owner choice; audit doc durable per-export evidence][issue-body edit ⇒ dev-process bookkeeping, CHANGELOG/NEWS warranted][Phase-3E N/— runtime surface][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: task refresh stale catalog/inventory artifact (issue body, ROADMAP table, generated index) — recompute authoritative current set artifact’s documented method, reconcile FULL artifact programmatically (applying -recent handoff’s delta, blind anything changed baseline), fix internal arithmetic/consistency errors find, verify published claim firsthand, leave dated marker edit otherwise invisible.

what: Learning 94 — Re-merging the long-lived add-methodology branch into master (the standing branch model): the safety check is NOT the ahead/behind count — prior PRs merged via MERGE COMMITS, so those merge-commit nodes show up as “master-only” commits and make master look “N ahead” when it has ZERO unique content. Verify a re-merge with a THREE-part recipe instead: (1) git merge-tree --write-tree origin/master origin/add-methodology → exit 0 = no conflicts; (2) git log origin/add-methodology..origin/master --oneline → confirm the only master-only commits are Merge pull request #NN nodes (add-methodology’s own work merged back), NOT independent work; (3) confirm master is strictly behind — comm -23 <(git ls-tree -r --name-only origin/master|sort) <(... add-methodology|sort) is empty (no master-only files) AND the tip-to-tip diff is an exact mirror (master-vs-add-methodology = the inverse of add-methodology-vs-master). All three held for PR #51 (S52–S99, 54 commits, +6001/−406, clean): merge-tree exit 0, the 2 master-only commits were Merge pull request #43/#41, no master-only files, mirror diff. (S100, PR #51 add-methodology → master)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: “2 ahead / 54 behind” divergence looked alarming completely benign — master‘s 2 unique commits GitHub merge nodes PRs #41/#43, .e. add-methodology’s prior work merged back, new work done master.** long-lived feature branch periodically PR-merged (deleted--recreated) accumulates pattern: every prior merge-commit lives master’s first-parent line branch line, git rev-list --left-right --count master...add-methodology reports master “ahead” count prior merges. Reading number “master work ’d regress” trap. (1) decisive safety signal merge-tree + content subsumption, commit count. merge-tree’s clean exit proves 3-way merge applies conflicts; clean 3-way merge preserves master’s content unless add-methodology explicitly reverted lines (surface conflict). Pair mirror-diff / -master--files check prove master strictly behind (zero unique content), merge provably safe purely additive. (2) repo’s standing model, recurs — Learning 91’s “cite prior run” corollary applies branch topology . branch model “work add-methodology; master gets via PR” (S51 = PR #41, S52-ish = PR #43, S100 = PR #51). future add-methodology → master PR show growing “master ahead K merge-commits” divergence; next session expect run three-part recipe rather re-discovering divergence benign. (3) Right-size SOLO even ultracode (call S96/S99 pushes). PR one gh pr create pre-flight investigation; investigation handful deterministic git/gh reads, adversarial check matters (merge cleanly / lose anything?) answered merge-tree + mirror-diff, done inline. Workflow fan-theater. (4) Outward-facing ⇒ confirm exact public title/body gh pr create. PR body published world; per SAFEGUARDS’ outward-facing caution, draft title/body, ground user-facing summary canonical NEWS.md/CHANGELOG.md (memory commits), confirm via AskUserQuestion creating — leave OPEN (merging master separate owner decision). (5) news-vs-changelog:** opening PR publishes already-committed, already-logged work (54 commits carry CHANGELOG/NEWS entries) → new software change → new CHANGELOG/NEWS entry ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S96/S99 push precedent). Phase-3E N/— creating PR changes runtime behavior machine (R//test change); FM #24 inapplicable (build step). Reflexes: [re-merging add-methodology→master ⇒ verify merge-tree (exit 0) + master--commits--merge-nodes + strictly-behind (master-files, mirror diff), ahead/behind count][long-lived PR-merged branch shows prior merge-commits “base ahead” — benign; count safety signal][clean git merge-tree --write-tree = authoritative “applies cleanly, loses nothing” proof — pair content-subsumption check][branch model work--add-methodology / master-via-PR (S51 #41, #43, S100 #51) — expect divergence grow PR; run recipe, don’t re-discover ’s benign][ground PR body’s user-facing summary NEWS.md/CHANGELOG.md, commit-message memory][outward-facing PR ⇒ draft + confirm title/body via AskUserQuestion gh pr create; leave OPEN unless told merge][PR creation = publishing already-logged work ⇒ new CHANGELOG/NEWS (push precedent S96/S99)][Phase-3E N/— runtime surface][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: task “PR add-methodology master” (re-merging long-lived periodically-PR-merged branch) — don’t alarmed “master N ahead” divergence; prove safety git merge-tree --write-tree (conflicts) + confirming master’s unique commits prior merge nodes + master strictly behind (unique files, mirror diff), draft PR body NEWS.md/CHANGELOG.md, confirm public title/body via AskUserQuestion, create , leave OPEN, add CHANGELOG/NEWS entry.

what: Learning 95 — Before planning a CRAN “update/submission,” VERIFY THE PACKAGE’S ACTUAL CRAN STATUS FIRSTHAND (it may be ARCHIVED, which reshapes the whole submission path), and treat a clean one-time local R CMD check as necessary-but-NOT-sufficient — CRAN’s PERIODIC re-checks (not the submission check) archive established packages for timing/policy drift, so the deliverable is “fast/clean under --as-cran on an ongoing basis,” not “passed once.” nprcgenekeepr reads in CLAUDE.md and the repo as an established CRAN package (8 dated cran-comments.md, a CRAN-SUBMISSION marker, revdep/), and the owner framed the task as a routine “version bump + NEWS rewrite.” A firsthand WebFetch of cran.r-project.org/web/packages/nprcgenekeepr/index.html proved otherwise: “Archived on 2025-07-29 as issues were not corrected in time” — and the Archive dir + R-pkg-devel thread showed it had a 1.0.8 publish (2025-07-26) RE-archived ~3 days later over tested elapsed times, plus a prior 2022-11-03 archive/2025-04-24 unarchive cycle. (S101, CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: status determines plan’s spine, Phase-0/scoping check, detail. Published-update vs first-submission vs archived-resubmission three different checklists (archived path adds mandatory root-cause fix + explicit archival cover-note cran-comments.md + stricter human review multiply-archived package). (1) positive, plan-shaping claim demands firsthand probe (Learning 90, applied external state). whole plan pivots “CRAN?”; research agent quoted page, re-fetched anchoring six phases — discipline grepping implementation calling issue “unbuilt,” extended remote authority. (2) “Build/check passes” ≠ “CRAN-stable” (FM #24 CRAN scale). repo’s cran-comments.md honestly recorded 0 errors | 0 warnings | 0 note 1.0.8 submission — package archived anyway, CRAN’s recurring flavor checks later exceeded elapsed-time limits one-time submission check tolerated. timing fix critical path must measured (profile examples/tests/vignettes ---cran), assumed mailing-list reason — index page says generic “issues corrected time.” (3) Right-size research Workflow, synthesis. CRAN-prep plan genuinely independent research angles (CRAN Policy / R-exts, named skills, devtools/usethis pipeline, CRAN status, NEWS conventions) codebase-audit angles (DESCRIPTION+version-string inventory, NEWS structure, check readiness, build cruft) — 9-agent fan-(wy9xitgt6) correct breadth tool ultracode, keeping 396k-token research/file-dump main context point. plan document deliverable author must internalize (SESSION_RUNNER planning rules: grep-based evidence inventory, per-phase completion criteria + verification commands + session boundaries, --dragons). (4) named external source bot-blocked, fall canonical upstream SAY . agent-almanac submit--cran skill’s lobehub mirror JS-blocked, agent fetched real SKILL.md raw.githubusercontent.com/pjt222/agent-almanac; mcpmarket skill stayed blocked (HTTP 429 Vercel checkpoint) reconstructed r-pkgs.org + marinedatascience checklist — flagged plan’s Sources verified-vs-reconstructed executor knows ’s authoritative. (5) Honor project’s NEWS convention general one. owner asked “Major changes / Minor changes” — also every prior NEWS entry’s structure — plan keeps ((breaking) lead-tags inside Major) rather imposing tidyverse’s “Breaking changes / New features / Minor improvements” ([[consult-project-source--truth]]). (6) plan deliverable — bump version touch NEWS session (FM #18 planning--implementation bleed): Phase 3 plan, separate session. Phase-3E N/— writing plan changes runtime behavior; verification appropriate deliverable firsthand CRAN-status probe + evidence-based inventory. Reflexes: [planning CRAN update, WebFetch package’s CRAN index page firsthand — archived vs published vs first-submission picks different checklist][archived-resubmission path = mandatory measured root-cause fix + explicit archival cover-note + expect stricter review (esp. multiply-archived)][one-time clean R CMD check necessary--sufficient — CRAN’s PERIODIC re-checks archive timing/policy; deliverable clean ---cran ongoing, FM #24 CRAN scale][measure archival cause (profile examples/tests/vignettes), don’t assume mailing-list reason — index page reason generic][CRAN-prep plan ⇒ Workflow independent research+audit angles, AUTHOR plan (grep inventory, per-phase criteria/verify/boundary, --dragons)][bot-blocked named source ⇒ fall canonical upstream (GitHub SKILL.md) mark verified-vs-reconstructed Sources][NEWS section names = project’s historical convention (Major/Minor ), imported one][speed-changes simulation/sampling numbers correctness regression — RED-first][version-bump blast radius: don’t touch deprecation = markers historical NEWS/inst strings; getVersion()-driven files auto-track][plan---deliverable — version bump / NEWS rewrite session, FM #18][Phase-3E N/— runtime surface][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: asked “prepare CRAN” / “submit CRAN” / “bump version release” — FIRST WebFetch CRAN package page learn true status (don’t trust repo’s cran-comments/CRAN-SUBMISSION mean “currently published”); archived, make measured root-cause fix critical path write archival cover-note; fan research+audit Workflow author phased plan grep-based inventory per-phase completion criteria; keep version bump + NEWS rewrite later phases.

what: Learning 97 — Profiling CRAN example/test/vignette TIMING (CRAN Phase 2) has three traps that make raw testthat numbers lie, and the profile — not the assumption — names both the offender and the fix mechanism. MEASURE FIRST, with the right harness, under the right conditions. (S103, CRAN Phase 2a — skip_on_cran slow shiny-module tests + native pipe)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: (1) Harness trap — run suite way package , internals vanish. Running tests via library(nprcgenekeepr) + testthat::test_dir() expose non-exported functions, every test calls internal (e.g. addErrTxt, confirmed internal: NAMESPACE, lives R/addErrTxt.R, reachable via :::) errors find function — 52 false “errors” LOOK like missing Suggests harness mistake. package’s tests/testthat.R uses test_check(); dev equivalent pkgload::load_all() (runs package namespace). load_all suite 0 failed / 0 errors. (owner caught misattribution — blamed missing shinytest2/RSelenium/spelling.) (2) skip_if_not(user) trap — raw-slowest ≠ CRAN-slowest. four raw-slowest test files (test_fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio/fillGroupMembers/groupAddAssign/makeExamplePedigreeFile, ~17s) guarded skip_if_not(Sys.info()[["user"]] == "rmsharp") → run owner’s machine, never CI CRAN; measuring rmsharp overcounts CRAN cost ~17s. genuinely CRAN-running slow tests shiny module testServer tests (test_modGeneticValue 4.4s, test_modBreedingGroups 4.6s, test_modInput 2.7s, test_modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign 2.2s, test_modPedigree_processing 1.2s) — use skip_if_not_installed("shiny") shiny Import, run. (3) NOT_CRAN trap. skip_on_cran() skips Sys.getenv("NOT_CRAN") != "true"; run NOT_CRAN=false see CRAN-effective cost, NOT_CRAN=true CI cost. NOT_CRAN=true also makes normally-skipped tests RUN error missing Suggests (shinyBS modSummaryStats/ORIP/founder UI tests) — env gaps, regressions; install dep (shinyBS) clean CI-mode read. profile named fix mechanism. Per-BLOCK timing showed mod* cost spread across MANY shiny::testServer() calls (~0.05–0.3s ), heavy blocks — surgical per-block skipping buys nothing; right move file-level top--file testthat::skip_on_cran() (verified empirically: top-level skip halts whole file cleanly, even helper defined tests reference ). shiny module integration tests; analytical functions exercise (reportGV/geneDrop/groupAddAssign/trimPedigree/qcStudbook) unit tests stay CRAN, file-level skip coverage-preserving spirit. Result: ~15s CRAN test time removed; CI/local still runs everything (NOT_CRAN=true). Examples already fine (6.6s total, slowest countLoops 1.28s — none > 5s, none flag); vignettes ~21s (ColonyManagerTutorial 7.8s / a2interactive 7.5s / simulatedKValues 5.4s) DEFERRED Phase 2b iteration-reduction lever (n=1000 gene-drop simulatedKValues, 3.68s) change displayed kinship numbers (correctness regression) — numeric-preserving fix precompute, larger separate effort. cited archival reason (“tested elapsed times”) = mod* shiny tests, Phase 2a (tests) high-confidence, -numeric-change fix; Phase 2b (vignette precompute) remains. build/check deps ≠ run deps, differ PER profiling surface (owner-reinforced). renv’s snapshot.type="explicit" package.dependency.fields=[Imports,Depends,LinkingTo] (renv/settings.json) means renv::restore() materializes ZERO Suggests — profiling needs precise per-surface installs: examples need Imports (present); vignettes need library() set (kableExtra/png/magrittr); tests need testthat/mockery/withr (+dplyr one) module UI tests need shinyBS; pkgload/roxygen2/devtools build-tooling belong Config/Needs/build, Suggests. RSelenium UNDECLARED (used e2e tests, absent DESCRIPTION Suggests — real gap fix later phase). Native pipe. package floor R (>= 4.1.0), base |> always available; converting %>%→|> removed DIRECT magrittr use (library(magrittr) simulatedKValues.Rmd) Suggests entry needed (%>% came dplyr/kableExtra re-exports). Every usage lhs %>% fn(...) — natively convertible (. placeholder, %T>%/%$%). Editing roxygen @examples %>% means generated .Rd shows \%>\% (% Rd comment char) — convert plain |> (escaping) hand-syncing. Re-rendering NEWS.md NEWS.Rmd (rmarkdown::render(output_format="github_document")) cleanly appended new bullet (whole-file reformat); > renders \> github_document (benign). TDD: edits REFACTOR/mechanical numeric change (skip guards change runs CRAN; pipe syntactic) → RED-first apply; gated PRE-RED→REFACTOR one AskUserQuestion spelling edits + verification, plus separate pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion (defer vignettes; convert pipes). Phase-3E: changed code executed (vignette/example/test) — verified rendering simulatedKValues.Rmd, running makeRelationClassesTable example, affected tests passing — “runtime” checked, skipped. Reflexes: [profile package tests pkgload::load_all() (namespace), library()+test_dir() — latter hides internals → false find function][raw-slowest ≠ CRAN-slowest: check slow test skip_if_not(user==…)/skip_on_cran/skip_if_not_installed blaming — skip_if_not(rmsharp) tests never run owner’s machine][NOT_CRAN=false = CRAN cost, NOT_CRAN=true = CI cost; NOT_CRAN=true surfaces missing-Suggests errors (env, regressions)][per-block timing decides surgical-vs-file-level skip — spread-testServer overhead ⇒ file-level top--file skip_on_cran() (halts whole file cleanly, even past later helper def)][skip shiny module integration tests CRAN; analytical functions unit tests stay][vignette speed-reduces simulation iterations changes displayed numbers = correctness regression ⇒ defer numeric-preserving precompute, RED-first][renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests ⇒ install precise per-surface set; build-tooling (pkgload/roxygen2/devtools) ⇒ Config/Needs/build][R>=4.1 floor ⇒ |> always available; %>%→|> removes magrittr direct user; \%>\% .Rd ⇒ plain |>][re-render NEWS.md NEWS.Rmd, never hand-edit NEWS.md][use plain descriptive language, jargon like “dragon-prone” — user-flagged S103, see [[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]]][macos-dupe-scan]. Apply: CRAN timing work — profile surface (examples via R CMD check -Ex.timings; tests via pkgload::load_all()+test_dir NOT_CRAN settings; vignettes via per-vignette render + isolate heavy compute) concluding; let per-unit/per-block data name offender; gate slow shiny-module integration tests file-level skip_on_cran() (numeric risk); defer iteration-reducing vignette fixes numeric-preserving precompute pass; install precise per-surface deps, recognizing build/check/run three different sets. file-path-.Rbuildignore simulation -reports exclusions; real build models directory pruning. naive per-file grepl(pattern, path) check reported inst/extdata/code_under_development/combinerKinshipTriangles.R “kept” — R CMD build excludes , existing pattern ^inst/extdata/code_under_development$ end-anchored DIRECTORY build prunes whole dir (walks include.dirs=TRUE), whereas per-file grep needs pattern match contained file. static sim useful pre-check tar tzf actual R CMD build authoritative (708 entries , 0 cruft, 0 hidden files). (1) macOS/R junk TRACKED git, just working-tree litter. git ls-files showed .DS_Store, man/.DS_Store, .Rapp.history, inst/extdata/.Rapp.history tracked — plan’s root-anchored ^\.DS_Store$ MISSED man/.DS_Store. END-anchored, front-UNanchored \.DS_Store$ / \.Rapp\.history$ (paren-free, per .Rbuildignore perl-regex hazard) catches every copy subdir. Build-ignoring keeps tarball WITHOUT git rm, preserving owner’s standing .DS_Store keep-call (de-tracking via git rm --cached separate, owner’s-call tidy). (2) \value two exported functions lacking , roxygen2 unavailable: add @return roxygen source hand-sync .Rd (modern roxygen2 places \value \arguments \description); Phase 4’s roxygenise() (post renv::restore()) canonicalizes cosmetic whitespace diff. appServer() = “return value, called side effects” (Shiny server, invoked side effects — confirmed ends module-server calls explicit return); appUI() returns shiny.tag.list. (3) Phase 1 RED/GREEN — metadata + docs + build-ignore config behavioral test surface (verified R CMD build + read.dcf + tools::parse_Rd, testthat); named guard tests (test_appUI_version.R, test_getVersion.R) unaffected version/logic changed — verified READING (full suite deferred Phase 4’s renv gate, skipped). PRE-RED→REFACTOR transition still went ONE AskUserQuestion gate spelling every edit (contract’s permission requirement holds even RED/GREEN don’t apply). Phase-3E N/— runtime/app behavior changed (roxygen additions comments; DESCRIPTION/LICENSE/.Rbuildignore aren’t loaded runtime); build-equivalent (R CMD build + tarball inspection) appropriate verification run (FM #24 — untested runtime behavior masked, actual deliverable verified). news-vs-changelog: packaging/metadata hygiene = dev-process history → CHANGELOG ; user-facing NEWS rewrite Phase 3 ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]). Reflexes: [CRAN Phase-1 hygiene ⇒ verify R CMD build ---build-vignettes ---manual (base-R , renv) + tar tzf, hand-rolled ignore sim][tar tzf real build authoritative file-path .Rbuildignore simulation — sim can’t model directory pruning][end-anchored front-unanchored \.DS_Store$ catches subdir copies (e.g. man/.DS_Store); root-anchored ^\.DS_Store$ misses ][macOS/R junk may TRACKED — build-ignore keeps tarball without git rm; de-tracking separate owner’s-call][\value roxygen2 unavailable ⇒ edit roxygen source + hand-sync .Rd (); Phase 4 roxygenise canonicalizes][Phase-1 metadata/docs = REFACTOR, RED/GREEN, still gate change one AskUserQuestion][Phase-3E N/— runtime surface][news-vs-changelog: packaging hygiene → CHANGELOG, NEWS rewrite Phase 3][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode]. Apply: executing CRAN-prep Phase 1 (build cruft + DESCRIPTION + \value) — don’t wait renv::restore(); make edits, prove cleanliness R CMD build ---build-vignettes ---manual + tar tzf (authoritative ignore-simulation), use end-anchored \.DS_Store$/\.Rapp\.history$ catch subdir junk, build-ignore rather git rm tracked junk weren’t asked de-track, hand-sync .Rd roxygen2 isn’t installed.

what: Learning 98 — A “should we adopt technology X to future-proof?” decision splits into THREE independent questions — (1) is the incumbent actually dying? (2) what does X concretely buy ON OUR constrained surface? (3) what does X cost there? — and the answer is frequently HYBRID (adopt X where it’s unconstrained and free; stay on the incumbent where the constraint lives), not all-or-nothing. Verify every external-ecosystem claim by adversarial web research, never from model memory. (S104, Quarto-vs-R-Markdown documentation future-proofing analysis — docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: Don’t answer “convert X?” one question. owner asked whether migrate package’s docs R Markdown Quarto “future-proof.” naive framing binary (convert / don’t). correct decomposition three separable questions, different evidence base: (1) incumbent dead end? — R Markdown, : Posit’s -record line “going away, plans deprecation, actively supported long time come”; rmarkdown 2.31 (2026-03, GPLv3→MIT relicense) knitr 1.51 (2025-12) actively maintained CRAN-critical infrastructure (~800 reverse-imports; ’s engine Quarto runs R ). real cost staying feature stagnation (“new features may exist Quarto”), breakage. (2) X buy surface? — single-language R package’s CRAN HTML vignettes, gain NARROW CRAN vignette engine deliberately minimal (theme:none, minimal:true): callouts/tabsets/Bootstrap-5/multi-language irrelevant disabled; native cross-references realized. differentiators make Quarto compelling (books, websites, multi-format single-source, multi-language) apply manual/website, four simple vignettes. (3) X cost ? — Quarto vignette adds SystemRequirements Quarto-CLI dependency CRAN’s check machines DON’T guarantee (confirmed missing macOS flavors 2025), documented transient “vignettes” NOTE — bad trade already-archived package. Quarto maintainer (cderv) “advise” Quarto vignette engine CRAN vignettes. hybrid answer usually right one, ’s real “yes X,” dodge. Recommendation: keep CRAN vignettes knitr/rmarkdown (zero CRAN risk, officially supported indefinitely) adopting Quarto unconstrained surface pays carries CRAN dependency — pkgdown site, new long-form docs, slide decks, inst/extdata/ dev docs (two already .qmd). enabled fact, wish: pkgdown supports MIXED .qmd/.Rmd (since 2.1.0, 2024-07) via project: render: ['*.qmd']. one genuine strategic fork worth surfacing owner (deciding ): long-form manual CRAN vignette doc rewards Quarto — repositioned CRAN vignette set onto website. Conversion mechanical reversible (Quarto renders .Rmd unmodified), argument rushing: switching cost stays low whenever (ever) chosen. Process notes held. (1) owner reframed mid-session (timing → future-proofing); right move KEEP pass 1’s already-running CRAN-viability/timing research (still load-bearing guardrail new framing) ADD targeted pass 2 strategic/longevity dimension — discard restart. (2) Adversarial verification earns confidence: across two Workflows (9 + 8 agents, ~900k subagent tokens kept main context), six load-bearing claims survived explicit attempt refute high confidence — far stronger asserting “Quarto heavier” memory. (3) Ground inventory firsthand : read vignette engine headers, a3manual child-include structure, simulatedKValues.Rmd (set.seed sim → deterministic), confirmed vignettes/*.html|.R|.md git-ignored stale renders (precompute pattern) — just trusting audit agent. (4) Right-size Workflow--research + SOLO--synthesis: research breadth (CRAN policy, build-time, ecosystem trajectory, migration mechanics) genuine independent fan-; recommendation document mine author per planning rules (like S101’s CRAN plan). (5) Timing fix orthogonal decision: CRAN plan’s deferred Phase 2b vignette timing fixed precompute EXISTING engine (.Rmd.orig→committed .Rmd), Quarto — keep two efforts separate. [news-vs-changelog]: analysis/planning doc = dev-process history → CHANGELOG , NEWS ([[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]], S101 plan precedent). Phase-3E N/— analysis document changes runtime behavior; verification appropriate deliverable adversarially-verified research + firsthand inventory (FM #24 — build step mistaken correctness). Used plain language, jargon ([[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language]] — “dragons”/“dragon-prone” new doc). Reflexes: [decompose “adopt X future-proof?” -incumbent-dying / -X-buys---constrained-surface / -X-costs-— three different evidence bases][future-proofing answer often HYBRID: adopt X unconstrained/author-controlled surface, stay incumbent hard constraint (, CRAN’s guaranteed toolchain) lives][verify external-ecosystem trajectory claims (deprecation status, maintenance, CRAN support) adversarial web research refutation, never model memory][rmarkdown/knitr maintained CRAN-critical infrastructure — “going away, deprecation”; staying safe, cost feature stagnation][Quarto CRAN vignette = SystemRequirements Quarto-CLI guaranteed CRAN machines + minimal engine disables Quarto features ⇒ narrow benefit, real risk, esp. archived package][pkgdown supports MIXED .qmd/.Rmd (2.1.0) — hybrid split officially enabled][owner reframes mid-task ⇒ keep still-relevant -flight research guardrail, ADD pass new axis; don’t discard][ground inventory firsthand (engine headers, child includes, gitignored renders), just audit agent][Workflow research breadth, author recommendation SOLO (planning rules)][CRAN vignette timing fix = precompute existing engine, orthogonal Quarto decision][analysis/planning doc → CHANGELOG , NEWS][Phase-3E N/— runtime surface][use plain language, jargon][right-size-Workflow--research-SOLO--synthesis]. Apply: asked “adopt/convert  future-proof?” — split (1) incumbent actually retired (verify firsthand, don’t assume), (2) new thing concretely buy specific constrained surface ship (often little, constraint strips differentiators), (3) cost (new dependency / risk); expect answer deliberate HYBRID — adopt ’s free pays , keep incumbent constraint lives — present options clear recommendation owner’s decision, authored one analysis doc (CHANGELOG ), external claims adversarially web-verified.

what: Learning 99 — RECORDING an adopted decision is not the same as STAMPING it “adopted”: when the owner picks a recommendation that has open sub-options, resolving a sub-option can invalidate a top-level claim the source doc made under a different sub-choice — so propagate the chosen sub-option’s consequences and CORRECT the now-stale claims, don’t just flip a Status line. (S105, adopt Hybrid documentation strategy — docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: trap. S104’s analysis recommended Hybrid (Option B) , §8, stated “Option intersect CRAN submission; B/C plan unaffected” — TRUE §6.3 manual sub-decision stayed () keep--knitr-vignette. owner adopted B §6.3 → (b) (reposition manual onto website, dropping CRAN vignette set), removes CRAN vignette = changes package contents R CMD check sees, adopted path now intersect resubmission. Flipping Status “ADOPTED” without fixing §8 left self-contradicting policy doc — kind stale load-bearing claim FM #11/#20 warn , introduced act recording rather memory drift. “record decision” actually entails (checklist worked): (1) flip doc’s decision surfaces — Status header, TL;DR, §7 options-table verdict, “default unaddressed” line — reads policy, pending recommendation; (2) RESOLVE every open sub-decision chosen option carried (§6.3 → (b)) mark inline option described; (3) re-derive CORRECT consequence source doc asserted conditional different sub-choice (§8’s “intersects”); (4) record implementation slices future sessions look — project, analysis doc (new §7.1 slices table per-slice CRAN-risk + ordering) + ROADMAP “Planned”; (5) CHANGELOG entry (documentation-process decision = dev-process history → CHANGELOG , NEWS — [[backlog-vs-changelog-placement]]); (6) auto-memory — decision now lives repo, memory duplicate source truth ([[consult-project-source--truth]]). deliverable boundary held (FM #18). “Adopt Hybrid” DECISION-recording session; converts NOTHING. first conversion slice separate, owner-approved session. owner’s menu framing (“() Decide recommendation … first implementable slice separate session”) made explicit, recording slices §7.1 table (slice-4 resubmission-coordination gate) sets sessions without starting . one CRAN-touching slice (manual) flagged gated CRAN plan, free-standing. Two AskUserQuestions front pinned two genuine owner sub-decisions (§6.3 manual disposition; file-issues--) writing — changed exactly got recorded. Right-sized SOLO ultracode: editing 4–5 docs record decision owner just made — facts already established already adversarially verified S104 — breadth fan fresh claims verify; Workflow theater (S101/S102/S103/S104 call). Verification = firsthand cross-document consistency re-read (§6.3 = §7 = §7.1 = §8 = ROADMAP = CHANGELOG tell ONE story?). Reflexes: [recording adopted decision ≠ stamping “adopted” — resolve open sub-options CORRECT source claim assumed different sub-choice][flip doc’s decision surfaces: Status, TL;DR, options-table verdict, default line][record slices future sessions look: analysis doc + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG entry, NEWS][auto-memory decision now recorded repo][decision-recording converts nothing — FM #18; slices separate owner-approved sessions][gate one CRAN-touching slice CRAN plan, keep zero-risk slices independent][ask genuine owner sub-decisions front via AskUserQuestion][verify decision-recording deliverable cross-document consistency re-read, SOLO][right-size-SOLO-even--ultracode]. Apply: owner says “adopt ” — treat decision-recording deliverable (convert nothing); ask option’s open sub-decisions first; flip every decision surface source doc, resolve sub-decisions inline, re-derive fix consequence doc stated different sub-choice, record implementation slices doc + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG (NEWS, auto-memory), verify reading touched docs one consistent story.

what: Learning 100 — Converting a doc Rmd → Quarto .qmd is not just a rename: (a) the target format has SEMANTIC differences the source didn’t (Quarto treats ::: as fenced-div/callout syntax; R Markdown doesn’t), so you must RENDER to catch them — a static rename hides them; (b) verify the conversion changes no package contents by applying the .Rbuildignore regexes to BOTH filenames, don’t assume “it was ignored so the new one is too”; (c) when the doc isn’t reproducibly renderable (dead hardcoded paths, unmaterialized packages), render with --no-execute to verify FORMAT validity and STATE the execution limitation — do not fake a full render, and do NOT “fix” the doc into being executable (that’s a behavior change, not a format conversion); (d) preserve the author’s historical prose byte-for-byte rather than rewrite it to silence a cosmetic warning whose output is verified correct (FM #22). (S106, Quarto Hybrid §7.1 slice 1 — inst/extdata/meeting_notes.Rmd → .qmd)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Slice 1 adopted Hybrid doc policy: convert build-ignored developer doc inst/extdata/meeting_notes.Rmd .qmd. mechanical change tiny — git mv + one YAML line (output: html_document → format: html, matching two already-.qmd sibling dev docs); git diff -M reported similarity index 99%, body byte--byte. value verification, surfaced three things rename-“conversion” missed: - real Rmd→qmd semantic difference. Rendering .qmd (Quarto 1.7.33) emitted warning string nprcgenekeepr::: “looked like fenced div.” Quarto treats ::: fenced-div / callout syntax; R Markdown . token R’s internal-function operator quoted 2020 CRAN-review reply, sitting prose. Checking rendered HTML proved rendered correctly (mid-line ::: div delimiter — heuristic false-positive), left byte-faithful. general point holds: static rename never revealed ; render . latent Quarto-vs-Rmd differences (header attributes {}, raw-HTML/$math$ handling, callouts) live blind spot. - doc isn’t reproducibly renderable — ’s mine “fix.” Five embedded R chunks hardcode 2020-era absolute paths (/Users/rmsharp/.../20160816_GeneticManagementTools) need packages absent default library (standing renv--materialized condition). include=FALSE hides output chunk still executes, full render fails dead paths even packages. historical meeting-notes log, live computational document. build-equivalent (SAFEGUARDS “Verify Build Equivalent”) therefore satisfied quarto render ---execute (verifies YAML + markdown + structure → 81 KB HTML, code shown run) execution limitation stated, silently skipped. Making re-executable (e.g. eval: false globally, repointing paths) behavior/content change masquerading format conversion — FM #8 / SAFEGUARDS two-mode “’m ” trap. - “build-ignored” proof new file . confirmed reading relevant .Rbuildignore regexes (^inst/extdata/meeting_notes\. — extension-agnostic — ^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$) running every .Rbuildignore pattern meeting_notes.Rmd meeting_notes.qmd R (ships=FALSE ). ignore pattern extension-specific (...\.Rmd$), conversion started shipping new .qmd CRAN tarball — silent package-contents change. archived package mid-resubmission (Learning 95), ’s exactly kind invisible regression rule , assume. Reflexes: [Rmd→qmd format change SEMANTIC differences (::: fenced divs), just rename — RENDER catch ][verify doc conversion changes package contents running .Rbuildignore regexes old new filenames, assuming][doc isn’t reproducibly renderable (dead paths / unmaterialized packages), render ---execute verify FORMAT STATE execution limitation — never fake full render][“fix” historical doc executable format conversion — ’s behavior change (FM #8)][preserve author’s historical prose byte-faithful; don’t rewrite silence cosmetic warning whose output verified correct (FM #22)][git mv doc renames — preserves history, fully reversible][1--done: one slice, bundle next (FM #18/#25)][right-size SOLO single-file mechanical conversion even ultracode].
