Apply: converting doc markup engines — minimal faithful change (git mv + format-line), RENDER (using ---execute doc isn’t reproducibly executable) catch target-format semantic differences, confirm via ignore/build rules shipped-contents changed, state verification perform, leave author’s content byte-faithful unless real output defect (cosmetic warning) forces change.

what: Learning 101 — A “make it render/build” doc deliverable is verified by building it through the REAL consumer’s integration path, not a proxy renderer — and an adversarial reviewer’s “correction” is a claim to check against ground truth, not a verdict to act on. (S107, Quarto Hybrid §7.1 slice 2 — pkgdown mixed mode + vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Slice 2 adopted Hybrid doc policy: stand pkgdown mixed .qmd/.Rmd mode author first Quarto pkgdown article (scripted breeding-group-formation walkthrough shipped examplePedigree data). Three things made verification real work: - Recon canonical source corrected imprecise plan instruction. §7.1 policy note said “add _quarto.yml (project: render: ['*.qmd'])” without saying . authoritative pkgdown docs (+ usethis use_article()) say _quarto.yml .qmd live inside vignettes/articles/ (pkgdown turns dir Quarto project), single .Rbuildignore line ^vignettes/articles$ makes whole dir website-— covering article, _quarto.yml, .quarto/ cache. root _quarto.yml (Phase-1 assumption) needed ignore entry pulled .Rmd vignettes Quarto project. Reading source truth beat acting plausible-sounding policy paraphrase. - Render-proxy; build real consumer. quarto render proves article’s R chunks execute (, ~1.7 s, deterministic via set_seed(1L)). deliverable’s actual claim “pkgdown mixed mode works.” installed pkgdown 2.2.0 + quarto R package locally ran pkgdown::build_article — surfaced integration detail renderer never : pkgdown’s name article articles/breeding-group-formation (articles/ prefix), build succeeded used (bare stem errored “Can’t find article”). two-path verification (quarto render pkgdown build) makes “mixed mode stood ” tested claim rather hopeful one. Zero CRAN risk likewise proven (real R CMD build tarball shows vignettes/articles/ tree absent shipping vignettes unaffected), assumed. - Verify verifier. fresh adversarial reviewer flagged article’s “threshold 0.015625 = 1/64 = second cousins” wrong, asserting 1/64 third cousins. Checked acting: confused coefficient relationship (r; second cousins = 1/32) kinship coefficient (φ = r/2; second cousins = 1/64) — package computes φ. package’s manual (vignettes/manual_components/_breeding_group_formation.Rmd) says default ignores “relatedness distant second cousins.” original correct; left unchanged. adversarial pass trustworthy check run claims. Reflexes: [verify doc/render deliverable REAL consumer’s build path (pkgdown::build_article), just standalone renderer — integration path catches discovery/naming/wiring renderer can’t][read canonical/authoritative source config placement; don’t act plan’s paraphrase ][prove zero-CRAN-risk website artifact real R CMD build tarball + build-ignore whole vignettes/articles/ dir — don’t assume][adversarial reviewer’s “correction” candidate, verdict — check ground truth (math package’s docs) changing anything; distinguish kinship coefficient φ coefficient relationship r][build -article function’s roxygen @examples pipeline exercises code R CMD check runs][seed front (set_seed) reproducible rendered stochastic output][right-size: recon Workflow breadth/context-economy, author prose SOLO, verify real build paths]. Apply: deliverable “make X render/build surface Y,” verify building Y (install Y’s real toolchain needed), prove “won’t ship / risk” claim actual build artifact, ground config choices authoritative docs rather plan’s restatement, treat every adversarial finding — including reviewer’s — claim verify, accept.

what: Learning 102 — When documenting a function’s outputs/scoring, the GROUND TRUTH is the implementation run on real data plus the scoring source — NOT the function’s own roxygen, which can drift. Run the pipeline first; read the ordering/scoring code; treat @return/@param prose as a hint. (S108, Quarto Hybrid §7.1 — second article vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd, a scripted reportGV() walkthrough)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Authoring accurate -reportGV() meant getting three things right package’s roxygen states incorrectly — caught reading implementation: (1) orderReport.R @return says High-Value mean-kinship tier “mean kinship less 0.25,” code (orderReport.R:63) gates z-score (zScores <= 0.25), raw mean kinship; (2) calcFEFG.R roxygen calls founder weight p “average number descendants,” code computes Mendelian-halved proportional contributions (calcFounderContributions.R); (3) reportGV.R @return says returns “dataframe,” returns list class nprcgenekeeprGV. article correct three written code + real run, roxygen. Run--pipeline-first paid . Executing GVA shipped examplePedigree (deterministic via set_seed(1L) — gene-drop behind genome uniqueness / fe / fg stochastic; mean kinship ) produced real numbers prose describes (327-animal population interest, 199/128 High/Low Value, fe 109.67 / fg 47.62) surfaced teaching example free: rank-1 animal higher genome uniqueness lowest mean kinship — exactly ranking uses metrics. article prints computed values chunks rather hardcoding , rendered output ground truth. Verify--verifier held (Learning 101). time adversarial reviewer’s flags real — confirmed code applying three precision fixes: meanKinship.R colMeans whole matrix incl. diagonal → “animals incl. ,” “every ”; orderReport.R:59 tier-2 sort breaks ties ascending z-score; orderReport.R:32 imports tier also requires id %% founders. adversarial pass returns “must-fix” still earns cost: converted three latent imprecisions fixes independently confirmed article -corrects roxygen. Discovered, mine (flagged, fixed — FM #8). sibling S107 article breeding-group-formation.qmd comments focal set “founders still colony,” filter !(.na(sire) & .na(dam)) selects non-founders (≥ 1 known parent). one-line prose inversion; left owner-approved session. Reflexes: [run function’s real pipeline shipped data let printed output ground truth writing descriptive sentence][read scoring/ordering implementation (orderReport/rankSubjects/calcFEFG), function’s @return, doc’s whole job explain behavior — roxygen drifts][seed front reproducible stochastic rendered output][adversarial reviewer’s flags claims verify code, even turn right][-built function’s @examples shape exercises code R CMD check runs][right-size: recon Workflow breadth, author SOLO, verify real build paths]. Apply: documenting function’s outputs/scoring — derive every claim real run + implementation; roxygen wrong, FIX-FORWARD doc note roxygen future repair session; flag, don’t fix, errors find sibling artifacts.

what: Learning 103 — Ground-truth-first documentation also means running the UNHAPPY paths: a function’s error/edge behavior is part of what you are documenting, and running it can refute an assumption you would otherwise have written down as fact. (S109, Quarto Hybrid §7.1 — third article vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd, a scripted qcStudbook() walkthrough)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Documenting qcStudbook() meant describing two modes (reportErrors = FALSE production vs TRUE diagnostic) handles every category bad input. reading code assumed invalid dates silently coerced NA production mode. Running qcStudbook(pedInvalidDates) showed actually stops (“invalid dates row(s) 3 4”). Running shipped error-demo set modes produced exact, verified behavior table article rests — auto-corrected (female-sire/male-dam, exact duplicates), fatal (missing column, invalid date, sire==dam, young parent, period--ID), diagnostic list’s named elements (returning NULL clean). shipped pedGood/pedFemaleSireMaleDam/pedInvalidDates/pedDuplicateIds/pedMissingBirth/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam data sets purpose-built worked examples — find use rather constructing inputs. Determinism reading, hoping. qcStudbook() random component (seed needed — unlike GVA/breeding siblings whose reportGV/groupAddAssign gene-drop stochastic), one Sys.Date() dependency (calcAge living animals) bypassed examplePedigree ships age column, live-date value enters displayed output. Confirmed empirically render-determinism review lens: two render passes produced byte-identical HTML. fatal call must appear render, wrap tryCatch(..., error = function(e) cat(conditionMessage(e))) real message shown without breaking build (vs #| error: true chunk whose toolchain support proven ). Two-lens adversarial review + verify--verifier (Learning 101/102). Ran review two parallel lenses (code-correctness vs implementation; pedagogy + render-determinism). must-fix; confirmed flag code acting — H sex-code flag real (qcStudbook calls convertSexCodes(ignoreHerm = TRUE) → H/HERMAPHRODITE/4 fold U, never output), fixed article (-correcting -broad “M/F/U/H” claim) added ## Setup section house-style parallelism siblings; left “defensible digest” column-rename nit -. Reflexes: [run unhappy paths, just happy path — error/edge behavior part documentation surface, running can refute code-reading assumption][prefer shipped purpose-built example data sets constructed inputs][prove render determinism reading RNG + Sys.Date() reachability byte-identical double render][show fatal call via tryCatch + cat(conditionMessage(e)) build stays clean][two adversarial lenses (correctness vs pedagogy/render) beat one doc article; still verify every flag code]. Apply: documenting function modes/error handling — exercise every mode real data let output table; behavior read source hypothesis run .

what: Learning 104 — When the documentation IS a data visualization, the figure’s input filter is part of the truth: read what the plot actually includes/excludes, and run it on the example data — a plot built from incomplete data can show the OPPOSITE of reality, not just an attenuated version. (S110, Quarto Hybrid §7.1 — fourth article vignettes/articles/age-sex-pyramid.qmd, a scripted getPyramidPlot() walkthrough)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. getPyramidPlot(qcPed) looked like plot “living colony,” naive article said . Running (fillBins) showed pyramid plots living animals known age — 46 89 living, 43 living animals lack birth date aged. 43 male, plot shows 35 F / 11 M (≈ 3:1 female) living colony actually 35 F / 54 M (male-majority). visualization shows reverse real sex ratio. article built verified numbers turned central lesson (missing birth dates can invert demographic plot, just shrink — quality-control first), second adversarial lens sharpened “-counts males” “skew reversed.” Determinism base-graphics figure. pyramid plotrix::pyramid.plot() (base graphics), plotting call must chunk’s last expression, #| results: hide keeps function’s (non-plot) return value rendered output. render deterministic shape — RNG, seed (unlike GVA/breeding siblings), qcPed ships frozen age column — title embeds lubridate::now(), date label build date. characterized honestly rather trying force byte-identical output. Two-lens review + verify--verifier (Learning 101/102/103). Code-correctness re-ran every number qcPed (held). pedagogy lens caught reversed-skew understatement V&R reference -attribution; confirmed data (living 35F/54M) paper’s actual subject applying. Found (fixed, FM #8) getPyramidPlot @return roxygen drift (claims par('mar'); returns pyramid.plot()’s value) — flagged roxygen-repair session. Reflexes: [documentation---plot, read plot’s input filter (fillBins: living + non-NA age) run example data — figure’s coverage part claim][plot incomplete data can show OPPOSITE reality, just attenuated version — check stratum--stratum (dropped 43 male, flipping sex ratio)][base-graphics chunk: plot last expression + #| results: hide drop return value][now() title honest “render date,” determinism defect hide]. Apply: documenting plotting function — derive description function actually plots real data, stress-test whether missing data invert (merely shrink) visual conclusion.

what: Learning 105 — A flagged error from a previous session is a claim to re-verify, not a fact to action: confirm it two ways (internal corroboration + ground truth on the example data) before correcting, and right-size a one-line fix as SOLO. (S111, doc fix vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd — corrected the inverted “founders” → “non-founders” focal-population description)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S108 discovered, S109/S110 carried forward, breeding-group article called focal set “founders still colony” filter !(.na(sire) & .na(dam)) & .na(exit) actually selects non-founders. Rather trust prior-session flag swap word, confirmed two independent ways: (1) internal corroboration — sentence trims pedigree focal set “plus ancestors needed compute kinships,” founders definition ancestors, focal set logically must non-founders; (2) ground truth — ran qcStudbook(examplePedigree) + article’s filter: 327 focal animals, none founders, ≥ 1 known parent (1,668 founders studbook, zero focal set). agreed flag, fix safe. correction also adds inline definition (“least one known parent”) term clear without reader cross-referencing code. Right-sizing ultracode. one-line prose correction deterministic two-way verification breadth fan competing claims adjudicate — done SOLO. Spawning Workflow theater (S100/S106 call genuinely single-threaded mechanical work). Ultracode means exhaustive correct, always multi-agent; exhaustiveness two-way verification, parallelism. Reflexes: [flagged error inherited predecessor hypothesis verify code + data, fact action blindly — even predecessor reliable][confirm prose-vs-logic mismatch two ways: internal corroboration (surrounding text agree?) + ground truth (run actual filter example data)][correcting inverted term, add inline definition fix also removes ambiguity allowed inversion][right-size: one-line fix deterministic verification SOLO, Workflow]. Apply: actioning “discovered, mine” flag prior session — re-verify firsthand editing; discoverer couldn’t fix precisely wasn’t deliverable, claim never re-checked.

what: Learning 106 — Regenerating a generated artifact (here man/ via roxygenise()) with a dev tool newer than the committed baseline silently reformats EVERY file and migrates config — read git diff --stat after any codegen step, and if it touches files beyond your edits, revert the version migration and apply the change surgically rather than bundling a tooling bump into a content fix. And: re-verifying inherited flags can REFUTE them, not just confirm them. (S112, roxygen-repair pass — fixed 3 of 4 inherited @return/p drifts across orderReport/calcFEFG/reportGV + the calcFE/calcFG siblings)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened (blast-radius trap). deliverable 5-line roxygen prose fix + man/ regen. Running roxygen2::roxygenise() rewrote ~30 .Rd files changed DESCRIPTION — far beyond 4 functions edited. Cause: committed man/ generated roxygen2 7.3.2 (RoxygenNote: 7.3.2), dev library 8.0.0, reformats every page (data-doc \usage qcPed → data(qcPed), re-wrapped \value) migrates field (RoxygenNote: → Config/roxygen2/version:). Committing bundled tooling-baseline migration — deliberate, CRAN-coordinated decision — doc fix. Countermeasure: git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION drop version artifacts, hand-edited four affected \value blocks match source, keeping diff exactly 5 R/ + 4 man/ files. 7.3.2→8.0.0 migration flagged separate task. General rule: codegen/regeneration step, read git diff --stat; touches files intend, version/tooling mismatch reformatting — commit part content change. (codegen sibling SAFEGUARDS “Verify Render-Dependency Completeness” — tool’s version render dependency.) Re-verifying inherited flags can refute, just confirm (extends Learning 105). Four @return/param drifts inherited code-read claims (three S108, one S110) never confirmed runtime. 8-agent verification workflow (one verifier + one adversarial cross-checker per claim) confirmed three refuted fourth: getPyramidPlot.R’s @return (“return value par('mar')”) correct — function returns plotrix::pyramid.plot()’s value, pyramid.plot ends return(oldmar) oldmar <- par("mar"), returned value par("mar") vector (verified: returns c(5.1, 4.1, 4.1, 2.1)). S110’s flag false-dichotomy code-read (“returns pyramid.plot, par(‘mar’)”) never ran function. Trusting replaced correct @return wrong one. cross-check also surfaced -defect instances beyond named scope (p description sibling calcFE/calcFG; second orderReport @return bullet), owner approved folding . Right-sizing: one earned Workflow S111 . Adjudicating four independent inherited claims ship CRAN — needing code trace + adversarial check — real breadth competing claims, verify+refute fan-warranted (contrast Learning 105’s single deterministic one-liner, correctly SOLO). Verification fans ; mutation (edits + regen) stays single-threaded main loop keep control avoid worktree conflicts. Scope discipline held one disclosed extension. Fixed exactly agreed p-description calcFE/calcFG (kept r clause), flagged — fix — adjacent pre-existing defects (calcFE’s @return mentions r though FE = 1/sum(p^2) none; sum( (p^2) / r} formula unbalanced parens; pre-existing reportGV.Rd \title-ends--period checkRd note). Reflexes: [codegen/regeneration step, read git diff --stat trusting — diff touching files beyond edits means tool/version mismatch reformatting ; revert apply surgically][never bundle generated-tooling version migration (roxygen 7.3.2→8.0.0) content fix — ’s separate, deliberate decision][generated artifact (man/*.Rd) may hand-edited scoped, disclosed exception regenerating force version migration — content matches source, later proper regen reconciles cleanly][re-verify inherited flags runtime, just code-read — can WRONG, merely unconfirmed; one four ][verification fans (verifier + adversarial refuter per claim), mutation stays single-threaded][doc-change build-equivalent = tools::checkRd() + pkgload::load_all() + render \value, full R CMD check; devtools renv lib, roxygen2/pkgload/pkgbuild ]. Apply: task editing roxygen/generated docs — check regen blast radius, keep tooling migrations content fixes, re-verify every inherited claim firsthand acting .

what: Learning 107 — A generated-tooling version migration, taken as its OWN deliverable, is the clean inverse of the blast-radius trap (Learning 106): the diff is small and reviewable, and regenerating with the newer tool also REPAIRS content that had silently drifted between the source and the committed artifact. Adopt it safely by reading the COMPLETE regen diff (every change must be an intended kind) and confirming the new tool introduces ZERO new linter findings — not by spot-checking. (S113, owner pick A — adopted roxygen2 8.0.0, regenerated man/)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S112 hit roxygen2 8.0.0 reformatting man/ mid-way content fix correctly isolated version migration separate, deliberate task. S113 executed task whole deliverable. Counter “blast radius” framing, isolated migration small: 26 190 .Rd files + DESCRIPTION, 31 insertions / 29 deletions, NAMESPACE untouched. changes exactly three kinds — Config/roxygen2/version field rename (RoxygenNote: 7.3.2 retired), 24 dataset docs adopting 8.0.0’s canonical \usage{ data(name) } form, cosmetic \value re-wrap — plus fourth, unexpected beneficial: regenerating man/nprcgenekeepr-package.Rd repaired shipped typo ('mulatto' → 'mulatta', already correct DESCRIPTION) added maintainer Authors list. committed 7.3.2 .Rd drifted source; regen reconciled . migration point, verification “WHOLE diff intended?”, “break anything?” adversarial check mattered reading complete diff (27 files) tallying every added line — confirming surprise edits hiding among mechanical ones — proving new tool added ZERO new checkRd problems checking 26 pages confirming 11 flagged \title-period NOTEs already present committed HEAD (62 190 package-wide). spot-check files missed either stray change mistaken pre-existing NOTE regression. Right-sizing: SOLO, full-diff audit, fan-. deterministic regen whose diff fully inspectable competing claims adjudicate, multi-agent workflow theater (S111/S106 call). rigor lived reading 100% diff HEAD-vs-working checkRd comparison, parallelism. Pinning old tool rejected alternative: roxygen2 isn’t renv.lock (dev-dep), pin needs manual downgrade doesn’t survive renv::restore — adopting less work durable. Reflexes: [tooling version migration legitimate standalone deliverable — isolate content fixes (Learning 106), deliberately ’s chosen task][verify regen reading COMPLETE diff classifying every change intended kind — regen’s correctness “whole diff intended?”, “spot-check looks fine”][prove new codegen tool adds regressions running linter (checkRd) every changed artifact confirming findings pre-exist HEAD — don’t assume NOTE changed file new][newer codegen tool can repair drift source stale committed artifact — treat benefit, disclose (ships)][prefer adopting pinning tool isn’t snapshotted lock — pin isn’t durable across renv::restore]. Apply: deliberate regeneration-tool formatter version bump (roxygen2, styler, codegen) — take commit, audit entire diff, confirm zero new linter findings HEAD.

what: Learning 108 — A large-but-uniform mechanical doc sweep (here 62 \title-ends-in-period checkRd NOTEs) is a legitimate SINGLE deliverable when the edit is ONE kind and the verification is an ORACLE: dry-run a deterministic script and audit every before/after BEFORE writing, then prove via a per-page HEAD-vs-working checkRd diff that ONLY the target NOTE disappeared and NOTHING was added. (S114, owner pick A2 — title-period mop-up across 52 title files + 3 GV @return files = 55 R/ total)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. flagged work — 62 190 .Rd carrying \title end period, plus calcFE’s spurious r clause unbalanced-paren fg formula calcFEFG/calcFG — re-verified true current tree (flag claim, fact; Learning 105/106). blast radius (~55 source files) looks like violates SAFEGUARDS “5 files” rule, rule targets unrelated multi-file scope creep; single uniform mechanical change (remove one trailing period per title) verified oracle different shape. Right-sized ONE deliverable owner blessed scope via pre-RED AskUserQuestion (full sweep vs. narrower; concrete previews; ASCII-labels per [[ascii---question-options]]). SOLO + script + oracle, fan-. tools::checkRd() ground-truth oracle: 0 title-period NOTEs remain changed page gained new note, titles correct. check oracle, parallel agents add variance, safety, “remove one character” — deterministic script reliable 51 hand-edits 51 sub-agents. Edits made roxygen SOURCE man/ regenerated (never hand-edit .Rd). rigor lived two places: (1) dry-run title script printed every title paragraph + /51 audited write (correctly identified title = first roxygen paragraph, including multi-line titles); (2) per-page HEAD-vs-working checkRd comparison (normalize away line numbers, set-diff messages) proving 62 pages period NOTE removed 0 pages gained anything — precise “new problems” proof, scoped 65 changed pages (125 byte-identical HEAD). Confirmed load_all (162 exports) + NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION unchanged. extends Learning 107 (deterministic regen → SOLO full-diff audit) deterministic source sweep. shell backslash trap (recurring — bit S112/S113 ). Rscript -e '... pattern="\\.Rd$" ...' grep("^\\\\title", ...) get mangled Bash tool layer — backslash level stripped, R sees invalid escape ('\.' unrecognized escape) \t tab matches nothing. Fix: WRITE R probe file (Write tool) run Rscript file.R — file contents passed verbatim, shell re-escaping — /use backslash-free constructs: endsWith(f, ".Rd") regex, [.] \\., fixed = TRUE, POSIX [[:space:]]. Reach file-based probe FIRST given documented history. Scope discipline. Period-removal mechanical, left adjacent CONTENT bugs intact, FLAGGED fixed (FM #8): findPedigreeNumber’s title copy-paste findGeneration’s; pedMissingBirth/pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam data docs claim “errors” though error-demo sets; focalAnimals/convertSexCodes grammar. minimal-vs-proper fork data-doc titles (remove period vs. rewrite run-short title + @description) surfaced owner part scope question; owner chose minimal, long titles now checkRd-clean proper rewrite deferred. Reflexes: [large--UNIFORM mechanical doc change one deliverable edit single kind linter/oracle can confirm — SAFEGUARDS “5 files” rule targets unrelated scope creep, one verified uniform sweep][prefer deterministic script hand-edits sub-agents change “remove/replace one token” — DRY-RUN audit every /writing][prove “regressions” per-page HEAD-vs-working linter diff (normalize line numbers, set-diff messages), scoped changed files — don’t eyeball][linter ground-truth oracle, SOLO + script + oracle beats fan-; parallel agents add variance safety trivial mechanical edits][write R probes FILE dodge recurring shell backslash-mangling trap, use endsWith/[.]/fixed=TRUE/POSIX classes][edit roxygen SOURCE titles + regenerate; never hand-edit .Rd][period-removal leaves CONTENT bugs intact — flag copy-paste/garbled titles, don’t fix mechanical pass]. Apply: mechanical lint-cleanup sweep (trailing periods, deprecated tags, formatting) across many files — pose scope owner, dry-run + audit deterministic fixer, regenerate, prove via per-file /linter diff target finding cleared nothing new appeared.

what: Learning 109 — A flagged doc “bug” is often one token inside a larger wrong claim; the correct fix verifies and repairs the WHOLE sentence against ground-truth data, and a regenerated data-doc’s auto-computed \format block is a free oracle for shape claims. (S115, owner pick A — repaired the 4 content bugs S114 flagged)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S114 flagged pedMissingBirth’s data doc saying “representing full pedigree errors” (error-demo set). Loading data verify (Learning 105/106) showed demonstrated error birth_date column ABSENT entirely — sentence’s “5 columns (ego_id, sire, dam_id, sex, birth_date)” therefore ALSO wrong (object 4 columns). flagged token (“errors”) sat inside larger inaccurate claim; fixing token left “missing birth_date column” contradicting “5 columns including birth_date” sentence. Surfaced fork (minimal touch vs. full accuracy) owner via AskUserQuestion grounded probe output (concrete /previews, ASCII-labels per [[ascii---question-options]]); owner chose full accuracy. regenerated .Rd’s auto-generated \format line already read “8 rows 4 columns” — independent corroboration hand-written “5 columns” prose drifted data object. Verify claim, flag. four flags re-checked ground truth editing: pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam row o3 dam_id = s1 (male) s1 sires o1/o2 (male sire dam; 5 columns correct, error clause changed); focalAnimals 1 column (id) / 327 rows; findPedigreeNumber assigns connected-component number (pedNum vector), generation (title verbatim copy findGeneration’s); convertSexCodes’s “standardized codes” number-agreement error. fix states data/function actually . mid-session owner constraint can already-satisfied — confirm, don’t re-. owner interjected pedMissingBirth must “retain characteristic Birth column.” deliverable touched documentation (never data/), constraint already met; right response verify data object post-edit (4 columns, birth_date absent) confirm, change anything (FM #23 inverse — don’t manufacture work constraint ’s already honored). Right-sizing: SOLO firsthand data probes + checkRd oracle. Five deterministic prose fixes whose correctness established printing actual data objects — fan-agents re-print data already printed theater (S111/S113/S114 call). Verification = per-page checkRd HEAD-vs-working diff (0 problems, 0 new) + load_all (162 exports) + data/-unchanged check. Edits roxygen SOURCE, man/ regenerated; never hand-edit .Rd. Used file-based R probes first command, dodging recurring shell-backslash trap (Learning 108) bit S112–S114. Scope discipline. Fixed exactly four flagged items; FLAGGED fix newly-discovered adjacent bugs (FM #8): \code{pedgood} wrong-case cross-reference six error-set docs (dataset pedGood), si.re-vs-sire data/doc column-name mismatch across qc data family, roxygen 8.0.0’s @importFrom must 1 line long errors mod*.R. Reflexes: [flagged doc bug claim one token — verify WHOLE surrounding sentence ground-truth data, correct fix often must repair second, unflagged inaccuracy coherence][load actual data object read columns/rows describing dataset — names lie, \format computed][regenerated data-doc’s auto-generated \format block free oracle shape claims (row/column counts) — cross-check hand-written prose ][mid-session owner constraint may already satisfied deliverable’s scope — verify confirm, don’t re-open work][SOLO + firsthand probes + checkRd oracle deterministic prose fixes; flag (don’t fix) adjacent bugs found along way]. Apply: task fixing flagged documentation error — re-verify flag data, repair entire inaccurate claim (just flagged word), cross-check counts generated \format, flag adjacent bugs separate pass.

what: Learning 110 — A flag can be wrong about a bug’s NATURE and SCOPE, not just its existence; before fixing, disprove your own hypothesis with the oracle, scan the whole codebase for the pattern, and trace “messy” data through its consumer — the principled fix sometimes INVERTS the obvious one. (S116, owner pick A — fixed the 3 adjacent doc/data-doc bugs S115 flagged)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S116 inherited three flags S115 needed re-examination beyond “real?”: (1) Nature wrong. S115’s flag — restatement — called wrapped @importFrom tags “latent NAMESPACE-drop hazard.” fully-reverted regen probe (roxygenise() current source, git diff NAMESPACE + count, git checkout) disproved : roxygen 8.0.0 prints ✖ @importFrom must 1 line long still parses every continuation line — NAMESPACE came back byte-identical (140 importFrom, 0 removed). bug cosmetic lint, regression. (2) Scope undercounted 3×. flag named 3 mod*.R files; file-based detector scanning every @importFrom R/ (checking whether next roxygen line non-tag continuation) found 20 wrapped tags across 10 files, including non-mod files (appServer.R, appUI.R). (3) obvious fix inverted. si.re (pedOne’s si re) looked like corrupted sire rename data; tracing consumer showed fixColumnNames strips spaces periods (si re → si.re → sire) qcStudbook(pedGood) returns canonical sire — si.re intentional raw QC-demo fixture (make.names("si re") .rda creation), renaming gut fixture exists exercise. correct fix docs, data. Disprove hypothesis oracle, cheaply reversibly. single valuable step throwaway regen whose entire output reverted (git checkout -- man/ NAMESPACE DESCRIPTION). converted plausible-sounding inherited claim (“hazard”) measured fact (“cosmetic; NAMESPACE byte-identical”) touched anything — reported correction owner rather quietly proceeding. Verify--claim---flag (Learning 105/106/109) applies characterization , just predecessor’s. Trace “messy” data consumer cleaning . value looks like corruption (si.re) can deliberate test input. Read normalization/validation function (fixColumnNames) run end--end consumer (qcStudbook) see messy form . round-trips canonical value, ’s load-bearing — fix description, datum. Two owner-decisions → two pre-RED gates, grounded evidence. data-vs-doc fork (item 2) 3-vs-10-file boundary (item 3, surfaced scope/nature discovery) posed separate AskUserQuestion recommendation concrete tradeoffs (ASCII-labels per [[ascii---question-options]]); owner chose full-accuracy docs -10-files. discovery changes chosen option means (scope tripled, hazard→cosmetic) warrants re-surfacing, silent expansion (FM #8) silent half-fix. Prove neutral claim oracle, match surrounding style. Item 3’s safety rested NAMESPACE byte-identical real regen (git diff --quiet -- NAMESPACE), reasoning. reformat split -long list multiple single-line @importFrom pkg ... tags wrapped ≤80 chars (deterministic dry-run-first script, audited writing, per Learning 108) — matching authors’ existing wrapping rather collapsing one 180-char line. Regression read confirmed 0 new failures (executable line changed — #' comments). Reflexes: [run reverted regen/build probe measure codegen tool’s ACTUAL behavior believing flag effect — “✖” lint functional change][scan whole codebase flagged pattern deterministic detector — flag’s file list sample, inventory (SESSION_RUNNER evidence-based-inventory applies bug-fix scope )][“cleaning” value looks corrupt, read consumer/normalizer run end--end — ugly raw value may intentional fixture round-trips canonical][correct mis-characterization owner re-pose scope discovery changes chosen option means][prove “won’t change X” claim byte-level oracle (git diff --quiet), reasoning][reformat match surrounding style — multiple ≤80-char single-line tags, one mega-line]. Apply: inherited-flag fix touching generated artifacts (roxygen/NAMESPACE/codegen) example/test data — probe tool’s real behavior, inventory pattern across repo, trace messy data consumer, gate genuine owner-forks, verify neutrality artifact oracle.

what: Learning 111 — For a doc-rewrite that is structurally oracle-checkable but whose QUALITY is not, split the verification: do the edits SOLO with firsthand probes + oracle (checkRd/load_all/tests), then fan out an adversarial critic panel for the subjective dimension (title aptness, completeness, cross-doc consistency) that no oracle can judge. (S117, owner pick A2 — short-@title rewrite of all 24 data docs)

what: Learning 112 — “Merge X to master” is about origin/master, not the local master branch — git fetch first and reason about the REMOTE topology; a local mainline branch can be far behind, and the repo’s real merge mechanism (PR vs direct push) is encoded in its history. (S118, owner pick C — merge add-methodology → master)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Asked “merge add-methodology master”, naive read local fast-forward: local master (4790b64f) clean ancestor add-methodology, 183 behind. git branch -vv showed local master [origin/master: behind 168] — LOCAL master stale red herring; real target origin/master (7a8433b3 = “Merge pull request #51 rmsharp/add-methodology”). git fetch, add-methodology origin/master DIVERGED: 18 ahead / 3 behind — 3 “behind” commits merely PR #41/#43/#51 merge bubbles (master accumulates merge commits; add-methodology stays linear never pulls back). Fork point 14032640 (S100); unmerged work S101–S117 (18 commits). repo’s established mechanism GitHub PRs (commit add-methodology, 6175fe66, even records “PR #51 merged master”). correct action fresh PR #52, local merge. Fetch reasoning remote branches; trust origin/<branch>, local. Remote-tracking refs fresh last fetch, local mainline branch can sit hundreds commits behind remote (168). first move “merge/compare/rebase master” task git fetch, reason origin/master. [behind N] annotation git branch -vv tell local branch stale. Read merge mechanism history choosing one. run “Merge pull request #N ” commits target — plus feature branch stays linear never contains merge nodes — means project merges via PRs never reconciles feature branch backward. Matching (open PR #52) keeps history consistent; local ---ff merge + direct push deviate may hit branch protection. Confirm mechanism outward main-branch action even explicit instruction. “Merge master” authorizes goal, pushing/merging main branch hard reverse -merges split real choice — one-question gated AskUserQuestion (-merge vs -merge vs local) resolved (owner: push + open PR, owner merges). Apply: “merge/sync/compare branch X mainline” task — fetch first, target origin/<mainline>, identify established merge mechanism history, gate outward step.

what: Learning 113 — When the task is to fix an AUTHORIZED SET of flagged bugs, treat that list itself as a sample, not an inventory: after fixing exactly the authorized items, run a completeness scan of the WHOLE artifact for the same bug CLASS and FLAG (don’t fix) the siblings. (S120, owner pick A — fixed the 3 adjacent data-doc content bugs flagged in S117)

what: Learning 114 — Verify a completeness-scanner’s flag firsthand against ALL parallel siblings — a per-group scanner’s finding is itself a sample and can miss a same-text sibling in another scan group. And when one bug CLASS recurs across many sessions, propose a single batch sweep as the better deliverable. (S121, owner pick A4 — fixed the 2 same-class data-doc bugs S120 flagged)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings

what: Learning 115 — The batch sweep that Learning 114 proposed paid off: one oracle-grounded audit of all 24 data docs found 10 discrepancies where the serial cadence found ~1-2/session. An accuracy audit also surfaces a 2nd-order class — “data-artifact” bugs (the data itself looks degraded), which is the OWNER’s call, not the auditor’s — and the adversarial pass must BOUND scope (reject non-claims), not just confirm. (S122, owner pick A5 promoted to a full 24-doc audit + fix)

what: Learning 116 — Re-exporting an opaque / non-reproducible bundled data object = COERCE the existing object’s types in place (preserve every value) via a committed data-raw/ script — never re-derive from source. Investigate provenance + dependents FIRST to prune unsafe options before the scope gate, and keep the .rda + its doc + man/ + tests in ONE deliverable so you don’t reopen the doc-mismatch class. (S123, owner pick A6 — re-export rhesusPedigree with corrected column types)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Learning 115’s flagged “data-artifact” (owner’s deferred A6) became session’s deliverable: rhesusPedigree shipped degraded types — id/sire/dam/birth factors, exit -NA logical — stringsAsFactors/obfuscation-era artifact. fix re-exported object canonical types matching examplePedigree (id/sire/dam → character, birth → Date, exit → Date -NA), preserving every value (dim 375×8, ids, NA patterns, BRI2MW birth 1998-12-06, date range). Strict TDD: pre-RED scope gate (Type-correctness, full canonical match — gratuitous sex widening), pre-RED reproducibility gate (add data-raw/), RED (31 assertions, confirmed failing) → GREEN (coerce + doc + man regen, green) → REFACTOR (N/; flagged adjacent redundant test conversions). Verified: new tests pass, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, 162 exports, real runtime smoke test. Coerce--place, never re-derive, generator non-reproducible. read-provenance investigation (workflow) established .rda committed generator: obfuscated inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv via obfuscatePed() hand-saved 2020, never scripted seeded. Obfuscation non-deterministic — re-running CSV produce DIFFERENT obfuscated ids/dates shipped object (desync sibling objects like rhesusGenotypes share ids). sound re-export load existing object coerce column TYPES leaving VALUES untouched (.character, .Date(.character(...)), typed -NA Date). committed data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R encodes exactly constraint, idempotent, establishes reproducibility pattern opaque .rdas can follow. Lesson: “re-export data” mean “regenerate source” — opaque/obfuscated artifact means value-preserving type coercion, data-raw script’s job make transform reproducible, reinvent original generation. Investigate provenance + dependents scope gate, owner never offered foot-gun. pre-RED investigation pruned option set: “re-derive CSV” eliminated non-determinism, “drop exit column” eliminated getPotentialParents() reads ba$exit (dropping breaks object’s main consumer). plausible-sounding options un-investigated gate might offered. verifying firsthand test asserts current factor/logical types (fix breaks nothing) exit required, gate posed contained genuinely-safe choices. Test value-preservation, just types; smoke-test real consumer SHIPPED object. Reflexes: [re-exporting opaque/non-reproducible data object = coerce existing object’s TYPES place, preserve VALUES, via committed idempotent data-raw/ script — never re-derive source (non-deterministic obfuscation changes shipped values desyncs sibling objects)][investigate provenance + dependents scope gate PRUNE unsafe options (: re-derive--CSV, drop-required-column) owner chooses among safe ones][data change R/data.R doc + man/ regen ONE deliverable — splitting reopens data-doc-mismatch class (S114–S122)][RED tests type fix must pin VALUE preservation (dim, NA counts, known id→value pairs, ranges), types][Phase 3E DATA change = run real consumer SHIPPED object test-harness conversions — “suite passes” can masked now--op conversions]. what: Learning 117 — When re-exporting a SIBLING opaque object on a predecessor’s one-line characterization, re-derive the FULL degradation firsthand — the named symptom (“id still factor”) can be the least consequential of several, and a “cosmetic type fix” can intersect downstream code semantics (factor-indexing) so scope becomes a CORRECTNESS decision, not a cosmetic one. Flag the surfaced code fragility; keep the data change atomic. (S124, owner pick A7 — re-export rhesusGenotypes with corrected column types)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings

Owner chose coerce three columns (full type-correctness). coerce--place + data-raw/ + atomic doc/man/test pattern Learning 116; full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, idempotent, independent CSV cross-check (three columns identical shipping inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv). predecessor’s one-line characterization lead, spec — re-derive full degradation live object. “id still factor” true framed task cosmetic id-. Probing object showed real degradation three columns; probing consumer (addGenotype) showed named column irrelevant one. implemented literal flag, ’d shipped half-degraded object still produced inconsistent genotype codes. Reflexes: [predecessor’s one-line scope label (“X still factor”) lead — re-derive FULL degradation firsthand; named symptom may least consequential column][calling type coercion “cosmetic,” trace affected column flows consumers — factor-vs-character changes index/c() semantics, hygiene fix can secretly correctness fix][data fix exposes latent CODE fragility (: addGenotype mis-indexing factor allele columns), FLAG separate deliverable — keep data re-export atomic (FM #8)][reuse Learning 116 pattern: coerce--place via idempotent data-raw, bundle doc+man+tests, value-preserve tests + independent cross-check (shipping CSV) + consumer--shipped-object smoke test]. what: Learning 118 — When realizing a predecessor’s flagged CODE fix, decide the fix’s HOME by the call graph, not the doc contract — direct callers that bypass the documented “gate” mean the only complete fix lives at the point of use. Coerce at the TOP of the function (not just at the buggy index expression) to also neutralize an adjacent version-fragile path. Drive the RED test from a minimal fixture whose per-column factor codes DIVERGE from the global sort. (S125, owner pick A9 — harden addGenotype() against factor allele columns)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S124’s Learning 117 flagged addGenotype() fragile factor allele columns — genoDict[genotype[, 2L]] indexes name-keyed integer vector factor, R uses factor’s INTEGER CODES labels, giving encoding inconsistent two allele columns. S124 deliberately fix function (FM #8 — kept data re-export atomic) pre-named code fix A9. session realized . Strict TDD: pre-RED scope gate (addGenotype-vs also-checkGenotypeFile), RED (two failing tests, 2 failed / 0 error) → GREEN (2-line coercion; green) → REFACTOR (one-line @details note + man/ regen). Verified: full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE byte-identical, diff confined three files, Phase-3E smoke factor-coerced copy real rhesusGenotypes (first/second identical character path; max distinct codes per allele = 1; 35-allele dictionary 10001–10035). fix’s HOME decided call graph, doc contract. addGenotype’s roxygen says genotype “provided checkGenotypeFile checked” — reads invitation harden upstream gate. read-breadth scan refuted complete answer: addGenotype DIRECT callers never touch checkGenotypeFile (roxygen example passing rhesusGenotypes, test_addGenotype.R, test_geneDrop.R), gate-coercion leave every direct caller exposed. fix closes bug callers point use, inside addGenotype. breadth scan also confirmed bug isolated (sibling function indexes named vector possibly-factor column), scope stayed narrow. Lesson: function’s doc says “input pre-validated upstream,” verify actually calls locating fix gate — unenforced contract comment, guarantee. Coerce TOP, buggy index expression. breadth agent’s suggested patch wrapped just two index lookups (genoDict[.character(genotype[, 2L])]). fixes lookup, dictionary BUILD lines (sort(unique(c(genotype[, name1], genotype[, name2])))) still relies c() combining two factors — behavior differs across R versions (pre-4.1 c() dropped factor attributes returned integer codes, mis-name dictionary). Coercing columns character top function makes build lookup operate labels, one place, version-robustly. hardening fix one downstream use unsafe value, normalize source rather patching use site. minimal RED fixture index--factor bug must make per-column codes DIVERGE global order. naive fixture (columns allele set sorted order) reproduce bug — column’s factor codes happen align global sort(unique()) positions, buggy correct encodings coincide. bug manifests column’s factor level set/order differs global combined order. minimal trigger: first_name = c("","b"), second_name = c("b","c") — globally →10001, b→10002, c→10003, per-column factor codes (,b→1,2 b,c→1,2) make allele “b” encode 10002 one column 10001 . Designing fixture required tracing exact index arithmetic, just “make factor.” Character input oracle: assert factor-input output identical character-input output. Reflexes: [locate flagged fix CALL GRAPH — direct callers bypass documented validation “gate,” complete fix lives point use, gate (unenforced “input pre-checked” doc comment, guarantee)][confirm bug isolated breadth scan sizing scope, verify scan’s load-bearing claims firsthand][unsafe value multiple downstream uses, normalize SOURCE (top function) — patching obviously-buggy use site can leave adjacent version-fragile path (c()--factors dict build)][RED fixture index--factor bug must make per-column codes DIVERGE global sort — equal/aligned sets won’t reproduce ; use character-input output oracle][realize predecessor’s deferred code-fix flag RED→GREEN→REFACTOR deliverable — bundle data change surfaced (FM #8/#18)]. Apply: session implementing fix prior session flagged--deferred — re-derive bug firsthand, decide fix’s home actually calls function (doc), normalize unsafe inputs source, build RED fixture actually reproduce (index--factor bugs, diverge per-column codes).

what: Learning 119 — A data-doc @source/provenance citation is NOT an attribute of the live object, so an audit-vs-live-object pass structurally cannot catch it — verify it against the FILESYSTEM (cited file exists AND is value-identical to the object), and drive the RED probe off the GENERATED man/.Rd so the failing assertion binds to the rendered artifact (which also proves the regen ran). Reflexes: [@source/provenance citation live-object attribute — verify FILESYSTEM (cited file exists + value-identical), since audit-vs-live-object pass catch phantom source file][bind RED probe GENERATED man/.Rd (parse \emph{}/source token), roxygen source — doubles regen check][fixing one doc-claim instance, sweep whole class (grep every sibling citation) flag others candidate fixes, don’t fix (FM #8)][doc-@source fix Phase-3E-N/— runtime behavior changes; proportionate build-equivalent checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE byte-diff + man-source probe]. Apply: data-doc provenance-citation fix — verify cited file disk (existence + value-identity), drive RED rendered man page, grep every sibling citation hand next session located list.

what: Learning 120 — When more than one shipped file is value-identical to an opaque data object, a filename-based provenance guess can mislead — verify each candidate against the object firsthand and cite the exact-shape value-identical export, not a superset. To close the LAST instance of a doc-claim class, re-run the class sweep after the fix to PROVE closure; don’t inherit “this is the only one left” from a predecessor’s note. (S127, owner pick A11 — correct the rhesusPedigree @source)

Reflexes: [filename — even one prior script calls “source” — HYPOTHESIS provenance; confirm shipped file value-identical comparing values firsthand citing ][multiple files value-identical, cite exact-shape twin (columns), superset extra columns][close LAST instance doc-claim class, re-run class sweep fix show every member resolves — prove closure, don’t inherit predecessor’s note][reuse Learning 119: provenance = filesystem check + value-identity, RED probe binds generated man/.Rd]. what: Learning 121 — A REFACTOR-only deliverable (delete provably-redundant code) still runs the strict-TDD gate, but with NO RED phase — the existing green suite IS the safety net; you need only a pre-REFACTOR scope gate + the GREEN→REFACTOR gate. Prove “no-op” by whole-frame identical(with, without), not per-column class checks, and sweep the file with a robust single-quoted ERE grep — the handoff’s named line-ranges can be incomplete, and a BRE \|/\$ sweep silently matches nothing on macOS (BSD) grep. (S128, owner pick A8 — remove redundant test no-op conversions)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S123 flagged A8: test_getPotentialParents.R test_modPotentialParents.R re-coerced rhesusPedigree-sourced fixtures’ id/sire/dam character birth Date — conversions became -ops S123 re-exported rhesusPedigree canonical column types (Learning 116). deliverable delete . Strict TDD RED: established GREEN baseline (files pass), proved -op precondition firsthand, gated scope (blocks) GREEN→REFACTOR, deleted 12 lines across three identical-class blocks, re-verified (files pass, full suite 0/0, diff confined deletions). 0 stakeholder corrections. pure refactor RED phase — existing passing suite test. TDD’s “never refactor without test” already satisfied: code cleaned test code passes must pass . new behavior drive failing test, writing one theater. apply gate sequence: separate pre-REFACTOR scope decision (author’s call) plus GREEN→REFACTOR transition gate. Declaring RED phase-invention error; skipping gates discipline error. Correct shape: GREEN (prove baseline) → scope gate → GREEN→REFACTOR gate → REFACTOR → re-prove GREEN. Prove “-op” whole-frame identity, column class. class(col) == "character" shows conversion probably idle; airtight proof removing preserves behavior identical(frame---conversions, frame-). one check covers every column attribute generalizes: fixture sourced object inherits proof (pedOne pedDF draw nprcgenekeepr::rhesusPedigree, one identity check licensed deleting three blocks). handoff’s named line-ranges lead, census — sweep file . S127’s handoff named two blocks; robust ERE sweep found THIRD identical block file (pedDF, lines 116-119) never enumerated, plus two look-alikes class (.Date inside age arithmetic test_fillBins.R:22; .character inside expect_setequal test_modGeneticValue.R:1274). Leaving third left file internally inconsistent. first sweep used double-quoted BRE (.character(pedOne\$...\|...) matched NOTHING — macOS grep (BSD) honor \| alternation treat \$ intended — shell/backslash trap drives project file-based R probes (Learnings 108/109), now seen grep. Fix: single-quoted grep -rEn real ERE. Reflexes: [delete-redundant-code refactor RED phase — existing green suite safety net; run pre-REFACTOR scope gate + GREEN→REFACTOR gate, don’t invent RED probe][prove conversion/transform true -op whole-frame identical(, without), per-column class checks — one identity check licenses every fixture drawn object][treat handoff’s named line-ranges lead, census — sweep file/dir class single-quoted grep -rEn ERE; classify look-alikes (coercions inside arithmetic/assertions redundant self-assignments) leave ][shell-backslash trap extends grep: double-quoted BRE \|/\$ silently matches nothing macOS BSD grep — use single quotes + -E][test-refactor Phase-3E-N/— runtime/production code changes; proportionate build-equivalent targeted files green + full suite 0/0 + diff confined intended deletions]. Apply: “remove now-redundant code” cleanup — prove redundancy true -op (whole-frame identity), sweep file/dir robust ERE grep rather trusting handoff’s line list, run scope + GREEN→REFACTOR gates without inventing RED phase.

what: Learning 122 — A carried documentation nit’s wording is a LEAD, not a spec — verify the ground truth before applying it literally; the literal fix can be wrong. A vignette doc-fix binds RED to the .qmd source + the live object (not a generated man/.Rd), and its build-equivalent is a pkgdown/Quarto render — which writes HTML to the gitignored pkgdown_site/ but LITTERS the tracked tree (pkgdown/ favicons, a *.rmarkdown intermediate, an auto vignettes/articles/.gitignore); clean those before commit. (S129, owner pick A3 — fix the studbook-QC vignette’s pedGood column description)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S116 flagged A3 (carried S117–S128): Studbook Quality Control article said pedGood “deliberately messy headers (ego_id, si.re, dam_id, birth_date)” — one-line carried framing “omits sex column.” Strict TDD: pre-RED approach gate (rewrite lead-vs append clause; owner chose rewrite) + PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR (N/— prose, structure preserved). RED: file-based probe parsing lead-paragraph .qmd binding names(pedGood) — 3 ground-truth preconditions pass, 2 RED assertions (lead-mentions sex; states “five”) fail. GREEN: 3-line rewrite → 0 failures. Build-equivalent: pkgdown::build_article(...) rendered clean; new sentence unchanged 4-rename changedCols output verified HTML. 0 stakeholder corrections. literal carried wording produced WRONG fix. “Omits sex column” reads “add sex parenthetical.” probe live object showed sex messy header: names(pedGood) = ego_id, si.re, dam_id, sex, birth_date, article’s changes chunk renames exactly four non-sex columns — sex absent changedCols already uses canonical name. Inserting sex “deliberately messy headers” list mislabeled contradicted chunk’s rendered output two lines . accurate fix conveys “five columns: four messy + already-clean sex.” earlier, richer S116 framing (“dataset 5 columns”) survived notes 350 lines ; compressed one-liner propagated forward lost nuance. Verify claim live object applying . vignette doc-fix binds RED source .qmd + live object — generated man/.Rd bind . Learning 119’s man-page-binding pattern transfer directly: vignette checked-generated artifact. strongest available binding () ground-truth preconditions read live object (names(pedGood)), test fails data ever diverges prose, plus (b) RED assertions source paragraph’s text. render separate build-equivalent proves .qmd still compiles prose now matches chunk output. pkgdown::build_article writes gitignored output dir litters tracked tree — clean commit. HTML landed pkgdown_site/articles/... (gitignored, line 39), never showed git status. render created three NEW untracked items tracked tree: pkgdown/ (generated favicons), vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.rmarkdown (Quarto intermediate), vignettes/articles/.gitignore (Quarto auto-created). Capture git status --porcelain render, rm -rf pkgdown && rm -f vignettes/articles/.gitignore vignettes/articles/*.rmarkdown , re-check tree back intended set. (family stray-Rplots.pdf gotcha — build step can drop artifacts commit must exclude.) Reflexes: [treat carried/handoff nit’s wording HYPOTHESIS — verify live object applying ; literal fix can wrong (, sex messy header, must join “messy headers” list)][vignette/.qmd doc-fix, bind RED source paragraph + live-object ground truth (names()), since generated man/.Rd; pkgdown/Quarto render separate build-equivalent][render changed article verify new prose nearby executed chunk’s output stays consistent ][pkgdown::build_article/quarto render writes HTML gitignored pkgdown_site/ litters tracked tree (pkgdown/, *.rmarkdown, auto .gitignore) — snapshot git status , clean , re-check][vignettes/articles/ website-(.Rbuildignored) — CRAN ship, NEWS line, quarto/pkgdown dev-lib ]. Apply: documentation/vignette fix carried one-line nit — re-derive ground truth firsthand, drive RED source + live object, render build-equivalent, clean render’s tracked-tree litter committing.

what: Learning 123 — To rewrite a large doc section deterministically, write the new section to a FILE and SPLICE it in by heading boundaries with R (read lines, find the start heading + the next heading, recombine head/new/tail) — far more robust than a fragile multi-hundred-line Edit match. For a NEWS rewrite: bind RED to the rendered NEWS.md; fold the user-facing delta from the per-session CHANGELOG/handoffs (DROP internal/test/VCS sessions); then adversarially verify completeness (vs git show HEAD:NEWS.md + the plan’s classification), accuracy (every named function exists/exported), and dedup/style BEFORE tightening prose. rmarkdown::render(output_format="github_document") drops an untracked NEWS.html preview — clean it before commit. (S130, owner pick B — CRAN Phase 3a, the NEWS 2.0.0 rewrite)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. CRAN plan’s Phase 3 headline rewrite single verbose, internally-doubled 1.1.0.9000 NEWS section terse # nprcgenekeepr 2.0.0 Major/Minor entry fold S112–S129 user-facing changes. plan (line 154) authorized splitting content-rewrite version-bump+regenerate; owner chose split, session delivered NEWS rewrite + re-render (Phase 3a). Strict TDD: scope gate (full vs split) + three transitions. RED: probe bound rendered NEWS.md (one 2.0.0 heading; 1.1.0.9000 gone; Major+Minor; NEW-/PED-/XARCH-; addGenotype + rhesus data folded ; module mechanics dropped) — 6 fail → 0 rewrite+render. 3-lens adversarial workflow (completeness/accuracy/dedup-style) returned 0 completeness, 0 accuracy, 0 dedup findings 6 style nits; REFACTOR tighten resolved probe staying 7/7. 0 stakeholder corrections. Splice heading boundary, Edit-match hundreds lines. old section spanned 178 lines. Edit requires entire old_string match byte--byte — fragile error-prone size. Instead: Write new section scratch file, R splice — x <- readLines("NEWS.Rmd"); v <- grep("^# nprcgenekeepr 1\\.1\\.0\\.9000", x); w <- grep("^# nprcgenekeepr 1\\.0\\.8 ", x); writeLines(c(x[seq_len(v-1)], readLines(newfile), "", x[w:length(x)]), "NEWS.Rmd"). Deterministic, keys stable heading anchors, leaves prior history untouched. REFACTOR re-splice keyed new 2.0.0 heading way. (document-section cousin dry-run-first R-script edits used roxygen sweeps S114/S116.) Fold user-facing delta per-session CHANGELOG, classifying ship-vs-internal. plan’s §6.3 classification predated S112–S129, recent sessions folded . per-session CHANGELOG entries source: ship/user-facing ones (S123/S124 data-type re-exports; S125 addGenotype behavior) became Minor bullets, many shipped help/data-doc corrections (S112/S114–S117/S120–S122/S126/S127) collapsed ONE omnibus “Documentation” Minor bullet rather 12 lines. non-shipping sessions — S113 (roxygen tooling), S118/S119 (PR/VCS), S128 (test-fixture cleanup), S129 (website-article) — correctly DROPPED. handoff’s [news-vs-changelog] note (“ships → fold NEWS” vs “website-→ NEWS line”) classification signal; trust confirm change actually touches. Bind RED rendered artifact, adversarially verify user-facing release artifact polishing. man-page doc fixes (Learning 119), probe binds GENERATED NEWS.md (just NEWS.Rmd), also proves re-render ran. CRAN-facing release note, 3-lens workflow earned keep: completeness compared draft OLD content (git show HEAD:NEWS.md), plan’s Major/Minor lists, CHANGELOG delta; accuracy grepped NAMESPACE/R/ confirm every named function exists exported cross-checked behavior claim; dedup/style checked house convention. Run verify style-tighten tighten can’t silently drop fact — token-presence sweep confirms . github_document render litters NEWS.html. rmarkdown::render(output_format = "github_document") html_preview = TRUE default, drops untracked NEWS.html next NEWS.md (shipped artifact). NEWS.html gitignored — remove commit (render html_preview = FALSE). family pkgdown render litter (Learning 122) stray Rplots.pdf: build step can emit artifacts commit must exclude — snapshot git status , clean . Reflexes: [replace large contiguous doc region, Write replacement file splice stable heading anchors R — never hand-match hundreds lines one Edit][fold NEWS/changelog delta per-session CHANGELOG, using handoff’s news-vs-changelog note classify ship-vs-internal; collapse many small shipped doc fixes one omnibus bullet][bind RED probe rendered NEWS.md also proves re-render ran][adversarially verify user-facing release artifact (completeness vs git show HEAD: + plan + CHANGELOG; accuracy vs NAMESPACE/R/; dedup/style) tightening prose, token-sweep confirm tighten dropped fact][rmarkdown::render(github_document) drops untracked NEWS.html preview — clean commit]. Apply: large documentation-section rewrite, especially NEWS/release-notes rewrite — splice deterministically, fold delta per-session history, RED-bind rendered file, adversarially verify, tighten clean render litter.

what: Learning 124 — For a version bump / metadata change with NO new behavior, do strict TDD by binding RED to the version-consistency invariant (every version-bearing artifact == target; stale strings absent), not to a function’s behavior. Re-render generated docs with rmarkdown::render(output_format = <fmt>(html_preview = FALSE)) to avoid the HTML-preview litter outright (improves on Learning 123’s clean-up-after). getVersion() tracks DESCRIPTION under load_all, so re-rendering README auto-updates its version line; its date is sessioninfo-derived (provisional, not literal-today). A version retitle in one file leaves dangling cross-references in other docs — sweep for them. (S131, owner pick B-cont — CRAN Phase 3b, the 2.0.0 version bump)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. CRAN plan Phase 3 split (Learning 123): S130 NEWS rewrite (Phase 3a), leaving version bump Phase 3b. deliverable purely mechanical — DESCRIPTION:4 Version 1.1.0.9000 → 2.0.0, re-render README.md, CITATION.cff version 1.0.7 (stale) → 2.0.0 + date-released field — two version-dependent tests (test_getVersion.R, test_appUI_version.R) dynamic (read packageVersion()), bump make existing test go red--green. new behavior. Strict TDD still applied: two pre-RED scope gates (version-consistency ; defer carried NEWS Minor→Major promotion) + three transitions, GREEN→REFACTOR declared N/. Full suite stayed 191 files / 0-0. 0 stakeholder corrections. RED surface -new-behavior change invariant, behavior. deliverable changes configuration/metadata rather logic, bind RED property change must establish. property version-string inventory consistent: file-based probe parsed DESCRIPTION (read.dcf(..., fields="Version")), CITATION.cff (version: line), README.md (rendered version line) asserted three equal target stale strings (1.1.0.9000, 1.0.7) gone, plus date-released field exists — 7 assertions, failing current tree, passing edits + re-render. metadata-bump analog binding RED rendered NEWS.md (Learning 123) generated man/.Rd (Learning 119): probe verifies deliverable’s invariant, existing dynamic tests verify runtime path (appUI() -tab render = Phase 3E). probe transient (removed commit); permanent test added unless owner asks (offered, declined). html_preview = FALSE beats clean--. Learning 122/123 note rmarkdown::render(github_document) drops untracked NEWS.html/preview must cleaned. Passing output_format = rmarkdown::github_document(html_preview = FALSE) suppresses source — render produced intended README.md change, litter remove. Prefer snapshot--clean re-rendering github_document. getVersion() tracks DESCRIPTION load_all; README date sessioninfo-derived. README.Rmd:10 calls nprcgenekeepr::getVersion(), pkgload::load_all packageVersion("nprcgenekeepr") returns dev DESCRIPTION version — editing DESCRIPTION re-rendering README auto-updates version line; README.Rmd needs edit (§3.1 “edit — auto-tracks” list, like appUI panel). getVersion()’s date comes sessioninfo::package_info(), source package environment-derived date (2026-06-17, literal today 2026-06-18) — README date can differ hand-set date-released. provisional reconfirmed actual submission (Phase 5); force match. version retitle leaves dangling pointers. S130 retitled NEWS 1.1.0.9000 section 2.0.0; closing sweep old version string found ROADMAP.md:6 still says “see NEWS.md 1.1.0.9000” — now dangling cross-reference section longer exists. Also CLAUDE.md:18 carries stale “(Version 1.1.0.9000)” prose nprcgenekeepr_notes.txt:5 now-resolved TODO. .Rbuildignored dev docs (CRAN artifacts) locked three-file scope — flagged follow-, fixed mid-session (FM #8). lesson: version rename (just numeric bump), grep whole tree old string triage hits bumpable / historical-keep / dangling-pointer. Reflexes: [config/metadata change new behavior, bind RED change’s invariant (version-string inventory consistent; stale strings absent), function’s behavior — metadata-bump cousin RED-binding rendered artifact][verify plan’s version-string inventory (§3.1) live tree git grep editing, split hits bumpable vs historical-must--bump vs dangling-pointer][re-render github_document html_preview = FALSE avoid preview litter outright][getVersion()/packageVersion() track DESCRIPTION load_all, re-rendering README auto-updates version line — README.Rmd needs edit; date sessioninfo-derived provisional][version retitle, sweep whole tree old string — rename leaves dangling cross-references dev docs]. Apply: version bump metadata-change — drive RED consistency invariant, verify inventory tree, re-render html_preview=FALSE, triage every lingering old-version hit.

what: Learning 125 — When a plan marks a phase “open,” a prior session may have already executed it — verify against process history (git log on the phase’s target files + the CHANGELOG + the handoff chain) BEFORE re-running; a stale status propagates through many handoffs unreconciled. And a string fixed only at its canonical source can survive in its DOWNSTREAM SINKS — a generated file, a child-include rendered into multiple places, and a hand-maintained mirror — so sweep the whole tree and scope the invariant probe to git-TRACKED files (an unscoped sweep flags untracked render litter as false offenders). (S132, owner pick B-Phase1 re-scoped — finish the mulatto→mulatta typo across README/vignette/CITATION)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. owner picked “CRAN Phase 1 static hygiene.” Orientation’s process-history check (memory [[check-process-history--rerunning-work]]) found Phase 1 already executed S102 (commit a3cf3623) — .Rbuildignore cruft lines, DESCRIPTION mulatto→mulatta typo, renv Config/* reordering, VignetteBuilder: knitr, @return/\value docs, LICENSE-year reconcile — verified place live tree. Yet plan (authored S101) never marked done, handoff chain S102→…→S131 carried “Phase 1 … still open” forward; S131’s gotcha even named DESCRIPTION:23 still carrying typo, S102 fixed. tree-wide mulatto sweep showed S102 fixed typo DESCRIPTION: survived README.Rmd:48 (→ shipped README.md, twice), shipped vignette child vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd:48, CITATION.cff:16. Re-scoped owner finishing typo tree-wide; strict TDD throughout (3 transition gates + pre-RED scope gate; REFACTOR N/); RED probe 9 failures → 0; full suite 0/0; 0 stakeholder corrections. plan’s phase status claim, ground truth — verify process history executing. SESSION_RUNNER’s planning discipline says plan’s “files change” come search results, architectural memory; dual execution time plan’s completion status comes commit record, plan’s text. re-running phase plan calls “open,” git log --oneline -- <phase's target files>, grep CHANGELOG, scan handoff chain evidence already done. two commands (git log -- .Rbuildignore DESCRIPTION → a3cf3623 … CRAN Phase 1 static hygiene (S102), grep -ni 'phase 1' CHANGELOG.md) proved Phase 1 complete saved session re-settled work. stale status propagates: S101’s plan said open, S102 work without marking plan done, every handoff since copied “Phase 1 open” forward. fix twofold — verify executing, reconcile artifacts (plan + CHANGELOG) find drift, next session inherits truth instead re-discovering . fix canonical source can survive downstream sinks. S102 corrected typo DESCRIPTION (canonical Description field) reasonably treated handled. species name lives five independent sinks: (1) hand-maintained mirror — CITATION.cff’s abstract duplicates Description prose (cffr regenerate DESCRIPTION, cffr absent, drifted); (2) generated file — README.md knit README.Rmd; (3) child-include — README.Rmd states reference directly (line 48) child-includes _introduction.Rmd (line 32), carries block, typo renders README.md twice; (4) child ships a3manual.Rmd vignette; (5) website-config mirror — _pkgdown.yml’s description: field also copies Description prose, typo appear published pkgdown site. plan’s location list (“DESCRIPTION:23 + CITATION.cff:15”) missed README, vignette, _pkgdown.yml sinks. Lesson: fixing string appears user-facing prose, sweep whole tree trace generation/include/mirror chains — fixing source fixing output. Scope tree-wide invariant probe git-tracked files — , curated dir list. Two opposite scoping errors bit session caught. () broad: RED probe’s first sweep flagged untracked, git-ignored local render litter (vignettes/a3manual.html|md, stale builds) offenders; filtering git ls-files fixed — invariant committed/shipped content, working tree. (b) narrow: scoped sweep hand-picked dir list (R//man//vignettes/ + README.md + DESCRIPTION) — missed _pkgdown.yml, fifth sink. miss caught close-belt--suspenders git ls-files | grep mulatto tracked files. lesson: curated dir list embeds blind spot let typo survive first place. Sweep every tracked file (git ls-files), subtract dev-process-history docs legitimately describe fix (CHANGELOG, SESSION_NOTES, PROJECT_LEARNINGS, docs/planning/*, dev notes) — don’t add back curated content subset. Reflexes: [re-running phase plan calls “open,” verify process history — git log --oneline -- <phase target files>, grep CHANGELOG, scan handoff chain — stale status propagates many handoffs unreconciled][find plan/CHANGELOG drift, reconcile artifacts (mark phase done; correct false status transparently) next session inherits truth, don’t just route around ][typo/string fixed canonical source can survive downstream sinks — generated file, child-include rendered multiple places, hand-maintained mirror, website-config copy — sweep whole tree trace generation/include/mirror chains][scope tree-wide invariant sweep git ls-files (excludes git-ignored render litter) tracked files minus fix-describing dev docs — curated dir list re-creates original blind spot; always run close-git ls-files | grep belt--suspenders pass]. Apply: “resume plan / Phase N” pickup (verify N isn’t already done executing) single-location content/typo fix (sweep tracked files, subtract fix-describing docs, run close-grep).

what: Learning 126 — A plan’s named root cause is a hypothesis: measure before fixing. The documented cause (“timing”) may not reproduce in the current tree, and the profile may instead surface a different real defect the owner re-scopes to. Verify a handoff’s environment/feasibility claim firsthand (the “Phase 2 blocked — devtools/renv absent” premise was stale; base-R R CMD build/check --as-cran need no devtools). Don’t invent a fix where the measurement shows no problem. And encode a check’s invariant with the tool’s OWN mechanism (R’s parser SYMBOL_PACKAGE tokens for pkg::), not a fragile text regex. (S133, owner pick B-Phase2 re-scoped — CRAN Phase 2 archival timing root cause)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. owner picked “CRAN Phase 2 — archival timing root cause.” plan named example/test/vignette elapsed time cause listed gene-drop vignettes + LabKey examples “prime suspects,” also (dragon #1) said measure first; let profile name offender. Profiling — authoritative R CMD check ---cran --timings — showed timing defect reproduce 2.0.0 tree: examples 20s, vignettes 16s, slowest example countLoops 1.43s, zero examples ≥ 5s; “prime suspect” gene-drop vignettes rebuild 16s total (cheap tiny smallPed). profile expose different, genuinely CRAN-blocking finding: Status: 1 WARNING undeclared withr test dependency. Per plan’s STOP--re-scope rule, owner re-scoped session fixing (one Suggests line, strict TDD); re-run ---cran dropped 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING. 0 stakeholder corrections. phase’s premise claim — measure executing, just verify phase’s status (Learning 125). Learning 125 said plan’s open/done status claim verify process history. sibling execution time: plan’s named root cause hypothesis verify measurement. plan says “fix timing” can wrong whether timing still broken — archived defect 1.0.7/1.0.8, post-1.0.8 work (merely faster hardware ) may resolved . plan anticipated exactly (dragon #1, “let profile name offender”) gave STOP--re-scope boundary; honoring — running gold-standard measurement surfacing changed premise owner grounded re-scope question — kept session inventing timing “fix” non-problem. Verify handoff’s environment/feasibility claim firsthand accepting “blocked.” S131 S132 handoffs said Phase 2 “blocked — needs devtools + renv::restore(), absent env.” 20-line env probe disproved : R 4.6.0 renv library already materialized, 18 Imports present, pandoc present — crucially R CMD build/R CMD check ---cran base-R binaries need devtools. real gap couple missing build-Suggests (markdown vignette; installed seconds). “blocked” inherited handoff hypothesis, fact — cost test (one probe script) far cost deferring runnable, critical-path phase. Don’t invent fix measurement shows problem. plan offered timing mechanisms (\donttest, skip_on_cran(), reduce iterations). profile showing unit near limit, applying solve non-problem — reducing simulation iterations risks dragon #2 (“speed-” silently changes GVA/kinship numbers). disciplined output measurement clean say evidence stop, perform plan’s listed actions sake. (owner offered “+guard heaviest examples machine-speed insurance” option declined ; honest framing flagged belt--suspenders slightly reduces CRAN example coverage.) Encode check’s invariant tool’s mechanism, hand-rolled text scan. RED probe mirror R CMD check’s “unstated dependencies tests” check. first-draft regex raw test text false-flagged two non-deps: devtools (appears #' comments) shinytest2.R (filename comment). R CMD check ignores parses code. Rewriting probe use getParseData(parse(f)) collect SYMBOL_PACKAGE tokens — exactly parser resolves package half pkg:: — made comment/string/filename-immune yielded precisely one real offender (withr), matching check. probe must reproduce tool’s code-level judgment, reach tool’s parser/AST, regex approximation . Reflexes: [plan’s named root cause hypothesis — run gold-standard measurement (R CMD check ---cran --timings) applying plan’s fix; documented cause may reproduce][honor plan’s STOP--re-scope boundary: profile contradicts premise, surface owner evidence + grounded re-scope question — don’t barrel listed fix silently narrow][verify handoff’s “blocked / tool absent” claim firsthand quick env probe — base-R R CMD build/check ---cran need devtools; renv may already materialized][measurement clean, say evidence STOP — don’t apply timing fixes units aren’t slow (non-problem + dragon #2 risk)][reproduce tool’s code-level judgment probe, use tool’s parser (getParseData/SYMBOL_PACKAGE), text regex scans comments/strings]. Apply: “fix [named root cause]” pickup (measure cause reproduces first) probe must mirror R CMD check finding (parse, don’t regex).

what: Learning 127 — Verify a spell-flagged word before adding it to WORDLIST: a “misspelling” can be the symptom of a real defect, and adding it masks the defect. A dictionary the plan calls “nearly clean” can be far off — reconcile against the live tool output, not the plan’s guess. And R CMD check --as-cran is a base-R gate that needs no devtools wrappers; for a true gate, install the missing Suggests so nothing is skipped. (S134, owner pick A — CRAN Phase 4 full --as-cran gate)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Phase 4’s gate came back clean first run — Status: 2 NOTEs = 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING (NOTE 1 = expected archived/new-submission incoming-feasibility; NOTE 2 = local-old-HTML-Tidy/-V8, CRAN-absent), timings comfortable, regression read 0 failed / 0 error. interesting work inst/WORDLIST reconciliation plan listed almost afterthought (“confirm EHR/Raboin/kinships remain mulatta fix”). Reality: spelling::spell_check_package() flagged 37 words — dictionary drifted ~35 legitimate code identifiers behind S100–S133 work (changedCols, femaleSires, qcResult, …) — .e. plan’s stated expectation wrong order magnitude. Verifying flagged word source location (blindly running update_wordlist()) paid twice: isolated curly-quote tokenizer artifact (names' quoted base-R error 'row.names' NEWS — faithful quote, word) , importantly, surfaced real data defect. spell flag can visible symptom defect nothing spelling. flagged token si traced column literally named si.re 5 6 example pedigree datasets (pedGood, pedDuplicateIds, pedFemaleSireMaleDam, pedMissingBirth, pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam); 6th (pedInvalidDates) correctly uses sire. si.re almost certainly data defect (sire/sire_id, pair ego_id/dam_id) — spell_check split period flagged si fragment. run update_wordlist() (adds flagged words), si enshrined dictionary defect masked forever. Instead added 35 clean terms deliberately left si flagged stays visible, documented si.re finding session. Lesson: adding flagged word project dictionary, look occurs — ’s fragment malformed identifier, right fix upstream, WORDLIST. owner-approved scope can refined verification reveals — transparently, conservatively. owner approved “add 36 legitimate terms” ’d traced si si.re. Discovering si defect symptom, narrowed 35 (excluding names' si) — conservative approved, serving stated intent (“don’t enshrine artifacts”), reported deviation explicitly owner redirect. New information post-dates approval refine action toward approval’s intent, change surfaced — silently, rigidly executing now-outdated literal instruction. “true” ---cran gate installs Suggests; forced check weaker proxy. S133’s check used _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false covr/shinytest2/shinyWidgets/spelling absent (real partial gate). S134 installed four (+ urlchecker) renv library — installing package shinytest2 install Chrome (chromote fetches lazily, e2e tests skip_on_cran check never launches browser), safe — ran forcing flag, condition CRAN checks .
