Pre-gate hygiene single R CMD check subsumes anyway still worth running explicitly captured evidence: roxygenise() (zero diff → docs sync), urlchecker::url_check() (17/17 correct), spell_check_package() (WORDLIST reconcile). WORDLIST gate-invariant (consumed skip_on_cran tests/spelling.R), changing alter check result — confirmed second rebuild+recheck (identical 2 NOTEs). Reflexes: [adding spell-flagged word inst/WORDLIST, read source location — fragment malformed identifier (si ← si.re) defect symptom; update_wordlist() mask , add clean terms hand leave symptom flagged][treat plan’s “dictionary nearly clean” guess — reconcile live spell_check_package() output; drift accumulates silently code adds identifiers][installing R package shinytest2 install Chrome — safe add true ---cran gate e2e tests skip_on_cran; install missing Suggests rather _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_=false][WORDLIST gate-invariant (skip_on_cran spell test reads ) — don’t re-run full check “safe” WORDLIST-change unless session submission gate][HTML-manual NOTE ('tidy' recent enough / package 'V8' unavailable) local-toolchain artifact, CRAN-absent — document , don’t chase ]. Apply: WORDLIST/spell reconciliation (verify word’s source adding) local ---cran gate (install Suggests true gate; know NOTEs CRAN-absent).

what: Learning 128 — A guessed future identifier (a PR number, a branch name, a “next” version) is a claim that propagates through handoffs unverified — confirm branch/PR/remote state firsthand (gh pr view N, gh pr list --state all, git show origin/<branch>:DESCRIPTION, git merge-base --is-ancestor) before citing it in a runbook or handoff. And adversarially verify an external-facing submission document (a CRAN cover note) with a skeptical fresh-eyes pass — the author, close to the work, misses implied-passes framing, “delete-before-pasting” foot-guns, and over-scoped quantitative claims. (S135, owner pick B-Phase5 scope A — CRAN Phase 5 cover note + runbook)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Phase 5’s local half (rewrite cran-comments.md; write win-builder/R-hub runbook) authored verified repo facts, run 3-lens adversarial verification (repo fact-check + runbook-command/API + skeptical-CRAN-reviewer). Every factual claim cover note confirmed tree, pass caught three classes author-blind defect — one long-propagated false premise. guessed identifier rides handoff chain fact. Every handoff since ~S112 said branch “rides future PR #53 (Learning 112).” : gh pr view 53 → “resolve PullRequest,” GitHub shares one number space issues PRs issues #45/#46 consumed range — next PR later number, 53. Worse, gh pr list --state showed PR #52 already merged carried S101-S117, git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION still Version: 1.1.0.9000 git merge-base ---ancestor e24a53a2 origin/master = — master none 2.0.0 commits. runbook’s first draft repeated “PR #53 / merge master” framing miscast branch risk “older tree, 2.0.0” origin/add-methodology fact already 2.0.0 missing S133’s withr fix (R-hub unpushed tree re-report WARNING win-builder won’t). Lesson: future PR number, “rides PR #N” note, “merge master” prediction; verify gh/git moment depend , never let guessed number sit handoff settled fact (Learning 125/126 — verify inherited status/premises — applied repository plumbing). Adversarially verify external-facing artifact, just code. CRAN cover note read skeptical human reviewer; failure modes rhetorical, compilable, build-equivalent (’s valid markdown) tells nothing. reviewer-lens agent caught , close draft, : (1) listing win-builder/R-hub “## Test environments” results reads implied passes twice-archived package — fixed explicit “run submission” markers; (2) “NOTE MAINTAINER (delete pasting)” block placeholder result numbers inside file pasted CRAN foot-gun (left , ship fake numbers + internal path) — moved entirely runbook cover note CRAN-facing-; (3) “3-5x headroom” true per-example (~1.4s vs ~5s) false per-phase (tests phase ~43s ~1.4x minute) — scoped claim; (4) wording implies deliberate timing fix measured truth “cause reproduce” overclaims timing-archived package — reworded state ’s true point win-builder/R-hub (independent, slower hardware) confirming evidence. None factual errors; framing/honesty risks fresh adversarial reader surfaces author misses. Reflexes: [citing PR number / “merge master” / “rides PR #N” runbook handoff, run gh pr view N gh pr list --state , check target branch’s real version git show origin/<branch>:DESCRIPTION + git merge-base ---ancestor <commit> origin/<branch>][guessed future PR number unreliable — GitHub shares issue/PR number space, intervening issues shift ; don’t enshrine specific number handoff][R-hub v2 checks pushed GitHub tree, win-builder uploads local tarball — local branch ahead, push rhub_check() R-hub re-reports already-fixed problems][keep CRAN cover note CRAN-facing-: “delete--pasting” blocks, placeholder numbers, internal paths — stage runbook][never list test platform “Test environments” without result explicit “pending” marker — archived package reads implied pass][scope quantitative headroom/limit claim unit actually holds (per-example vs per-phase); don’t imply deliberate fix measurement shows never needed]. Apply: runbook/handoff referencing branch/PR/remote state (verify firsthand), external-facing submission/correspondence document (run skeptical fresh-eyes adversarial pass ships).

what: Learning 129 — A firsthand-verified remote-state claim still has a shelf life: a branch can be pushed or changed between sessions, so a prior session’s correct “push first / N commits behind” snapshot can be stale by the time you act on it. git status “up to date with origin/” compares only against the LOCAL tracking ref, which updates only on fetch — so it reads “up to date” both when you already pushed and when your view of the remote is merely stale. Run git fetch THEN git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<branch>...HEAD to get ground truth before acting on an inherited “push first” prerequisite. (S136, owner pick Phase5-finish scope “prep + hand off” — CRAN Phase 5 local prep)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S135’s handoff runbook said origin/add-methodology “2 commits behind — git push rhub_check() R-hub re-reports already-fixed withr WARNING.” true S135 verified (Learning 128 getting exactly right). S135 S136 branch pushed, time S136 ran prerequisite already satisfied — git fetch + git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD → 0 / 0, fully sync HEAD 24175785. session’s job flipped “push” “confirm supersede caveat.” trap nearly hid : orientation git status said “branch date ‘origin/add-methodology’”, safely read “already pushed” — equally means “tracking ref hasn’t fetched.” explicit fetch + rev-list disambiguated. Learning 128’s sibling pointed time rather guessing: 128 says verify inherited/guessed identifier; 129 says even correctly-verified remote snapshot expires — re-fetch moment depend , retire inherited caveat, mark superseded doc (command + result retired ) next reader doesn’t re-run obsolete step. Reflexes: [acting inherited “push first / N commits behind / unpushed commits” prerequisite, run git fetch git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<branch>...HEAD — remote can change sessions, git status “date” reflects last-fetched tracking ref][distinguish “already pushed” “local view remote stale” — surface “date”; fetch disambiguates][superseding prior caveat runbook/plan, write “superseded  ” exact command + result, just silent deletion, supersession auditable][documentation/prep deliverable’s build-equivalent still real: R CMD build . confirm artifact assembles + re-confirm every external-doc fact firsthand current tree, even “nothing changed since gate” — verify unchanged claim (git diff --name-<gate>..HEAD)]. Apply: session inherits branch/remote/push prerequisite, hands “X remote first” instruction later session.

what: Learning 130 — An adversarial verify pass can itself be wrong — cross-check ITS corrections firsthand, not just the synthesis it attacks; and when the evidence genuinely splits, FRAME the decision for the owner instead of manufacturing one “correct” answer. A fan-out’s skeptic struck the synthesis’s recommended target name and argued for a different one, citing the package’s own @example as its evidence — but reading that example firsthand showed it used the opposite form, undercutting the skeptic’s own claim. Both the proposer and its adversary were partly right; only the firsthand file read settled the ground truth. Also re-derive inherited magnitudes: the carried “si.re in 5 of 6 pedigrees” note undercounted — 6 datasets are malformed (a second, space-form variant was missed). (S137, owner pick F — investigate + file the si.re example-data defect as GitHub issue #53)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Auditing si.re malformed-sire-column defect GitHub issue, ran 4-reader → synthesize → adversarial-verify workflow. synthesis recommended renaming malformed column sire_id (symmetric sibling ego_id/dam_id). adversarial pass correctly caught synthesis overstated case (headline “period header contradicts package’s value-level period-rejection rule” false — headers data values separate code paths qcStudbook), argued convention-consistent target instead space form si re, citing R/fixColumnNames.R’s documented @example evidence package’s sire exemplar uses space. take face value: reading R/fixColumnNames.R:16 firsthand, @example actually uses Sire_ID (underscore) — opposite skeptic claimed, evidence synthesis’s underscore idea, skeptic’s space idea. real ground truth (reading files, either agent’s summary): repo ships three messy-sire conventions — si re (space, createPedOne + ~8 local fixtures), Sire_ID (underscore, @example), si.re (period, five .RData files) — period form matches none. neither proposer’s adversary’s single “correct name” right; honest deliverable document defect present target-name choice (si re / sire_id / sire) trade-offs maintainer decision issue. Separately, inherited handoff’s “5 6” framing undercounted: firsthand names() scan every dataset found pedOne also carries malformed si re (space) sire column, making 6 — fixColumnNames() empirically normalizes forms canonical sire, defect internal inconsistency, functional break (confirmed CRAN blocker). Filed issue #53 bug + low priority. Reflexes: [adversarial/verification agent CORRECTS synthesis, re-verify correction’s cited evidence firsthand adopting — skeptic can confidently wrong, “verifier said ” ground truth][two passes disagree decision partial evidence, read primary source ; evidence genuinely splits, deliverable frame choice trade-offs owner, assert false single answer (FM #23 — contested call owner’s)][re-derive inherited count/magnitude (“N M”, “5 files”, “~35 words”) firsthand — carried characterization drifts magnitude, just truth value][shipped-DATA defect, separate “functionally broken” (ingestion/mapping still produce canonical schema?) “internally inconsistent / typo-looking” — run normalizer (fixColumnNames) variant prove calling blocker][audit/issue deliverable’s “build-equivalent” firsthand verification every file:line claim makes + running relevant code path (pkgload::load_all + fixColumnNames), just assembling prose]. Apply: session runs verify/adversarial pass findings (standing practice ultracode), issue/audit must recommend contested decision, fix scoping inherits count “’s broken” claim.

what: Learning 131 — A data/fixture change can break a test that asserts on the SHAPE of derived output (a count, a row total, a newline tally) without that test ever referencing the changed value literally — so a grep-for-the-changed-token pre-flight scan will miss it; the full test suite is the only reliable breakage check. (S138, owner pick — the real fix for issue #53: rename the example datasets’ sire column to sire.id)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. regenerating 6 example pedigrees (si.re/si re → sire.id), ran breakage scan: grep tests/ literal si.re/si re change-log bucket names. reported “test breaks” — every si re tests self-contained local fixture, test referenced shipped data’s sire column literal name. true yet incomplete: full-suite VERIFY surfaced one real failure test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R, counts newlines qcStudbook summary (stri_count_regex(...$txt, "\\n") == 9L). Renaming column sire.id makes normalization fire one EXTRA step (sireIdToSire, sire.id→sireid→sire, whereas si re→sire already canonical), summary gained line count became 10. test never mentions si.re/si re/sire.id — asserts shape output derived data — token grep found . Updated 9L→10L confirming new output correct (reports one real normalization step). fix otherwise clean (full suite 0/0, R CMD check Status: OK). Reflexes: [fixture/data change’s breakage check FULL test suite, grep changed token — tests asserting counts, row totals, lengths, formatted-text shape break invisibly grep][changing example data flows reporting/summary/format function, expect output-shape assertions (newline counts, “N rows”, nrow(...)) shift; re-run ][pin data CONTRACT RED test (exact column names) rather trusting pre-flight grep predict breakage — contract test deterministic, grep heuristic][output-shape count changes, verify NEW value correct updating literal — don’t just bump number make pass]. Apply: session regenerates shipped/example data fixtures consumed reporting/summary/format functions.

what: Learning 132 — A spell/lint flag on a GENERATED file can be a render-time artifact, not a source defect: pandoc’s smart-quotes turn straight '...' in an .Rmd into curly '...' in the .md, and a quote sitting against a word ('row.names' → names') becomes a bogus token. Fix it at the SOURCE in a way the renderer can’t undo — a code span (backticks) bypasses smart-quotes — then RE-RENDER and confirm the regenerated-file diff is confined to the one intended line. Never hand-edit the generated .md. (S139, owner directive — clear S138’s punch-list)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S138 flagged lone names' spell hit NEWS.md:51 (“fix quote, don’t whitelist”). obvious read — “’s curly quote straighten source” — wrong: NEWS.Rmd:53 already used straight ASCII quotes ("duplicate 'row.names' allowed"). curl introduced pandoc’s smart extension render time (github_document); closing ' sits directly names, tokenizer emits bogus names'. nothing “straighten” source. Two tempting wrong fixes: editing NEWS.md directly (’s generated NEWS.Rmd — overwritten, plan gotcha #3), disabling smart globally (curl-strip every quote 500-line file — huge unwanted diff). right fix wraps literal base-R message backticks NEWS.Rmd (`duplicate 'row.names' allowed`): code spans exempt smart-quotes, straight quotes survive, reads better (verbatim error message code). Re-rendered NEWS.md (github_document, html_preview=FALSE) → exactly one-line diff; spell_check_package() 1 flag → 0. discipline README de-dup: edited .Rmd source (removed README.Rmd’s hardcoded citation block, leaving copy renders shared _introduction.Rmd child manual/tutorial vignettes untouched), re-rendered via build_readme(), read git diff confirm confined removed block (+ correct incidental getVersion() date refresh) committing. Reflexes: [spell/URL/lint flag generated file (NEWS.md, README.md, man/*.Rd) — trace SOURCE (.Rmd/roxygen) fix , re-render; never hand-edit generated file (’s overwritten)][name'-style bogus token usually comes pandoc smart-quotes curling straight quote sits word — source may already “correct”; wrap literal code span (backticks) renderer leaves alone, rather disabling smart globally (rewrites every quote file)][editing generated file’s source, RE-RENDER git diff generated file — confirm diff confined intended change; incidental--correct refresh (getVersion() date) fine, storm formatting changes means committed file drifted source — investigate committing][duplicate renders shared child doc + local block, remove LOCAL block keep blast radius one file — deleting shared child changes every consumer (a3manual.Rmd + ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd)][re-run checker fix (spell_check_package() → 0) — don’t assume edit worked]. Apply: fix flag/warning knitr/pandoc/roxygen-generated artifact, edit requires re-rendering NEWS.md / README.md.

what: Learning 133 — Before merging a PR, triage its CI checks: a red check is not automatically a merge-blocker. gh pr view --json mergeStateStatus distinguishes BLOCKED (a required check is failing — do not merge) from UNSTABLE (mergeable; only non-required checks are red). When a check is red, read its log and CLASSIFY it — required vs optional, correctness vs style, introduced-by-this-PR vs pre-existing — and report that classification; don’t merge blind, and don’t refuse a merge the owner directed just because a non-required style check is red. (S140, owner directive “merge #54”)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. PR #54 (2.0.0 merge) came back mergeable: MERGEABLE mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE. gh pr checks 54 showed every R-CMD-check (macOS / Windows / Ubuntu devel+oldrel+release), test-coverage, codecov, pkgdown PASS — exactly one red: lint. Reading failed job log, lint failure ~30 lintr STYLE warnings (return_linter “explicit return() needed”, seq_linter “use seq_along”, coalesce_linter “use %||%”, nonportable_path_linter, boolean_arithmetic_linter) long-standing core files (qcStudbook.R, geneDrop.R, removeDuplicates.R, makeFounderStatsTable.R, mod*.R modules, …) plus CI config wart (cyclocomp package installed → exit 31). None correctness issues, none introduced S139 (docs — zero R code), lint non-required (hence UNSTABLE BLOCKED — GitHub allowed merge). disciplined output surface finding (“red check non-required pre-existing style lint; correctness checks pass”) proceed owner-directed merge — neither merging silently red check refusing . Merge landed: merge commit 46dfc766, origin/master now Version: 2.0.0. trap en route: gh pr view 54 --json merged errors (merged field — use state / mergedAt); field error made successful merge look failed re-checked state: MERGED. Reflexes: [merging, run gh pr checks <N> gh pr view <N> --json mergeStateStatus,mergeable — BLOCKED = required check failing (stop), UNSTABLE = mergeable non-required checks red][check red, open log CLASSIFY (required vs optional, correctness vs style, introduced vs pre-existing) report — merging blind refusing---red wrong][non-required lint/style check failing informational debt, correctness gate — explicit owner merge directive proceeds, finding surfaced][use merge commit (squash) preserve long-lived branch’s per-session history; never --delete-branch unless told][verify merge actually landed (git fetch + git show origin/master:DESCRIPTION + git merge-base ---ancestor <head> origin/master) — don’t trust gh pr merge exit alone; bad --json field query can mask success, confirm state: MERGED / mergedAt]. Apply: PR merge, especially protected/default branch mixed required + informational CI checks. (Records known state: lint RED master — pre-existing lintr style debt; future REFACTOR session clear green.)

what: Learning 134 — Clearing a lint check is a FIXPOINT problem, not a one-pass edit: an autofix that satisfies its TARGET linter can TRIGGER other linters (a cascade), so the only reliable completion signal is re-running lint_package() over the WHOLE tree after the per-file fixes and repeating until 0 — never trust a per-file fixer’s “done.” And a linter message is a suggestion, not a mandate: nonportable_path_linter fires on any /-bearing string (incl. MIME types), coalesce_linter assumes %||% exists — when the literal fix is wrong or unavailable, prefer # nolint (extending the author’s intent) or disabling the linter in .lintr, not the transformation the message names. (S141, owner pick — Lint cleanup → green CI; this clears the exact state Learning 133 flagged)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. CI lint log showed “~30” warnings (Learning 133’s count); firsthand lint_package() found 57 across 33 files. Owner picks: disable coalesce_linter (11), fix data-raw (5), continue add-methodology. .lintr got coalesce_linter = NULL, 46 code fixes remained across 28 files; ran fix→adversarial-review workflow (one fixer + one independent behavior-neutrality reviewer per file, 56 agents). 27 files came back clean; central re-lint read 5, 0 — removeDuplicates.R: boolean_arithmetic autofix sum(duplicated(x)) == 0L → !(duplicated(x)) cleared target triggered three new linters (if_not_else, any_duplicated, unnecessary_nesting). per-file fixer see — knew assigned lints. rewrote function (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL converted stop() branch guard clause ((anyDuplicated(p$id) > 0L) stop(...); p), satisfied four linters preserved exact behavior (sum(duplicated)==0 ⟺ dups ⟺ anyDuplicated > 0 false, including empty-vector edge: duplicated(character(0)) → dups, anyDuplicated → 0). Re-lint → 0. Two context-blind-linter judgments: () nonportable_path_linter flagged MIME-type string modPedigree’s fileInput(accept = c(...)) — author already put # nolint: nonportable_path_linter adjacent "text/csv" line (clear intent: false positive) missed second string; fixer “fixed” wrapping MIME type file.path() (output identical, semantically wrong), restored readable string + extended # nolint. (b) coalesce_linter wants %||%, base R added 4.4, package Depends: R (>= 4.1.0), shiny 1.13 dropped export — adopting silently break R 4.1–4.3, disabling (consistent 7 linters .lintr already disables) correct, transformation. cyclocomp wart: .lintr already NULLs cyclocomp_linter, linters_with_tags() constructs config-load warns cyclocomp absent (non-fatal — clearing lints alone makes CI green); added ::cyclocomp lint.yaml silence log noise. one line_length fix roxygen comment (R/data.R:358) meant generated .Rd stale, regenerated man/ (installed roxygen2 == pinned Config/roxygen2/version: 8.0.0, version-churn) — diff confined man/rhesusPedigree.Rd’s one reflowed line. Final triple: lint_package() = 0, full suite 0/0, R CMD check Status OK (0/0/0), plus firsthand read entire diff. Reflexes: [lint sweep iterative — applying fixes, re-run linter WHOLE package repeat 0; autofix can clear target trigger others (boolean_arithmetic → if_not_else + any_duplicated + unnecessary_nesting), per-file fixers blind cascade][don’t trust fan-’s per-file “fixed” completion signal — authoritative checks central re-lint + full test suite, run ][linter message suggestion, mandate: nonportable_path_linter fires MIME types / URLs, coalesce_linter/undesirable_* assume idiom exists — literal fix wrong (MIME--path) unavailable (%||% R floor), prefer # nolint (extend author’s existing intent) disable linter .lintr (consistent already-disabled set)][check package’s R-version floor adopting “newer idiom” autofix — %||% base since R 4.4; pkg Depends R≥4.1 shiny 1.13 longer exports ][style fix touches roxygen comment, regenerate man/ (confirm installed roxygen2 == pinned Config/roxygen2/version first, avoid version-churn) diff man/ confirm regeneration confined][behavior-neutral REFACTOR’s build-equivalent full triple — lint_package()=0, full suite 0/0, R CMD check Status OK — plus firsthand read entire git diff, fixers’ summaries]. Apply: lint/style/formatter cleanup, session acting linter autofixes, fan-whose per-agent outputs need central re-verification.

what: Learning 135 — To make a CI check’s fix demonstrably green you may have to MERGE it where the workflow runs: a check whose workflow triggers only on pull_request / push-to-default is invisible on a feature-branch push, so the fix’s green status is unverifiable until a PR runs — open the PR, watch all checks, then merge (do not merge blind). And when adopting a default-branch workflow, the local default branch is often badly STALE and git pull may be reconfigured to rebase (pull.rebase=true) — which a standing junk modification (.DS_Store) will block; sync a stale-but-ancestor local branch with git fetch + git reset --hard origin/<branch> (after git merge-base --is-ancestor HEAD origin/<branch> proves it’s an ff), NOT git pull. (S142, owner directive “work on 1, 2, and 3” + branch plan “merge now, switch to master”)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S141 cleared lintr lints add-methodology, repo’s lint GitHub Actions workflow triggers pull_request push main/master — push add-methodology — fix green locally yet lint check stayed RED master CI run anywhere prove fix worked. way see pass open PR. pushed S141’s 2 commits, opened PR #55 (add-methodology→master), watched checks via background gh pr checks 55 --watch → 11/11 PASS, including lint PASS mergeStateStatus: CLEAN (vs PR #54’s UNSTABLE lint red) — merged (--merge, merge commit f44a5322), verifying landed firsthand (merge-base ---ancestor 507de407 origin/master). , executing owner’s “switch master”, hit two git traps sequence: () local master 215 commits stale (never working branch — work add-methodology), (b) git pull failed “pull rebase: unstaged changes” repo pull.rebase=true standing .DS_Store modification (never-commit keep) tree. Rather stash-juggling force-flailing, confirmed local master strict ancestor origin/master (git merge-base ---ancestor HEAD origin/master = YES → hard reset exactly fast-forward tracked content, git status --untracked-files=showed .DS_Store tracked modification, nothing real lose), git reset --hard origin/master → clean merge tip. disposable Phase-1B stub discarded switch (gets rewritten full handoff anyway). standing keeps respected: PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html (untracked) untouched reset; .DS_Store reset committed bytes (keep “never commit ”, “preserve exact local modification”). Reflexes: [CI check RED fix lives branch workflow doesn’t run PROVABLE-green via PR (push trigger branch) — open PR, watch checks (gh pr checks <N> --watch), confirm target check PASS + mergeStateStatus: CLEAN, merge; never merge blind (Learning 133)][use background gh pr checks <N> --watch harness re-invokes CI finishes — don’t poll][merging, verify firsthand landed: state: MERGED + git merge-base ---ancestor <fix-commit> origin/<base> + expected DESCRIPTION version — gh exit alone][local default branch ’ve never used probably FAR behind origin — git fetch check git merge-base ---ancestor HEAD origin/<branch>; YES ’s safe fast-forward][repo pull.rebase=true — git pull becomes rebase ABORTS unstaged change (standing .DS_Store); sync stale--ancestor local branch use git reset --hard origin/<branch> (provably ff-equivalent) instead git pull][reset --hard, prove safety: confirm strict-ancestor git status --untracked-files=shows throwaway tracked modifications — reset --hard discards tracked-file changes leaves genuinely-untracked files (PED_GV_AUDIT keep survives)][“standing keep” junk file (.DS_Store) means never COMMIT , preserve exact local bytes — resetting fine]. Apply: session pushes/merges make CI check green, branch-strategy switch long-dormant local default branch, git pull/reset repo (mind pull.rebase=true).

what: Learning 136 — A technical-EVALUATION research deliverable still obeys the RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION workstream’s claim-source discipline — every technical claim must trace to a file:line / repo:path / CRAN page, not a recollection — and the right machine for it is a multi-agent investigate → ADVERSARIALLY-verify → synthesize pipeline, where the author then independently re-verifies the firsthand-checkable subset (the codebase baseline). The verify stage is not ceremony: it caught 3 nuance refutations + 1 uncertain among 37 load-bearing claims, and the synthesis carried the corrected claims, not the originals. (S143, owner directive — delete add-methodology + research LabKey integration options)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. deliverable evaluation doc (“nprcgenekeepr integrate LabKey; change”) — paper, workstream applies: treated codebase, four LabKey EHR-module repos (owner-supplied primary sources), Rlabkey CRAN/LabKey docs corpus, required every claim cite file:line / repo:path / URL. ran Workflow: 5 parallel investigators (Rlabkey/CRAN; base ehrModules; ONPRC; SNPRC/NIRC; architecture) returning structured {claim, evidence, source, confidence, loadBearing}; pipeline() stage adversarially RE-CHECKED load-bearing claim primary source (prompted refute); synthesis agent drafted full doc, instructed drop/caveat refuted claims flag uncertain ones. 37 load-bearing claims: 33 confirmed, 3 refuted, 1 uncertain — refutations “wrong” much imprecise mechanism (e.g. one thread sourced Id/parents/dam lookup ParentsDemographicsProvider/study.parentageSummary; verifier traced firsthand DefaultEHRCustomizer.java → study.demographicsParents, NOT_IN_DB query; another mis-sourced lastDayAtCenter’s computation; another mis-classified httr/jsonlite Imports CRAN lists Depends). synthesis carried corrected versions explicit “Correction review” notes + appendix marking verdicts. ship fan-’s synthesis trust: already read LabKey-touching R files firsthand build ground-truth anchor (fed every agent fact), draft re-verified codebase claims recommendations rest — getPedDirectRelatives source-agnostic seam (R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:41-43), test_getDemographics.R’s skip--CRAN/skip--network (integration never deterministically tested), mockery already test_getFocalAnimalPed.R, getLkDirectRelatives yet delegate seam. Placed doc new docs/research/ dir (prior LabKey doc existed — checked first, per “check process history” reflex), named labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md. Project facts worth re-deriving (doc): entire LabKey surface ONE labkey.selectRows() study.demographics + pure-R walk; auth implicit .netrc/API-key default (example config explicitly requires .netrc); Rlabkey unversioned Imports Rlabkey 3.x ratchets LabKey-server floor; ONPRC curates genetic-preferred parentage (overridden demographicsParents.sql) SNPRC/NIRC collapse 'Observed'. Reflexes: [technical-evaluation / architecture-research deliverable RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION pass — require every claim cite file:line / repo:path / fetched URL, build claim-source appendix, stamp time-relative claims date][research fan-, ALWAYS interpose adversarial per-claim verification stage investigate synthesize — prompt verifier REFUTE primary source default “uncertain” can’t check; fan-investigators confidently mis-source mechanism even high-level fact right][instruct synthesizer carry CORRECTED claim (original) anything refuted, flag “uncertain” inline, never invent version/date/path absent inputs][author still owns verification: build ground-truth anchor reading primary files FIRSTHAND fan-(feed agents fact), draft re-verify firsthand subset recommendation rests — don’t ship synthesized claims trust][read EHR-module repos without cloning: gh api "repos/<org>/<repo>/git/trees/HEAD?recursive=1" list, grep tree, fetch single files via gh api .../contents/<path>; note default branch (develop LabKey)][mark anything checkable live server “(unverified — requires confirmation)” collect Open Questions section — don’t launder fact][new doc category → new docs/<kind>/ dir (docs/research/), date-suffixed filename, confirming prior doc covers ]. Apply: research/evaluation/architecture deliverable, multi-agent investigation whose synthesized output acted , future work nprcgenekeepr’s LabKey/Rlabkey data-source layer (start docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md).

what: Learning 137 — A freshly-added package function makes lintr::object_usage_linter FALSELY report “no visible global function definition” — because lint resolves global symbols against the INSTALLED/loaded namespace, which doesn’t yet contain it; the fix is to re-lint after load_all() (or install), NOT # nolint on correct code. (S144, owner pick — LabKey research Rec #3: explicit optional API-key auth with .netrc fallback)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Implementing setLabKeyDefaults() (+ internal getConfigApiKey()/hasNetrc(), + call getDemographics()), fresh lintr::lint_package() reported 4 object_usage_linter warnings — four NEW cross-references (getDemographics→setLabKeyDefaults; setLabKeyDefaults→{getConfigApiKey, hasNetrc, labkey.setDefaults}); every pre-existing internal cross-reference (e.g. getSiteInfo→getParamDef) clean. asymmetry tell: object_usage_linter resolves globals package namespace built installed package, new functions weren’t installed yet, read undefined stale install’s functions resolved fine. Re-running lint_package() pkgload::load_all(".") → 0 lints; CI lint.yaml uses setup-r-dependencies extra-packages: ... local::., installs current source linting, CI clean (verified workflow firsthand). wrong move # nolint correct, already-exported code — Learning 134’s “linter message suggestion” cuts ways: sometimes message environment artifact, code problem. Two project constraints shaped design, found reading firsthand coding: () getParamDef() stop()s absent token, OPTIONAL apiKey token needed soft reader (getConfigApiKey), getParamDef; (b) test_getSiteInfo.R asserts getSiteInfo()’s EXACT field-name vector — adding apiKey field broken test rippled ~10 Shiny-module callers, credential reader stays self-contained. Testability: every external dependency setLabKeyDefaults (env var, config-file path, netrc location, baseUrl) routed INJECTED siteInfo list + withr-controlled env vars, true side-effect (Rlabkey::labkey.setDefaults) mockery::stub-bed — seven branch tests deterministic regardless host’s real .netrc/env (owner’s real netrc can’t perturb ). Final triple: suite 0/0, lint_package() 0 (loaded), R CMD check Status OK; plus runtime smoke real un-stubbed function across three branches (apiKey/netrc/-cred). Process slip caught: wrote “R CMD check Status OK” CHANGELOG running — caught , ran check (first run failed /tmp libpath couldn’t see renv library; re-ran R_LIBS="$(Rscript -e 'cat(paste(.libPaths(),collapse=\":\"))')" → genuine Status OK). Never let claim precede evidence. Reflexes: [fresh lint_package() flagging object_usage_linter “visible global function” functions just added = stale-install artifact, real lint — re-lint pkgload::load_all("."); 0, ’s clean (CI installs local::. linting clean — verify workflow)][never # nolint symbol genuinely exists package just silence object_usage; fix resolution context, code][OPTIONAL config token can’t reuse getParamDef() (stop()s absent) — write soft reader returning ""/NULL][adding field widely-used accessor like getSiteInfo(), grep callers check test asserting EXACT shape (test_getSiteInfo.R ) — prefer self-contained helper widening contract][make credential/IO function deterministically testable INJECTING environment (siteInfo arg + withr::local_envvar) stubbing side-effecting external call mockery — host-machine state can’t perturb tests][run R CMD check renv project R_LIBS="$(Rscript -e 'cat(paste(.libPaths(),collapse=\":\"))')" clean check R sees renv library — bare R CMD check /tmp reports deps “available”][never write verification result (Status OK, tests pass) file command actually produced ]. Apply: session adding new exported/internal R functions (expect object_usage artifact), new optional config token, test credential/server/IO function, future work nprcgenekeepr’s LabKey auth (start R/setLabKeyDefaults.R + docs/setup/labkey-authentication.md).

what: Learning 138 — Pinning a dependency VERSION FLOOR: derive the client-correctness minimum from the dependency’s OWN changelog matched to the EXACT call you make (every argument, not just the headline feature), and resolve a “what version does the site run” gate by mining the vendor’s open-source module repos (highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch = targeted version) — but a >= floor only enforces client-correctness + CRAN hygiene, NOT server-compat (a too-new client outrunning an old server needs an upper bound, which CRAN discourages). (S146, owner “1 then 2” — delete merged labkey-apikey-auth branch + Rlabkey version floor)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Pinning unversioned Rlabkey, DESCRIPTION. S143 research doc said API-key arg landed Rlabkey 2.1.130, package’s actual S144 call passes args — labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=) — Rlabkey’s installed NEWS shows baseUrl= support landed one version LATER, 2.1.131 (apiKey 2.1.130, baseUrl 2.1.131). true minimal client-correctness floor 2.1.131, 2.1.130 — one-version --one doc led ; reading dependency’s changelog firsthand EXACT call (arguments) caught . doc’s headline RISK opposite direction — client NEW live server (Rlabkey 3.x ratchets LabKey-SERVER minimum: 3.2.0 needs server ≥ 24.1, 3.4.1 ≥ 24.12) — >= floor defend (upper bound , CRAN discourages upper bounds; server-compat deployment matter, DESCRIPTION constraint). resolve gated “version ONPRC/SNPRC run?” (research doc Open Q §8.1, previously framed answerable live server), mined four vendor EHR-module repos via workflow: none pins server version -file (module versions build-injected via ManageVersion/centralized Gradle), authoritative signal highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch name LabKey’s YY.M scheme — four (base + ONPRC + SNPRC + NIRC) target 26.6 (corroborated newest *-26.000-26.001.sql dbscripts), adversarially verified per repo. Caveat carried handoff: maintained release branch = version module code BUILT , proof DEPLOYED production version (center can run older) — bounded maintained range (~19.x..26.6) pinned. Net: floor’s real job client-correctness + CRAN hygiene, owner’s pick conservative >= 3.2.0 (server ≥ 24.1, consistent 26.6 evidence) defensible POLICY bump 2.1.131 correctness minimum, correctness necessity. writing “3.2.0” confirmed firsthand real release header NEWS (Changes 3.2.0, “supported LabKey Server v24.1 later”) installed 3.4.6 ≥ 3.2.0 (claim never precedes evidence — Learning 137). Verified CONFIG change (owner pick “Config change, R CMD check”): devtools::check() Status OK 0/0/0 — satisfied floor bump runtime-inert, RED→GREEN→REFACTOR (behavioral logic test; build equivalent verification, guard test asserting floor’s mere presence near-tautological). Reflexes: [pin/bump dependency floor dependency’s NEWS/changelog, matched EXACT call make — check EVERY argument’s introduction version, just headline feature (baseUrl= 2.1.131 one past apiKey= 2.1.130 research doc cited)][>= floor protects -OLD client (client-correctness) + satisfies CRAN’s version--deps preference — stop -NEW client outrunning old server; ’s deployment concern, CRAN discourages <= upper bound ][resolve “server/site version running” gate mining vendor’s open-source module repos: highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch = targeted version (LabKey = YY.M); module.properties ManageVersion:true/centralized-Gradle ⇒ -file version pin, branch name signal — corroborate dbscript version ranges][ALWAYS distinguish module-TARGET (release branch) DEPLOYED production version — maintained branch proves code built , ’s running; bound maintained range mark residual unobserved][confirm specific version REAL release (changelog header) installed copy satisfies writing DESCRIPTION][DESCRIPTION version-floor change behavioral logic unit-test — verify CONFIG change via R CMD check/devtools::check() (build equivalent); satisfied floor bump runtime-inert, RED→GREEN→REFACTOR presence-asserting guard test near-tautological]. Apply: time pin bump dependency version constraint, “version server/site ” question (mine vendor repos), future Rlabkey/LabKey floor revisit nprcgenekeepr (start DESCRIPTION + docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md §3.4 / §7 Rec 1 / §8.1).

what: Learning 139 — Re-rendering a github_document Rmd (e.g. NEWS.Rmd) drops a <name>.html PREVIEW byproduct (github_document’s html_preview: true default) at the top level, which R CMD check then flags as a “Non-standard file/directory found at top level” NOTE — so after rendering NEWS, delete the stray NEWS.html (and any *_files/ dir) BEFORE checking/committing. (S147, LabKey research Rec #2)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. REFACTOR re-rendered NEWS.Rmd → NEWS.md via rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd"). Rmd’s output: github_document: default carries html_preview = TRUE, render ALSO wrote NEWS.html preview (untracked, git-ignored). first devtools::check() came back 0 errors / 0 warnings / 1 NOTE — NOTE exactly “Non-standard file/directory found top level: ‘NEWS.html’”, broken project’s standing 0/0/0 bar. rm -f NEWS.html (untracked, never part intended change set) re-run devtools::check() → 0/0/0. (S144 also re-rendered NEWS handoff never flagged — either cleaned artifact silently artifact predated check baseline; recording now next NEWS-rendering session expects .) Permanent fixes exist left candidates avoid scope creep: set html_preview: false NEWS.Rmd YAML, add NEWS.html .Rbuildignore (NOTE presence build, .Rbuildignore real fix) .gitignore. Reflexes: [rmarkdown::render() github_document Rmd, expect <name>.html preview byproduct (html_preview defaults TRUE) + possibly <name>_files/ dir — delete R CMD check/commit, surface top-level NOTE flip OK→1-NOTE][always re-run devtools::check() removing stray artifact claiming 0/0/0][permanent fixes (candidates, done S147): html_preview: false Rmd YAML, .Rbuildignore + .gitignore entries NEWS.html]. Apply: session re-renders NEWS.Rmd (github_document) runs R CMD check.

what: Learning 140 — A research/recommendation doc can be internally inconsistent — its Recommendation prose can contradict its OWN ground-truth sections and the live codebase — so before implementing a recommendation, ground it firsthand against (a) the doc’s evidence sections and (b) the actual code; here BOTH of Rec #2’s literal sub-instructions were unsafe for this repo. (S147, LabKey research Rec #2)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Implementing LabKey research doc’s Rec #2 (“move hardcoded ONPRC defaults config; reconcile example-config drift”), firsthand grounding showed literal phrasings wrong codebase: (1) doc’s alternative “reduce -config fallback clear error” BREAKING change, quick win — getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE)’s ONPRC fallback load-bearing: backs Shiny app’s default launch (appUI/appServer/modORIPReporting) values/structure pinned 5 test files (test_getSiteInfo, test_modSiteConfig, test-e2e-orip-module, test_shouldShowOripTab, test_modORIPReporting) + exported examples + silent-expectConfigFile = FALSE contract; (2) doc’s “align example’s flat dam/sire Id/parents/dam lookup form” make SNPRC example WRONG — doc’s §4.3 establishes flat dam/sire CORRECT form SNPRC (direct columns) lookup form ONPRC-specific. surfaced contradictions owner via pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion; owner chose safe readings — “Centralize, behavior change” (extract fallback internal defaultSiteParams() single source truth; return byte-identical) “Document center-specific form” (comment example reader sees SNPRC ≠ ONPRC, rather unify ). Net deliverable matched recommendation’s INTENT (one source truth + clear example) rejecting literal STEPS. blast-radius inventory (grep every getSiteInfo caller + every test asserting shape) load-bearing step decided refactor-vs-breaking — evidence-based-inventory discipline deletion/migration plan requires. Reflexes: [implementing recommendation research/plan doc, ground firsthand doc’s evidence sections live code — Recommendation can contradict doc’s ground-truth (Rec #2 vs §4.3) codebase (“quick win” ’s actually breaking)][change widely-used accessor’s fallback/shape, grep callers + every test asserting structure FIRST (5 test files + app default-launch path + examples) — inventory decides refactor vs breaking change][recommendation’s literal steps unsafe intent sound, surface contradiction via pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion safe interpretations options, implement intent, wrong literal step][centralize-without-behavior-change = extract one internal source + fallback-equals-source agreement test + characterization guard pinning documented invariant; verify consumer’s output byte-identical pre-change literals]. Apply: session acting recommendation docs/research/ plan doc (next : Rec #4, data-source adapter), change shared accessor’s default/fallback.

what: Learning 141 — Two functions that look like the “same walk” can diverge in result, so a recommendation to “make A delegate its walk to B” is a BEHAVIOR change, not a refactor — read BOTH algorithms and diff their results on a worked example before unifying them. (S148, LabKey research Rec #4 — data-source adapter)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Rec #4 LabKey research doc said formalize data-source adapter “make getLkDirectRelatives() delegate walk source-agnostic getPedDirectRelatives().” Reading walks firsthand showed equivalent: getLkDirectRelatives() re-seeds parents/offspring PREVIOUS generation (parents <- getParents(ped, parents)) — strict ancestors-+ descendants-lineage; getPedDirectRelatives() re-seeds FULL accumulated id set iteration (parents <- getParents(ped, ids), ids growing) — full connected-component closure, pulls collaterals (siblings, cousins) parent added, next iteration collects parent’s offspring. “delegate walk” ENLARGE live LabKey result set: behavior change, refactor. doc even hedged (“functionally analogous, byte-identical; differ seeding accumulation”) — code confirmed materially. scoped session FETCH boundary (extract pull+normalize internal getPedigreeSource(); getLkDirectRelatives() keeps exact walk), surfaced walk-unification separate behavior-changing decision via pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion (owner chose true-refactor slice + internal @noRd adapter via second approach AskUserQuestion), deterministic RED test asserts strict-lineage id set {O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1} EXCLUDING collateral sibling O2 — guard fails anyone later swaps full-component walk. Reflexes: [unifying/delegating two similarly-named functions, read algorithms diff RESULTS worked example — superficial structural similarity (“walk pedigree”) hides different result set][recommendation says “make delegate B” behavior-change proposal PROVEN B produce identical output — treat like breaking change, cleanup; Learning 140’s “ground recommendation firsthand” applied refactor][true-refactor slice “formalize adapter” FETCH boundary (swap data OBTAINED) consuming algorithm byte-identical; lock existing behavior characterization test (: id set incl. deliberately-EXCLUDED collateral) touching ][internal @noRd adapter declares @import/@importFrom tags keeps NAMESPACE stable even remove tags function extracted — verify git diff NAMESPACE roxygenise][parameter named source trips undesirable_function_linter (bare symbol resolves base source()); rename (e.g. sourceType) rather scatter # nolint]. Apply: “make X use/delegate-Y” “consolidate two” task; data-source/adapter extraction; refactor claiming “behavior change” two non-identical code paths.

what: Learning 142 — When you EXECUTE a behavior change a prior session deliberately deferred and GUARDED, the guard/characterization test IS the RED spec — flip its assertion to the new behavior; and before choosing between a “delegate” and an “in-place” implementation, PROVE they are result-equivalent (or characterize where they differ) instead of assuming. (S149, LabKey research Rec #4 — walk-unification)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S148 deferred unifying getLkDirectRelatives()’s strict ancestor/descendant walk getPedDirectRelatives()’s full-connected-component walk (behavior change — live LabKey result set grows include collaterals; Learning 141) left characterization test pinning OLD behavior (focal O1 → {O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}, asserting collateral sibling O2 EXCLUDED). Executing change session, RED step simply FLIP guard: assert full component {S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1} INCLUDING O2 (plus equivalence getPedDirectRelatives(ids="O1", ped=fixture)$id). deferred--guarded behavior change made RED test — guard’s inverted assertion spec, failed current code exactly predicted (Absent: O2). approach: research doc’s “delegate getPedDirectRelatives” “widen walk -place” rewrite LOOK different, proved result-IDENTICAL cases asking owner — full-component walk trailing addIdRecords(unrelated, …) guaranteed -op, walk’s fixpoint exactly getParents(ids) ⊆ ids (loop runs setdiff(union(getParents(ids), getOffspring(ids)), ids) empty), every non-NA sire/dam reference already set unrelated always empty. equivalence proven, “delegate” clearly-cleaner choice (one walk implementation, matches research doc) owner picked via pre-RED approach AskUserQuestion. Blast-radius check first (Learning 140 reflex): sole production consumer getFocalAnimalPed() (larger pedigree complete kinship/GVA, breaking; renames 7 positional columns — preserved getPedDirectRelatives’s ped[ped$id %% ids, ] return), every reference mocks getLkDirectRelatives wholesale (walk-agnostic), test moved. Verified end--end: suite 0/0 (1960 passed), lint 0, devtools::check() 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke REAL getLkDirectRelatives (full component incl. O2, fail-soft NULL path intact, body delegates). NEWS render trap recurred: -- Rmd smart-rendered en-dash NEWS.md (Learning 132) — reworded source drop -- (rest NEWS avoids ) re-verified NEWS.md pure ASCII; deleted NEWS.html byproduct (Learning 139). Reflexes: [execute behavior change prior session deferred--guarded characterization test, RED step FLIP guard’s assertion new behavior — deferred change carries spec; don’t write brand-new test scratch leave stale guard fail confusingly][choosing “delegate existing function” “reimplement -place” behavior change, PROVE two produce identical results (characterize difference) — proof full-component walk’s fixpoint (getParents(ids) ⊆ ids) makes trailing addIdRecords(unrelated,…) guaranteed -op, delegate ≡ -place cases; equivalence proven, pick cleaner one confirm via pre-RED approach AskUserQuestion][run blast-radius inventory behavior change (grep callers + tests MOCK vs EXERCISE function) — real consumer tolerates larger output every reference mocks function, change contained one characterization test][NEWS.Rmd -- smart-renders non-ASCII en-dash NEWS.md — reword source avoid -- (parentheses/commas) re-verify NEWS.md pure ASCII (LC_ALL=C grep -P '[\x80-\xFF]'), delete NEWS.html byproduct]. Apply: session executes behavior change prior session deferred (flip guard test); “delegate vs reimplement” choice (prove equivalence first); NEWS.Rmd re-render (mind --→en-dash + NEWS.html traps).

what: Learning 143 — When an owner offers a scope fork (“do A or B”), ground BOTH directions before recommending (a read-only parallel sweep is the right tool — keep file-mutating work solo), and weight OFFLINE TESTABILITY heavily; and when you EXTEND a pluggable seam, derive the new provider’s contract by mirroring its sibling branches, not by inventing one. (S150, LabKey research Rec #4/#5 — getPedigreeSource() "file" provider)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. owner’s directive named item 3 “add file/-EHR provider getPedigreeSource() seam; server-side filtering / executeSql.” Rather pick, treated “” scope decision ground: read-4-agent workflow (getPedigreeSource seam + callers; research doc’s Rec #4/#5; package’s existing file-ingestion subsystem; LabKey fetch path + offline testability) plus firsthand reads. evidence came back lopsided, asymmetry drove fast, confident scope AskUserQuestion: Direction (file provider) doc’s prioritized Rec #4 (walk-delegation half S149), reuses exported getPedigree(), additive/low-risk, fully offline-deterministic; Direction B (server-side filtering/executeSql) explicitly deferred doc (benefit unmeasured; executeSql needs per-center dot-notation SQL), tested observed without live LabKey server (absent ), carries landmine — naive focal-id server filter incompatible client-side connected-component walk (filtering focal rows leaves walk nothing traverse; ’s re-architecture, drop-optimization). Owner picked . implementation contract, invent "file" behave — read siblings: "labkey" branch fails soft (NULL) flaky network fetch; "dataframe" branch errors loudly bad input; neither runs qcStudbook (return un-curated, column-shaped peds — downstream runQcStudbook() curates). file -disk twin "dataframe" source, "file" mirrors : delegate getPedigree(fileName, sep), validate id/sire/dam, return un-curated ped, error loudly NULL/missing-file/missing-columns. Strict TDD held clean first pass: 5 RED tests (CSV round-trip + 3 constrained-message error paths + mockery delegation/sep-threading check) failed genuinely (match.arg/unused-argument + message mismatch — false-passes; S148 RED-discipline lesson), GREEN added one branch + two defaulted params (backward-compatible), REFACTOR extracted duplicated id/sire/dam check local helper preserving exact messages. Proactively whitelisted words rendered docs introduced (pluggable, plus S149’s never-listed collaterals) inst/WORDLIST devtools::check(), build came back 0/0/0 single pass (spelling-NOTE iteration). Verified: suite 0/0 (1979 passed), lint 0, check 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke real un-mocked "file" branch. provider new internal capability seam, yet wired production caller — honest tracer-bullet (end--end working + tested, getLkDirectRelatives() still hardcodes "labkey"). Reflexes: [owner offers “B”, treat scope decision GROUND, coin-flip — sweep directions bring recommendation backed doc priority + reuse surface + offline testability + blast radius; read-parallel workflow grounds keeping file-mutating TDD solo (right ultracode hybrid)][weight OFFLINE TESTABILITY heavily: direction “tested observed environment” (needs live server/credential) weak near-term deliverable vs one fully deterministic offline][extending pluggable seam/adapter/strategy, READ SIBLING branches mirror contract — return shape, error-vs-fail-soft, curated-vs-raw — consistency across providers spec, invention; "file" mirrors "dataframe" (loud errors, un-curated return), "labkey" (fail-soft NULL)][naive server-side focal-id filter incompatible downstream client-side connected-component walk — filtering focal rows leaves nothing traverse; “push filter ” re-architecture, drop-][adding defaulted params (fileName = NULL, sep = ",") internal adapter backward-compatible — existing callers unaffected][rendered NEWS/Rd introduces domain word, add inst/WORDLIST pass devtools::check() 0/0/0 first run — spelling-NOTE iteration]. Apply: owner “B” scope fork; new provider existing adapter/strategy seam; “optimize fetch/push server-side” proposal interacts downstream client-side traversal; NEWS/Rd change introduces new vocabulary.

what: Learning 144 — To WIRE a new provider/capability to a caller, prefer adding a clean SYMMETRIC SIBLING over parameterizing a domain-named function: a new wrapper costs one export but has zero blast radius on existing signatures and avoids a naming smell (a LabKey-named function that also reads files); the “but a user can already compose it” critique is answered by PARITY — the sibling gives the new source the same first-class entry point the old source already has (the existing function is itself just a thin fetch→delegate wrapper). And a wrapper inherits its source’s contract, so do NOT copy a guard that can’t fire (no NULL guard on a loud-erroring source = no untested dead code). (S151, owner “publish + delete + wire the file provider”)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S150 added "file" provider internal getPedigreeSource() seam left capability-— getLkDirectRelatives() hardcodes getPedigreeSource("labkey"), file source unreachable production. Item 3 wire caller. read-4-agent grounding sweep + firsthand reads surfaced four wiring shapes different blast radius/value: () new sibling getFileDirectRelatives(); (B) parameterize getLkDirectRelatives(sourceType=…); (C) wire focal-animal app pipeline (getFocalAnimalPed/modInput.R); (D) reconsider (doc framed file source test/offline provider; offline focal-subset already composable via getPedDirectRelatives(ids, getPedigree(file)); app already reads pedigree files directly). surfaced four via pre-RED scope AskUserQuestion evidence-backed recommendation; owner chose . decisive arguments B: B makes LabKey-named function (getLk…) read files — genuine naming smell, cosmetic — add params existing signature; costs one new export yields clean symmetric family — getPedDirectRelatives (-memory engine) / getLkDirectRelatives (LabKey wrapper) / getFileDirectRelatives (file wrapper). “composition already works, sibling duplicative” objection real answered PARITY: getLkDirectRelatives just getPedigreeSource("labkey") → getPedDirectRelatives, .e. thin wrapper whose value named, tested, discoverable entry point — getFileDirectRelatives gives file source first-class status, routes internal validated seam (id/sire/dam) bare composition skips. Contract: wrapper inherits source’s behavior, getFileDirectRelatives NULL guard (unlike getLkDirectRelatives, whose guard exists LabKey source fails soft NULL flaky fetch) — file source errors loudly, NULL guard untested dead code; documented loud-error contract instead. Strict TDD held clean first pass: 7 RED tests (CSV full-component read incl. collateral O2; equivalence getPedDirectRelatives file; mockery delegation check pinning getPedigreeSource(sourceType="file", fileName, sep) getPedDirectRelatives(ids, ped, unrelatedParents) unrelatedParents threaded; constrained-message errors NULL/missing fileName, missing file, missing id/sire/dam; sep=";" round-trip) failed genuinely — error-contract regexps (fileName/found/column) matched “find function” (false-pass; S148 lesson). GREEN 5-line exported wrapper (NULL guard); roxygenise added export(getFileDirectRelatives) + man page (EXPORT, unlike S148-S150’s @noRd work, NAMESPACE/man change — expected). REFACTOR needed structural code change (wrapper already minimal). Verified: new file 7/7, suite 0/0, lint 0, devtools::check() 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke real un-mocked function. Honest scope note: capability/plumbing parity (first-class file entry point), higher-value app-pipeline wiring (C, deferred) let Shiny focal-animal path run offline. Reflexes: [wire new provider/source/capability caller, prefer clean SYMMETRIC SIBLING function adding type= param domain-named function — sibling zero blast radius existing signatures avoids smell getLk…/getOracle…/etc. function something -name; cost one new export][answer “user can already compose , wrapper duplicative” objection PARITY, novelty: EXISTING wrapper old source thin fetch→delegate, sibling new source legitimate pattern — value named, tested, discoverable, validated entry point, routes internal seam bare composition skips][wrapper INHERITS source’s contract — copy guard fire (NULL guard loud-erroring source; LabKey NULL guard exists source fails soft) — unreachable guard untested dead code; document differing contract roxygen instead][several wiring shapes exist different blast radius/value (new sibling vs parameterize vs full-pipeline vs reconsider), enumerate pre-RED SCOPE AskUserQuestion evidence-backed recommendation — don’t assume owner’s example function names implementation site][EXPORTED new function changes NAMESPACE + adds man page (expect roxygenise diff), unlike @noRd internal — verify diff confined export line + new .Rd][runnable @examples writes tempfile, calls function, unlink()s keeps example real (exercised R CMD check) without depending fixture file]. Apply: task wires new provider/source existing adapter/strategy seam caller; “add type/source switch vs add parallel function” decision; thin wrapper fetch→delegate pattern; new EXPORTED R function (expect NAMESPACE/man diff + object_usage stale-install artifact, Learning 137).

what: Learning 145 — When wiring a capability THROUGH an app pipeline (not just adding a sibling fn), the wiring SHAPE is decided by concrete data-shape and error-contract mismatches found in grounding, not by abstract preference: a source-shaped transform can’t be shared by a differently-shaped source, and the new fn’s fail contract should match the CALLER’s modality (so it reuses the most existing caller code). (S152, Option C — file pedigree source through the focal-animal app pipeline)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S151 left getFileDirectRelatives() first-class file capability unwired app; Option C wire file pedigree source focal-animal pipeline Shiny app’s focal path runs offline. read-4-agent grounding sweep + firsthand reads surfaced two CONCRETE constraints decided design — beyond Learning 144’s naming-smell argument sibling parameterizing. (1) Data-shape incompatibility: getFocalAnimalPed.R:76 POSITIONAL 7-column rename (c("id","sex","birth","death","departure","dam","sire")) LabKey-shaped; file pedigree read getPedigree() carries named columns file’s order (e.g. id,sire,dam,sex,gen,birth,exit,...), sharing rename CORRUPT file pedigree — concrete reason separate getFocalAnimalPedFromFile() correct, just stylistic. (2) Error-contract direction: getFileDirectRelatives() errors loudly, app needs fail-soft. Rather mirror sibling getFocalAnimalPed() (returns nprcgenekeeprErr DB modality), made getFocalAnimalPedFromFile() return NULL bad pedigree file — matching app’s FILE modality (readDataFile() returns NULL → existing .null(rawData) “File Read Error” handler), dispatch reused existing caller code added ZERO new error branches. deliverable genuine VERTICAL slice (FM #25): new fn + modInput server dispatch + optional UI fileInput, end--end. testable core held strict TDD — 7 function unit tests (offline fixtures) + 2 shiny::testServer dispatch tests, offline test mocking getLkDirectRelatives stop() PROVE file path never touches EHR; one-line UI input Phase-3E smoke-verified. RED genuine (function: “find function”; modInput: EHR stop() fired dispatch didn’t exist yet — 163 existing modInput tests stayed green regression guard). REFACTOR safely touched WORKING sibling: extracted duplicated focal-id read shared internal readFocalAnimalIds() used focal functions (behavior-neutral, test files + 168-test modInput suite re-verified green); moving @importFrom readxl excel_format/utils read.csv tags extracted helper kept NAMESPACE stable (verified imports survived). Verified: new file 7/7, getFocalAnimalPed 62, modInput 168, full suite 0/0, lint 0, devtools::check() 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke real un-mocked function + rendered UI input. Reflexes: [wiring capability pipeline, let GROUNDING (abstract design taste) pick shape — look specifically source-shaped transform (positional rename, date format, column map) new source break: concrete incompatibility, just naming smell, decisive argument separate sibling parameterizing][choose new function’s fail contract match CALLER’S modality, reflexively sibling’s — NULL (file modality → reuse app’s existing “File Read Error” path) beat nprcgenekeeprErr (DB modality), adding zero new caller code; ask “contract lets caller reuse existing handling?”][make function+server+UI change real VERTICAL slice (FM #25): unit-test function, testServer-test dispatch, smoke-test one-line UI — PROVE “longer needs X” claim mocking X stop() X call loud failure][behavior-neutral REFACTOR may touch WORKING sibling (extract shared helper) test-covered, extracting helper RELOCATES calls absorbs, implementation-coupled test mockery::stub(= sibling, = movedFn)s calls SILENTLY BREAKS — stub longer intercepts (patches function’s body), real fn runs, surfaces ERROR FAIL; re-point tests new owner (stub(= helper, ...), test helper directly) re-verify ERROR column, just FAIL/SKIP (testthat counts thrown error separately — re-verify prints PASS/FAIL/SKIP miss ; devtools::check() won’t)][moving @importFrom tags extracted helper, confirm roxygenise keeps NAMESPACE imports intact (symbols just need ONE declarer package)][R-package test files gated top-level skip_on_cran() need NOT_CRAN=true actually run via Rscript/test_file — “0 failed, skipped” result gate, pass]. Apply: task wiring capability app/UI pipeline; “new sibling vs parameterize” decision sources differ column shape error contract; change spanning function + server + UI; behavior-neutral helper extraction across two functions; skip_on_cran()-gated test run.

what: Learning 146 — After gh pr merge, the git fetch that precedes git reset --hard origin/<branch> MUST be verified to have SUCCEEDED before resetting — a transient network/DNS failure leaves the local origin/<branch> ref STALE, and reset --hard origin/<branch> then resets to the OLD pre-merge commit without any error, silently leaving the local branch BEHIND the real remote. Gate the reset on a post-fetch assertion that the just-merged commit is now an ancestor of origin/<branch>. (S153, publish S152 — focal-file source merge)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Publishing S152: pushed wire-focal-file-source, opened PR #63, watched CI 10/10, confirmed CLEAN/MERGEABLE, gh pr merge 63 --merge → merge commit e1780c02 (verified state: MERGED firsthand). standard reconcile (Learning 135: fetch+reset, pull). ran post-merge git fetch git reset --hard origin/master command block — fetch hit transient resolve host: github.com. fetch failed, local origin/master stayed STALE pre-merge cb46616e, git reset --hard origin/master happily reset local master OLD commit, exit 0, error. reason caught : block also asserted “merged commit 4f362be9 ancestor origin/master?” printed — impossible right confirmed merge, ref stale. Recovered: retried git fetch loop (succeeded attempt 1) → origin/master advanced cb46616e..e1780c02; re-asserted 4f362be9 now ancestor (YES); git reset --hard origin/master → local master = e1780c02; confirmed R/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R + R/readFocalAnimalIds.R present. trap: git reset --hard origin/<branch> operates whatever LOCAL remote-tracking ref currently — failed/skipped fetch makes -op--stale, error, “exit 0” evidence reconcile worked. Reflexes: [never chain git fetch git reset --hard origin/<branch> fetch can’t fail — verify fetch SUCCEEDED (exit status / old..new ref update line) resetting][gh pr merge, assert merged commit ancestor origin/<branch> (git merge-base ---ancestor <mergedSHA> origin/<branch>) reset — post-merge means stale ref, missing merge][retry transient git fetch (DNS / resolve host) short loop rather treating first failure terminal][treat “exit 0” git reset --hard origin/<branch> necessary--sufficient — confirm git rev-parse origin/<branch> actually advanced expected SHA]. Apply: every post-merge local reconcile; reset --hard origin/<branch> depends just-run fetch; sandboxed/flaky-network sessions git fetch can fail transiently.

what: Learning 147 — To enrich a fail-soft boundary that swallows WHY into a bare NULL, return a DEDICATED classed error carrying the reason (do NOT overload the shared error/QC object with a different concern); wrap EVERY read the boundary performs (a read left outside the tryCatch is a latent UNCAUGHT throw, not fail-soft); MAP low-level stop() text to clean user-facing messages rather than leaking an internal fnName(): ... prefix; and treat a “silent empty” result (0-row / opaque NULL) as its own reported failure. (S155, richer offline-focal error messages)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S152 made getFocalAnimalPedFromFile() fail-soft returning NULL → app’s generic “File Read Error” / “read uploaded file.” S155’s deliverable surface . read-4-agent grounding sweep + firsthand reads found three things shaped work, none deliverable’s one-line description (“missing file vs missing columns”) named: (1) focal-id read getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R:50 OUTSIDE tryCatch, bad focal-id file threw UNCAUGHT inside observeEvent — latent app crash, strictly worse generic message, “richer errors” also CATCH ; (2) specific reasons already existed getPedigreeSource() stop() strings (e.g. “…must contain columns id, sire, dam”) discarded catch (logged flog.debug); (3) focal-IDs-absent case returned SILENT 0-row data.frame. app already renders per-error Details column (LabKey path shows failedDatabaseConnection ), richer “” natural home. Owner chose (pre-RED AskUserQuestion) DEDICATED nprcgenekeeprFileErr class () adding sibling field shared nprcgenekeeprErr/getEmptyErrorLst studbook-QC object — symmetric failedDatabaseConnection category stretch widens blast radius across checkErrorLst/summary/processQcStudbookResult — (C) making function throw catching modInput (smallest code, leaks raw “getPedigreeSource(): …” prefix flips documented fail-soft contract). Implementation wrapped reads, returned classed object pedigreeReadReason() mapper turning internal stop() text clean user-facing messages, converted silent 0-row walk reported “None focal IDs found” error; modInput got one inherits() branch mirroring existing one. RED genuine (function returned NULL / threw / 0-row; modInput showed generic detail — “find function” false-pass) regression guard (4 unchanged function tests + 167 modInput tests) green. Two self-caught process slips: () first ran lintr::lint_package(linters = default_linters), BYPASSES project .lintr (camelCase + line_length(80) + bans structure/source) flooded camelCase/line-length false positives — masking real count tiny; running BARE lint_package() showed exactly 4 real lints, mine; (ii) new constructor’s structure(list(...), class = ...) tripped project’s undesirable_function_linter → switched package’s class(x) <- ... idiom. Verified: focal 27, modInput 173, full suite 0/0 via check, lint 0, devtools::check() 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke 7 real paths + testServer Details check. Reflexes: [enriching fail-soft boundary = return DEDICATED classed error object carrying reason; overload shared/QC error type different concern — sibling-field symmetry tempting stretches category widens blast radius across type’s readers][audit boundary reads/calls left OUTSIDE tryCatch — “uncaught throw” path masquerading fail-soft latent crash; fix part “richer errors,” just add message already-caught path][map low-level stop() text clean user-facing messages small reason-mapper; never surface internal fnName(): ... prefix user][“silent empty” result (0-row, opaque NULL) failure REPORT, success — convert named error][repo run BARE lint_package() .lintr applies — linters = default_linters bypasses floods false positives; use class(x) <- ... structure(...)][behavior-neutral REFACTOR can decline DRY working sibling branches touching adds blast radius cosmetics (Learning 145)]. Apply: “improve/enrich error messages” “surface X failed” task; fail-soft boundary collapses distinct failures one opaque value; lint run repo.

what: Learning 148 — When publishing a PR, codecov/project can red on a fractional total-coverage dip even when codecov/patch is 100% — diagnose before deciding: confirm it is NON-BLOCKING (master unprotected) + ADVISORY (the coverage-generating workflow passed; patch is the meaningful signal), surface the real-but-non-blocking merge call via AskUserQuestion, and log any config fix as its OWN issue, not a scope-crept edit. (S156, publish S155 — PR #64)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Publishing S155 (PR #64), 8 real CI jobs (lint + R CMD check ×5 incl. ubuntu-devel + pkgdown + test-coverage) passed codecov/patch passed 100.00% diff hit — codecov/project FAILED: “89.66% (−0.18%) vs 791c51e”. instinct “merge, ’s just codecov” opposite instinct “refuse, check red” wrong; disciplined move diagnose red, surface decision. Diagnosis: repo two codecov configs root — codecov.yml (coverage.status.project.default.threshold: 1%) .codecov.yml (“Team/Repository Yaml” round/range/precision, status block). intended 1% threshold (target: auto, base 89.84% → floor 88.84%) −0.18% dip (head 89.66%) PASS; failed → 1% applied (codecov default 0% effect, dip fails), consistent two files conflicting. master unprotected (gh api .../branches/master/protection → 404) codecov/project non-blocking (mergeStateStatus: UNSTABLE, mergeable: MERGEABLE — gh pr merge --merge goes ). class S41’s [triage--root-cause] named (“codecov status checks external advisory; test-coverage workflow GENERATES coverage passed”), now precise config mechanism. Surfaced via AskUserQuestion grounded recommendation (merge — patch 100%, dip within intended tolerance, config artifact); owner chose merge. Mid-session owner asked whether degradation backlog item — answered reading BACKLOG/issues/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/ROADMAP firsthand (tracked; S41 reflex + ROADMAP >80% aspiration), logged config fix issue #65 rather fixing inline (FM #8 — config change separate, verify--next-PR deliverable). One honesty-calibration slip: first stated “red config artifact” certainty (strong) evidence warranted confirming codecov’s file-precedence rule — frame diagnosis “evidence strongly indicates” first mention. Reflexes: [red codecov/project green codecov/patch real CI jobs green almost always advisory, correctness failure — diagnose root cause merging refusing][treating failing check blocking, check branch protection (gh api .../branches/<b>/protection → 404 = unprotected = non-required); UNSTABLE+MERGEABLE PR merges][fractional total-coverage dip failing despite configured threshold evidence threshold applied — look duplicate/misplaced config files; target: auto floor = base − threshold][surface real--non-blocking merge decision via AskUserQuestion ([author-decision]); merge silently red check refuse owner’s directive][log config/infra fix issue (#65) — scope-creep publish (FM #8)][asked whether recurring CI annoyance tracked, read BACKLOG/issues/learnings/ROADMAP firsthand answering — answer memory][frame confident--unconfirmed diagnosis “evidence strongly indicates”, fact, first mention]. Apply: publish/merge session codecov (advisory) check reds; “X tracked?” question; time state root-cause diagnosis fully confirming mechanism.

what: Learning 149 – After ANY renv bump / self-upgrade, run a fresh-R-startup smoke test (Phase 3E): an renv self-upgrade can leave renv/activate.R with an UNSUBSTITUTED ..md5.. template placeholder, which throws object '..md5..' not found the moment .Rprofile sources it – breaking every R session started in the project. The lockfile looks correct and a check that does not cold-start R in the project root will not catch it. Fix by regenerating with the installed renv (renv::activate()), which fills in the md5; then re-source to confirm. (S157, commit the renv 1.1.4->1.2.3 bump)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Orientation found renv.lock + renv/activate.R modified – renv self-upgrade (1.1.4 -> 1.2.3) owner ran sessions. task “just commit .” committed two files, – regenerated activate.R runs every R startup (runtime behavior) – ran Phase 3E smoke test: Rscript -e '...' project root. FAILED: object '..md5..' found, halted, source("renv/activate.R") (called .Rprofile). Diagnosis: line 6 new activate.R attr(version, "md5") <- ..md5.. – literal ..md5.. template token, never substituted (sibling version <- "1.2.3" substituted; md5 placeholder leaked). Confirmed REGRESSION, pre-existing: old 1.1.4 activate.R sourced cleanly --vanilla (warning installed!=recorded renv, mismatch bump meant fix); new one threw ..md5... renv 1.2.3 installed system library, regenerated via Rscript --vanilla -e 'renv::activate(project=getwd())' (.Rprofile, broken file auto-sourced; restore/snapshot side effects – activate.R changed, renv.lock untouched). Line 6 became attr(version, "md5") <- "1bd9f58e..." fresh .Rprofile startup printed “renv: 1.2.3 – startup OK”. broken activate.R lived local-unpushed commit, --amended away (renv.lock half already correct; activate.R needed md5). Net: broken artifact never entered shared history. Reflexes: [renv lockfile bump RUNTIME change (activate.R runs every R startup via .Rprofile) -> Phase 3E applies: cold-start R project root watch startup errors, treat “lockfile committed” done][leftover ..xxx.. template token generated file = generator substitute ; grep -nE '\.\.[-zA-Z0-9_]+\.\.' file find unsubstituted placeholders][regenerate renv infra INSTALLED renv via renv::activate(project=getwd()) --vanilla (skips broken .Rprofile; restore/snapshot); rewrites activate.R, renv.lock][just-committed, still-local artifact found broken, --amend fix broken bytes never reach remote – ship--fix][isolate regression-vs-pre-existing sourcing OLD file (git show <ref>:path) way blaming change]. Apply: session touches renv.lock / renv/activate.R (bump, self-upgrade, snapshot); “just commit generated file” task – verify actually works calling done.

what: Learning 150 – When a duplicate-config-file precedence bug is the diagnosis, the fix is exactly ONE file (eliminate the precedence question entirely) – and you can verify the fix AT THE CONFIG LAYER this session rather than deferring to “the next PR”: codecov exposes https://codecov.io/validate, which echoes the PARSED config (so you see the actual thresholds it will apply); POST a SECRET-REDACTED copy so no committed token leaves the machine. Do the complete job (remove the now-dead .Rbuildignore entry for the deleted file), and PRESERVE – do not silently strip – an embedded credential you happen to be rewriting (rotation is a separate owner decision; FM #8). (S158, fix issue #65 – consolidate the two codecov configs)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Issue #65 (logged S156 PR #64 publish): repo two root codecov configs – codecov.yml (real one: comment: false, embedded token:, coverage.status.project/patch.default.threshold: 1% block) .codecov.yml (junk template artifact – comments # Team Yaml / # Repository Yaml / # Used Codecov updating copied verbatim codecov’s documentation example explaining YAML layering, THREE duplicate coverage: keys [last-wins -> round: , range: 20..100, precision: 2] status block). present status-less .codecov.yml won precedence, codecov never applied 1% threshold (default 0% effect -> PR #64’s -0.18% dip failed codecov/project despite 100%-covered patch). Evidence-based inventory deleting (SAFEGUARDS): git log -- .codecov.yml proved committed (58a9db26); repo-wide grep -codecov found two functional references – .Rbuildignore (^codecov\.yml$ + ^\.codecov\.yml$); README hit badge (app.codecov.io, config ref); CI workflow names config path (test-coverage.yaml uploads cobertura.xml via codecov/codecov-action@v4, authenticating secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN – YAML config read SERVER-SIDE codecov.io compute status checks, YAML’s embedded token: redundant action’s secret). Fix: rewrote codecov.yml single source (folded .codecov.yml’s display settings, kept 1% status block, added -one-file header comment), git rm .codecov.yml, removed dead ^\.codecov\.yml$ .Rbuildignore. Verified session, deferred: local yaml.safe_load confirmed valid + duplicate keys (root cause); curl --data-binary @<token-redacted-copy> https://codecov.io/validate returned Valid! echoed threshold: 1.0 project patch – conclusive codecov now reads applies 1%. Preserved embedded token verbatim flagged (redundant GH secret, low-sensitivity public repo, committed credential -> rotation owner’s separate call, #65 scope-creep). Direct master (CI/build config + bookkeeping, .Rbuildignored package, break R CMD check; S156/S157 hygiene--master pattern).
