Reflexes: [diagnosis “duplicate config files -> wrong precedence”, fix ONE file – “fix loser”; one file makes precedence question moot regardless host’s documented precedence order][verify config fix CONFIG LAYER instead deferring “next PR” tool offers validator – codecov’s https://codecov.io/validate echoes parsed thresholds; many CI services equivalent (gitlab-ci/lint, circleci config validate, actionlint)][REDACT secrets POSTing config external validator – sed 's#token:.*#token: REDACTED#'; schema check need real secret][config-file change .Rbuildignored / package code break R CMD check, feature-branch+PR adds CI-gating value config-PR ~coverage delta demonstrate dip-tolerance fix -> direct--master (S156/S157 hygiene pattern) correct][complete job: deleting file means removing now-dead .Rbuildignore / .gitignore / manifest entries (anti-FM #13)][rewriting file contains embedded credential, PRESERVE verbatim FLAG – removing/rotating committed secret separate, hard--reverse, owner’s-call decision; fold unrelated config fix (FM #8)][.codecov.yml full # Team/Repository Yaml comments duplicate coverage: keys codecov’s DOC EXAMPLE pasted mistake, intentional config]. Apply: “consolidate/duplicate config” CI-config fix; task touching codecov / CI YAML; time configured threshold provably applied; file rewrite happens contain secret.

what: Learning 151 – To silence a benign warning that a fail-soft boundary’s underlying read emits BEFORE the error that gets caught, add a TARGETED warning muffler at the READ SITE (withCallingHandlers + invokeRestart("muffleWarning") matched to the specific message) – do NOT broaden the shared muffler (widens blast radius across all its callers) and do NOT pre-check file.exists() (that changes a SHARED helper’s THROWN error and misses the exists-but-unreadable case). The muffle is control-flow-neutral: the error still propagates, so the caught classed result is unchanged – assert BOTH “no warning” AND “error still thrown”. (S159, quiet the offline focal-id read’s read.csv “cannot open file” warning)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S155 left benign residual: missing/unreadable focal-id file made getFocalAnimalPedFromFile() return correct classed nprcgenekeeprFileErr, read.csv() (inside readFocalAnimalIds()) signals open file '...' WARNING error – existing muffleIncompleteFinalLine() wrapper muffles “incomplete final line” warning, “open file” warning deferred top level printed console even though error already caught reported. Grounding pinned leak FOCAL-ID read : pedigree read already guarded getPedigreeSource()’s file.exists() pre-check (-found pedigree never reaches read.table, never warns). Two fix shapes table: () file.exists() pre-check + clean stop() mirroring getPedigreeSource – rejected readFocalAnimalIds() SHARED online getFocalAnimalPed(), change helper’s thrown error misses exists--unreadable (permission) case; (b) targeted warning muffler read site – chosen (owner-approved). Added small @noRd sibling muffleCannotOpenFile() (withCallingHandlers invokeRestart("muffleWarning")s conditionMessage(w) matches “open file”) nested around existing muffleIncompleteFinalLine(read.csv(...)). muffler handles warning, error still propagates caller’s tryCatch – classed result byte-identical; console noise gone. shared muffleIncompleteFinalLine 4 call sites stayed untouched (Learning 145 – broaden working shared code one-site need). RED asserted facets: boundary test (expect_no_warning + still-classed-error) helper test (still throws, via explicit withCallingHandlers/tryCatch recorder, + asserts warning) – failed right reason (warning leaked; function resolved, 0 errored, Learning 145), passed GREEN, pre-existing test’s leaked-warning count dropped 1 -> 0 (independent confirmation). Incidental: standard document() step re-synced stale man/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.Rd (S155-era @return reword never re-documented; pure text reflow, semantic change) – included + flagged, scope creep. Reflexes: [fail-soft boundary already returns classed error can STILL leak console warning – underlying read.*/file() signals warning error; muffle warning, error still flows][muffle READ SITE message-matched withCallingHandlers/invokeRestart("muffleWarning"), broadening shared muffler (blast radius across callers) file.exists()-pre-checking SHARED helper (changes thrown error; misses exists--unreadable)][warning muffle control-flow-neutral – PROVE : assert “warning” “error/return unchanged”, later refactor accidentally swallows error fails test][two siblings share internal helper, fix one site without touching helper unless need (Learning 145)][stale generated artifact surfaced standard document() step sync, scope creep – include flag pre-existing drift]. Apply: “quiet benign warning” / “silence console noise” task fail-soft read path; place caught error preceded leaked read.csv/read.table/file() warning.

what: Learning 152 – A config/threshold fix verified only AT THE CONFIG LAYER (e.g. codecov’s /validate echoing the parsed threshold, S158/Learning 150) is verified but not yet CONFIRMED: the live signal is the first real PR whose coverage delta actually exercises the check. When a prior session “verified but deferred confirmation to the next PR”, that next publish IS the confirming experiment – watch the SPECIFIC check that was failing (not just “all green”), and record the before/after so a multi-session infra saga (diagnose -> fix -> confirm) is auditably closed. (S160, publish S159 – PR #66, the live #65 confirmation)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. S158 fixed issue #65 – two root codecov configs meant intended 1% threshold applied (default 0% effect, total-coverage dip failed codecov/project; empirically PR #64’s -0.18% dip 100%-covered patch) – consolidating single codecov.yml, verified config layer (codecov’s https://codecov.io/validate echoed threshold: 1.0 project patch). Learning 150 conceded full PR-level confirmation come “next PR coverage delta.” S160 published S159’s warning-muffle, adds 2 tests (small positive coverage delta) – first coverage-changing PR since fix. standard safe publish ran (pre-flight: clean fast-forward, merge-tree 0 conflicts, exact-commit + 8-file check; pushed; opened PR #66; watched checks via background gh pr checks 66 --watch), codecov/project came back PASS – versus FAIL PR #64 old two-config state. live experiment confirming #65 fix end--end (S156 diagnosed -> S158 fixed + config-layer-verified -> S160 PR-confirmed). merge used full carried discipline (don’t-merge-blind fresh re-check; AskUserQuestion irreversible merge; Learning-146 ancestor-gated reset --hard; verified-merged--delete branch cleanup gh api 404 check) – new wrinkle ; new lesson purely config fix counts confirmed. Reflexes: [config/threshold fix verified config layer verified, yet confirmed – confirming signal first live PR whose delta exercises check; prior handoff says “verified, confirm next PR”, next publish experiment, run deliberately][publishing PR confirms prior fix, watch SPECIFIC check failing (codecov/project), just “green” – record /(PR #64 FAIL -> PR #66 PASS) loop auditably closed][multi-session infra saga (diagnose -> fix -> confirm) “closed” live confirmation lands; update standing gotcha “X keep failing fixed” “X confirmed resolved (PR #N)” successors stop carrying stale warning]. Apply: session publishes first coverage/threshold-changing PR CI-config fix; “verified config layer, confirm next PR” carryover; closing multi-session infra fix.

what: Learning 153 – To DOCUMENT/EXPOSE an already-exported-but-undocumented function, the right artifact is a WEBSITE-ONLY Quarto article in the existing scripting series (vignettes/articles/*.qmd, which is .Rbuildignored) – NOT a NEWS line, and NOT a shipped CRAN vignette unless the owner asks. Copy a sibling article’s EXACT shape (genetic-value-analysis.qmd: YAML title:-only, a hidden setup chunk knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse=TRUE, comment="#>"), ## X {#sec-x} sections, then Key arguments + See also + References), and ALSO add the function to inst/_pkgdown.yml’s reference list(s) so pkgdown does not warn “topic missing from index”. Verify the EXECUTABLE doc in two steps: (1) run every intended chunk in-session under pkgload::load_all to confirm REAL outputs BEFORE writing prose; (2) render via quarto render <file>.qmd as the doc build-equivalent, then REMOVE the render litter (.html, _files/, the quarto-created .gitignore, .quarto/) – only the .qmd is tracked. A website-only article earns a CHANGELOG entry but NO NEWS entry (NEWS is for SHIPPED package changes; S116 precedent + [[news-vs-changelog]]), which also sidesteps the NEWS smart-quote/en-dash render traps (132/139) – keep the .qmd pure ASCII regardless. (S161, document/expose the offline focal-animal workflow)

what: Learning 154 – When the deliverable is “make X CONSISTENT across a SET of parallel docs/files”, it is ONE coherent deliverable (single theme + single definition-of-done), NOT bundling – but only if the items genuinely share that theme; stitching unrelated small carryovers together IS bundling (FM #18/#25). Do it in this order: (1) build the actual coverage MATRIX first by firsthand reads (FROM->TO) so “consistent” is a measurable target, not a vibe; (2) MATCH the existing convention – do not redesign the mechanism (here: bold-title prose mentions, not hyperlinks; switching to links would be a separate out-of-scope mode switch); (3) pick a single canonical order + a per-item description policy so consistency is verifiable, not just asserted; (4) verify completeness with a GREP over the matrix, not by eyeballing; (5) the build-equivalent is proportionate to WHAT CHANGED – a prose-only edit needs the markdown->HTML render to pass, not a re-derivation of unchanged simulation outputs. (S162, cross-link the five vignettes/articles/*.qmd scripting articles)

what: Learning 155 – A recurring render-time artifact that a standing gotcha tells you to “work around on every render” should be eliminated at the RENDERER-CONFIG SOURCE so the workaround is never needed again – this turns a standing gotcha into a CLOSED one. Here two NEWS render traps that S139/S147 had been hand-mitigating every time were fixed permanently with two NEWS.Rmd github_document knobs: html_preview: false (no top-level NEWS.html byproduct -> no “non-standard file at top level” R CMD check NOTE; Learning 139) and md_extensions: "-smart" (pandoc’s smart extension OFF -> source -- and straight quotes render to ASCII --/" instead of en-dash/curly; Learning 132). Three execution rules that made it clean: (1) VERIFY both knobs on throwaway /tmp Rmds BEFORE touching anything tracked – prove -smart yields ASCII output and html_preview:false suppresses the .html; you then present a verified plan, not a hypothesis. (2) Smart-off fixes FUTURE renders, but pre-existing LITERAL curly quotes baked into the SOURCE survive a re-render – find them with grep -nP '[^\x00-\x7F]' on the .Rmd (here NEWS.Rmd:189/:213) and fix each at the source. (3) When re-rendering to clean historical bytes, prove the regenerated file is CONTENT-INVARIANT by normalizing the OLD file’s smart-bytes back to ASCII (perl -CSD -pe 's/\x{201C}|\x{201D}/"/g; s/\x{2018}|\x{2019}/\x27/g; s/\x{2013}/--/g; s/\x{2014}/---/g; s/\x{2026}/.../g') and diffing vs the new render – the residual should be EMPTY or only a benign SOFT-WRAP shift (an en-dash is 1 column, -- is 2, so a line near pandoc’s ~79-col wrap can break one word later – same words, different newline; NOT a content change). The NEWS-encoding change earns a CHANGELOG entry, NOT a NEWS line (NEWS infrastructure is not a user-facing package change; [[news-vs-changelog]]). (S163, permanent NEWS render fix)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Owner picked . Crucially, grep -P '[^\x00-\x7F]' SOURCE showed NEWS.Rmd 2 lines (:189 lint_dir(+curlyR+curly), :213 curly Y/YES/…) LITERAL curly quotes – smart-re-render leave , needed source edit. Verified mechanism two /tmp Rmds touching repo: default output produced – none/“Y”, md_extensions:"-smart" + html_preview:false produced ASCII -- none/"Y" .html. branch fix-news-render: 4 edits NEWS.Rmd (2 YAML knobs + 2 source-curly lines), re-rendered (rmarkdown::render). Result: NEWS.md 0 non-ASCII (40), NEWS.html created, title block reproduced faithfully. Content-invariance proof: normalized old NEWS.md’s smart-bytes ASCII diffed vs new render -> residual one soft-wrap (Checking (---cran --run-donttest) ... system. – ---cran 4 cols wider old –-cran, pandoc wrapped “system.” next line; identical words). Package build-equivalent: R CMD build + R CMD check ---cran (exit 0) -> tarball NEWS.md (NEWS.Rmd/NEWS.html), top-level-NEWS.html NOTE, full testthat suite OK; 2 WARNINGs + vignette-index NOTE artifacts fast ---build-vignettes build, rest standing (archived--CRAN/new-submission) – zero NEWS-related findings. Reflexes: [eliminate recurring render/tooling artifact renderer-config SOURCE rather re-applying manual workaround time – standing “delete X / reword Y every render” gotcha signal close permanently][verify tooling-config fix throwaway files touching tracked files, phase gate presents proven plan hypothesis ([[consult-project-source--truth]]/verify-first)][smart-re-render fixes FUTURE output pre-existing LITERAL non-ASCII baked SOURCE survives – grep SOURCE [^\x00-\x7F] fix source][prove bulk re-render content-invariant normalizing OLD file’s smart-bytes ASCII diffing vs new render; expect EMPTY benign soft-wrap shift (en-dash 1 col vs -- 2 cols moves wrap), never new/changed words – Learning 132’s “confirm diff confined” made rigorous][NEWS encoding/infrastructure change CHANGELOG--NEWS ([[news-vs-changelog]])][scope build-equivalent honestly: ---build-vignettes fast check introduces vignette-index WARNINGs build-flag artifacts, regressions – say rather treating findings, note CI’s full build won’t ]. Apply: “fix render config / make rendered output clean” task .Rmd/.qmd/templated generated file; standing gotcha form “rendering X, delete/reword Y” – prefer closing config source; bulk re-render must prove content changed.

what: Learning 156 – When “make X a FIRST-CLASS column/field” lands in a pipeline that ALREADY retains unknown inputs (here qcStudbook() keeps any unrecognized header as a trailing novelCol via intersect(getPossibleCols(), cols) then c(cols, novelCols)), the discriminator between first-class and retained-but-orphaned is CANONICAL ORDER + DECLARED TYPE, never mere presence – so the RED tests must assert PLACEMENT (the new column sorts into its registry position, e.g. immediately after sex) and TYPE-COERCION (a factor input becomes character), because “is in the output” and “is character (from a CSV read)” already pass for a novelCol and would be false-GREEN. The minimal GREEN was two edits: add "species" to getPossibleCols() (which alone fixes presence + ordering, since the intersect orders by the registry) and one if (any("species" %in% cols)) sb$species <- as.character(sb$species) beside the sibling optional-column conversions (which fixes the factor case). (S165, issue #46 item 1 – species as a first-class column)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings

Owner chose item 1 . Two test-authoring bugs surfaced first RED run fixed declaring RED clean (discipline RED must fail RIGHT reason): (1) fixColumnNames() lowercases every header, zNovelNote fixture column became znovelnote match() returned NA – fixed using lowercase novel name; (2) full-pipeline JMAC test errored sire/dam conflict – replaced column-mapping assertion. RED failed cleanly 6 expectations right reason; GREEN (2 edits ) made pass full suite 0 failed / 0 errors; REFACTOR added roxygen \item{species} + document() (confined man/getPossibleCols.Rd). Scoped (deferred, evidence-based, guessed): speculative fixColumnNames() aliases (literal “species” header already normalizes) LabKey mapPedColumns species mapping (source column name unknown). Reflexes: [“make X first-class” pipeline already RETAINS unknowns, pin discriminator – canonical ORDER + declared TYPE – presence; presence/character--CSV already pass retained novelCol false-GREEN][read dependency ARROW issue’s words: “Dependency Y” means Y depends , depends Y – state back let owner correct scoping ([[observation-vs-decision]])][“multi-species/general version UNBUILT single-species thing” premature groundwork absent consumer -> defer, treat blocking dependency; confirm consumer’s code exists generalizing ][real example dataset can carry unrelated PIPELINE-HALTING defect (sire/dam conflict) – test LAYER CHANGED (column mapping) whole pipeline run, also keeps test honest proves][fixColumnNames() lowercases headers -> test fixtures “novel”/passthrough columns must use lowercase names match() silently returns NA – spurious RED, real one][RED must fail RIGHT reason: re-run, read failure, fix test-authoring bugs (NA matches, data-defect errors) declaring RED clean]. Apply: “promote/recognize field X first-class / canonical” task, especially unknown inputs already passed ; feature whose scope item references “multi-species/general version ” another issue – check issue’s CODE state first; test ingests real shipped dataset end--end.

what: Learning 157 – Two sharpenings of the publish convention from publishing a user-facing CODE change (vs the recent NEWS-infra-only publishes). (a) NEWS-vs-CHANGELOG for the CHANGE TYPE, not the session: when the session being published shipped a real user-facing package change (here a newly recognized + typed pedigree column) and the prior session deliberately DEFERRED the NEWS line as “a pre-publish decision”, the publish session’s FIRST step is to resolve it – per [[news-vs-changelog]] a user-facing code change earns a NEWS entry (not CHANGELOG-only like the S155/S163/S164 NEWS-infra publishes) – add the bullet to NEWS.Rmd, re-render to NEWS.md with the permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart" config (Learning 155; verify grep -cP '[^\x00-\x7F]' NEWS.md -> 0 and the diff is a confined pure-insertion), and COMMIT it on the feature branch so it ships WITH the feature in the same PR (one PR, not a follow-up). (b) A long gh pr checks <n> --watch is NOT a reliable terminal signal: it can die mid-run on a transient HTTP 401: Requires authentication and exit NON-zero, AND the harness background-completion notification can report “exit code 0” while the captured $? was 1 – a disagreement that can read as “all green” when ~half the matrix was still pending. Defensive “don’t merge blind”: never trust the watch’s exit; after it returns, re-query FRESH with a non-watch gh pr checks <n> and confirm EVERY check is pass with overall exit 0 (gh returns 8 while any check is pending) before the merge. (S166, publish S165 – PR #68, issue #46 item 1)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings

Owner pre-authorized “merge devel passes” -> fresh pre-merge re-check (MERGEABLE/CLEAN, head == d2ea5919, origin/master still 5f4bcbe9), gh pr merge 68 --merge -> merge commit 0574648b (verified state=MERGED firsthand); reconciled master via verified fetch + ancestor-gated reset --hard (Learning 146; gate asserted old-master 5f4bcbe9 merged-head d2ea5919 ancestors origin/master resetting); popped stub (pre-existing WIP dev stash untouched); verified-merged--delete branch cleanup (local + remote deleted, gh api 404). Reflexes: [decide NEWS-vs-CHANGELOG CHANGE publish carries, session kind – user-facing CODE change earns NEWS line even “admin/publish” session, prior session’s explicitly-deferred pre-publish content decision publish session’s first step, committed onto branch ships PR][re-render NEWS.md NEWS.Rmd NEWS edit, confirm 0 non-ASCII + confined insertion diff (Learning 155 config permanent)][--watch terminal signal: can die transient HTTP 401 completion notification’s exit code can disagree real one – re-query fresh non-watch gh pr checks <n> require every check pass + exit 0 (8 = still pending) merging][even owner’s merge pre-authorized, still run fresh pre-merge re-check (MERGEABLE/CLEAN + head + origin/master unchanged) – “don’t merge blind” VERIFICATION, gate owner removed prompt]. what: Learning 158 – When a per-key feature ships a lookup table whose only seeded value EQUALS the fallback default (here speciesGestation = one row, rhesus -> 210, with a 210 fallback), the feature is a no-op on shipped data, so a CONSUMER-level test cannot observe key differentiation and an “ignores the key, always uses the default” bug would pass GREEN – the consumer-boundary corollary of Learning 156 (test the discriminator, not presence). The fix that keeps the owner’s single-row table AND makes per-key behavior genuinely testable is to add a table-INJECTION parameter at the consumer boundary (getPotentialParents(..., gestationTable = NULL), threaded to getSpeciesGestation) so a RED test can inject a multi-row fixture with DISTINCT values plus a mixed-key fixture and assert the per-key path changes the output (here a RHESUS=210 vs TESTSP=90 mixed-species pedigree where one shared candidate dam is excluded for the 210 focal but retained for the 90 focal – a single fixed window cannot satisfy both). Two operational corollaries on consuming a NEW data/ object from a package function: (a) reference it by bare name and add utils::globalVariables("<obj>") to suppress the “no visible binding for global variable” R CMD check NOTE (precedent R/modSummaryStats.R:4); (b) lintr’s object_usage_linter resolves globals against the INSTALLED namespace, so a NEW exported function or NEW data object referenced across files throws a FALSE “no visible global function/binding” lint until you devtools::install(quick = TRUE) – re-install before trusting single-file lint, then the warnings clear. (S167, issue #46 item 2 – species-keyed gestation period)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Owner picked #46 item 2 (make 210-day rhesus-specific maxGestationalPeriod species-keyed now-first-class species column). read-4-agent grounding workflow + firsthand reads getPotentialParents.R/modPotentialParents.R established flow: single scalar window used per focal animal sire (:69) dam (:84-85) checks; species rides along ped nothing keys ; package ships reference data 24 data/*.RData objects (LazyData) built data-raw/, vs inst/extdata user site-config; real species strings “JAPANESE MACAQUE” (JMAC csv, falls back 210) rhesus (210 default). pre-RED AskUserQuestion gate let owner choose: exported data/ object, seed rhesus=210 + 210 fallback, optional arg + per-focal lookup. load-bearing realization: rhesus=210=fallback, shipped table makes feature observably identical today, consumer test “keys per species” false-GREEN ignores-species impl – hence gestationTable injection param, made mixed-species RHESUS/TESTSP discriminator test possible. RED: 10 helper expectations (test_getSpeciesGestation.R) + 3 getPotentialParents expectations, failing right reason (missing function/data object, missing arg default, unused gestationTable). GREEN (minimum): data/ object + build script + R/data.R doc, vectorized getSpeciesGestation (case/whitespace-insensitive match(), NA/unknown/empty -> default, integer) + globalVariables, getPotentialParents precomputing mgpVec (per-focal getSpeciesGestation(pUnknown$species, gestationTable) maxGestationalPeriod NULL, recycled scalar otherwise) mgp <- mgpVec[] loop. First lint pass flagged new cross-file symbols “visible global function/binding” – artifacts linting load_all install; devtools::install(quick=TRUE) cleared 0 lints. Verification: full suite 0/0, R CMD check 0 errors/0 warnings/1 NOTE – NOTE firsthand-proven PRE-EXISTING (S166’s “untyped” NEWS.md, wordlist gap; NEWS.md/WORDLIST/spelling.Rout.save unchanged session, none files contain “untyped”), change introduced zero new check findings. REFACTOR skipped (owner-approved): GREEN code already matched house style. UI prefill + NEWS entry + pre-existing spelling NOTE deferred (flagged, omitted). Committed issue-46-species-gestation; publish separate session. Reflexes: [per-key feature whose SHIPPED table collapses fallback (value == default) observably -op shipped data – consumer test can’t see differentiation “ignores key” bug passes GREEN; add table/lookup INJECTION parameter consumer boundary RED fixture can supply DISTINCT values + mixed-key case prove per-key path changes output (consumer-boundary corollary Learning 156)][test per-FOCAL keying, build mixed-key fixture ONE shared candidate included one key’s window excluded another’s, keep second always-valid candidate exclusion trip empty-set fallback][consuming NEW data/ object package function: reference bare + utils::globalVariables("<obj>") kill -visible-binding NOTE (precedent modSummaryStats.R)][lintr object_usage resolves INSTALLED namespace -> NEW cross-file functions/data objects throw FALSE lints load_all; devtools::install(quick=TRUE) trusting single-file lint][make new exported optional arg backward-compatible defaulting historical behavior (maxGestationalPeriod = NULL keys species, explicit integer recycles old single-window path – existing callers passing 210L unchanged)][verification NOTE surfaces check lives file touch: prove pre-existing firsthand (git blame / unchanged--working-tree / files don’t contain term) flag follow-rather silently fixing scope (FM #8)]. Apply: “key behavior X field Y via lookup” feature, especially shipped table small collapses default; package function consumes new data/ object; session whose verification surfaces NOTE untouched file.

what: Learning 159 – A prior handoff’s prescribed fix can carry a wrong MECHANISM; verify the fix PATH exists before following it. The S167 handoff (and its CHANGELOG/Learning 158) said the pre-existing R CMD check spelling NOTE (“untyped” in NEWS.md) should be cleared by “adding to inst/WORDLIST AND regenerating tests/spelling.Rout.save” – but tests/spelling.Rout.save DOES NOT EXIST in this package. The spelling check is spelling::spell_check_test(vignettes = TRUE, error = FALSE, skip_on_cran = TRUE) (tests/spelling.R) backed by inst/WORDLIST as the whitelist; the WORDLIST is the ONLY lever – there is no .Rout.save to regenerate. Adding the bare word “untyped” to inst/WORDLIST took the local fast R CMD check (--as-cran, no vignettes/manual) from 1 NOTE -> 0/0/0, confirming both the NOTE’s source and the fix in one run. Second, empirically grounded: spelling::spell_check_package(".") SKIPS markdown code spans (backtick-delimited inline code), so backticked identifiers are never checked and need no WORDLIST entry – only bare PROSE words are. Proof: two new NEWS bullets dense with NEW backticked identifiers (getSpeciesGestation, speciesGestation) plus prose (“macaque”, “whitespace”, “lookup”, “extensible”, “seeded”) added ZERO new flagged words; the only unrecognized word stayed “untyped” (S166’s bare-prose term, which sits outside backticks). So a user-facing NEWS edit normally needs no WORDLIST change as long as code is backticked and prose stays dictionary-clean – but RUN spell_check_package to KNOW the exact delta rather than predicting which words trip. (S168, publish S167 – PR #69, issue #46 item 2)
title: nprcgenekeepr — Project-Specific Learnings
text: happened. Owner picked option 1 (“publish S167”), directed “2 deliverables, 1” – single publish session (item 2b UI prefill stays owner’s-pick; bundling publish + TDD slice FM #18/#25). Three S167-flagged pre-publish steps, shipped one PR: () NEWS entry – dev-version Changes bullet species-keyed getPotentialParents() window (phrased honestly: shipped rhesus-table falls back 210, existing data unchanged; mechanism extensible part) + two New features bullets exported getSpeciesGestation() speciesGestation data object; re-rendered NEWS.md (permanent html_preview:false+md_extensions:"-smart", Learning 155) -> 0 non-ASCII, stray .html, confined pure-insertion diff. (b)/(c): BASELINE spell_check_package(".") editing flagged exactly “untyped” (NEWS.md:9 prose); edits STILL flagged “untyped” – code-span-skip held WORDLIST delta single word. Discovered tests/spelling.Rout.save exist (handoff’s “regenerate” step wrong assumption); added “untyped” inst/WORDLIST alpha position; re-ran -> 0 unrecognized; local R CMD check -> 0/0/0. Committed NEWS+WORDLIST (a89e9e2e), stash-carried S168 stub. what: Learning 160 – A Shiny reactive PREFILL (“default this input from loaded data, but let the user override”) has a testServer trap and a clobber trap; both are solved by moving the logic OUT of the side-effect. Trap 1 (testServer): in shiny::testServer (MockShinySession) an update*Input() call does NOT echo back into input$<id> – there is no browser round-trip – so input$maxGestationalPeriod stays whatever session$setInputs() last made it, and a test that “sets a pedigree, then reads the input to see the prefill” can NEVER observe the update; it is a structurally false test. Fix: do not assert the updateNumericInput side-effect. Factor the computation that matters into PURE helpers (firstPedigreeSpecies(ped) -> first non-NA/non-empty species or NA_character_; pedigreeGestationDefault(ped, gestationTable=NULL) = getSpeciesGestation(firstPedigreeSpecies(ped), gestationTable)) and expose the computed default as a reactive in the module’s returned list (gestationDefault), so testServer asserts session$getReturned()$gestationDefault() directly while the observer merely drives the (untested-by-assertion, exercised-for-coverage) side-effect. The shipped-table-collapses-to-default false-GREEN (Learning 158) recurs here too: bundled speciesGestation maps every species to 210, so differentiation is proven only with an INJECTED gestationTable (RHESUS=210 vs TESTSP=90) threaded through a new optional module param. Trap 2 (clobber / re-fire): the documented “dragon” – an unguarded observeEvent(data(), update*Input(...)) (precedent modBreedingGroups.R:429, which resets viewGrp to 1 every time groups reform) overwrites a user’s manual edit whenever the data reactive re-invalidates. Guard with a reactiveVal sentinel holding the last value the module wrote (lastAutoSet <- reactiveVal(<UI initial>)) and a pure decision helper prefillGuardAllows(current, lastAuto) = TRUE iff current is NULL/NA or == lastAuto (i.e. the user has NOT edited away from what we set); prefill + record lastAutoSet(newDefault) only when it returns TRUE. Critically, the observer keys on the DATA reactive (pedigree()), never on the input it writes, so the updateNumericInput -> input$ change does not re-trigger it (no loop). Architecture choice at the pre-RED gate: keep the STATIC UI numericInput + a server observer over a server-rendered uiOutput/renderUI – smaller blast radius, the existing value="210" UI test stays green, and adding a reactive to the returned list is safe because the existing list test uses %in% (subset), not identity. (S169, issue #46 item 2b – species-keyed UI prefill of the gestation window)

what: Learning 161 – The local fast R CMD check is NOT apples-to-apples with the PR CI matrix, and its extra WARNINGs/NOTE can be artifacts of the LOCAL tree + build flags rather than publishable-tree defects – prove each is outside the git tree before chasing it, and treat PR CI (which checks out the git tree on 5 platforms and builds vignettes) as the authoritative gate. Three concrete sources seen this session, none a real defect: (a) an untracked stray file with a non-portable name trips “checking for portable file names” – R CMD build sweeps in UNTRACKED files from the package dir, so a macOS/cloud-sync duplicate like tests/testthat/test_species_first_class 2.R (the ” 2” suffix, a space -> non-portable) lands in the tarball and WARNs locally, yet it is not in git so CI never sees it; ISOLATE by moving it aside (mv to /tmp) and re-running the check (WARNING -> OK confirms it was the sole delta), then restore it as-found (do not delete an uncommitted file without owner say-so, SAFEGUARDS); (b) --no-build-vignettes leaves inst/doc empty -> two spurious vignette WARNINGs (“Files in the ‘vignettes’ directory but no files in ‘inst/doc’” + “Directory ‘inst/doc’ does not exist”) – CI builds vignettes so they do not appear there; for a clean local read use devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) (suppresses the inst/doc check) or actually build vignettes; (c) --as-cran incoming-feasibility yields the STANDING archived-on-CRAN NOTE (“Package was archived on CRAN”, “New submission”) whenever the check reaches the network – permanent since 2025-07-29, not a finding. Net: a docs-only publish that returns “3 WARNINGs + 1 NOTE” locally can still be 0 publishable-tree findings; the proof is PR CI passing clean on the merge-result tree. (S170, publish S169 – PR #70, issue #46 item 2b)

what: Learning 162 – Renaming an EXPORTED package function is a public-API change: the safe, owner-approved shape is “add the new canonical name, keep the OLD name as a thin DEPRECATED alias” (both @exported), NOT a hard rename – the old name’s body becomes { .Deprecated("<new>"); <new>(args) }. .Deprecated() infers the OLD name from the call stack and builds a message naming the NEW one, so the RED test pins the deprecation by matching the NEW name: expect_warning(oldName(x), "newName") plus expect_identical(oldName(x), newName(x)) for behaviour-preservation; the new name’s own behaviour test (expect_identical(newName(3L), list(1L,2L,3L))) fails RED with “could not find function” until GREEN. Three rename-specific reflexes verified this session: (a) git grep IS the exhaustive inventory – it lists every tracked reference deterministically, so SPLIT the hits into must-change CODE (the def file via git mv, the in-package callers, roxygen @examples, the test call, NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated by document(), and the inst/_pkgdown.yml reference index – list BOTH names there since both stay exported+documented or pkgdown warns “missing topics”) vs DO-NOT-TOUCH history (SESSION_NOTES.md, *-cran-comments.md, docs/audits/*, TECH_DEBT_* describe the PAST state; rewriting them is scope creep). (b) A newly-added cross-file function throws a FALSE object_usage_linter “no visible global function definition for ‘’” in single-file lintr::lint() AND lint_package() until the package namespace knows it – object_usage resolves globals against the INSTALLED/loadable namespace, so the new symbol looks undefined while the sibling already-installed call (makeGroupMembers) resolves fine; pkgload::load_all() then re-lint -> 0, and CI installs before linting so it never fires there. Same root cause as Learning 158b (a NEW EXPORTED symbol -> false object_usage until install(quick = TRUE)); here load_all() suffices and devtools::check() does not run lintr at all. (c) A function NAME that lands in man-page PROSE (the alias page \title{Deprecated alias for makeGroupNum}) trips spell_check_package because titles are prose, NOT the \name/\alias/\usage/\code contexts the checker skips – the lever is inst/WORDLIST (matching the already-present grpNum, which is in a title for the same reason; insert in C-locale sort position) OR wrap the identifier in \code{}; verified 0-unrecognized after adding makeGroupNum. Build-equivalent devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE) (Learning 161) = 0/0/0 confirmed the whole rename clean; the new deprecation warning is captured by the alias’s expect_warning and does NOT leak into the suite’s warning count (confirmed by isolating warning sources to the pre-existing test_modPyramid.R baseline). (S171, issue #29 – rename makeGrpNum -> makeGroupNum)

what: Learning 163 – An umbrella / tracking issue accumulates COMPLETED children over time, so its body – and even a predecessor handoff’s suggested-next – can be STALE. Before treating “work on the umbrella” as a planning task, re-verify the umbrella’s OWN acceptance criteria firsthand against (a) each sub/related issue’s state (gh issue view <n> --json state,closedAt) and (b) the actual code/tests; a satisfied umbrella should be CLOSED with an evidence-backed comment, NOT re-planned. (S173, close umbrella #45)

what: Learning 164 – The Genetic Value Analysis has TWO independent rank paths fed by ONE upstream value, and the Shiny-displayed rank is NOT the library orderReport rank. reportGV computes indivMeanKin <- meanKinship(kmat) (R/reportGV.R:93) – the single choke point – then path (A) orderReport/rankSubjects (R/reportGV.R:150, R/orderReport.R:27-85) buckets + ranks for the library report, while path (B) the Shiny module OVERWRITES that rank with rank(report$indivMeanKin - report$gu) (R/modGeneticValue.R:204-206) for the table users actually see. CONSEQUENCE for any GVA-ranking change: fix at the choke point (the shared indivMeanKin) to move BOTH paths; a fix only in orderReport is invisible in the app. Verified the issue-#9 premise adversarially (real qcPed: top-20 GVA ranks are 100% founders, mean kinship 0.0027-0.0035 vs colony 0.0066) – missing-parent animals become U-id founders (addUIds/addParents via qcStudbook.R:198-199), get self-kinship 0.5 + zero to all others (kinship.R:77-88), so low mean kinship + high genome uniqueness (own gene-drop alleles) => false top rank. The noParentage bucket (orderReport.R:46-47) protects ONLY is.na(origin) & totalOffspring==0 & founder and shipped under issue #8, not #9. Two blast-radius boundaries: kinship() has 5 R/ callers (reportGV.R:87, modBreedingGroups.R:173, modSummaryStats.R:357, createSimKinships.R:58, cumulateSimKinships.R:61) so a GVA-only fix must stay at the reportGV level, NOT in shared kinship(); and test_orderReport.R:24,42 hard-codes the buggy ranking (countUnk(top 100)==34), so a correct fix breaks it BY DESIGN – update intentionally. (S174, plan issue #9)

title: Safeguards
text: AI agents operate well two distinct modes: Engineer Mode — Narrow focus. Implement specific feature, fix specific bug, write specific test. Head , executing clear spec. Architect Mode — Broad focus. Analyze codebase, plan feature, evaluate tradeoffs, design approach. Head , surveying landscape. danger zone transition modes. agent mid-implementation “notices” something improved, refactored, redesigned — acts without stopping — ’s catastrophic failures originate. engineer picks architect’s pen without putting wrench.

title: Safeguards
text: Never switch modes without commit boundary. ’re Engineer Mode see something needs Architect Mode thinking: commit current work first, switch. ’re Architect Mode want start implementing: get explicit approval first, switch. catch thinking “’m …” “also noticed…” — ’s mode switch happening. Stop. Commit. Ask.

title: Safeguards
text: Run touching one file: uncommitted changes previous session, touch . Report user ask proceed.
code: 1. git status                    — What's the current state? 2. git diff                      — Any uncommitted changes? 3. Commit everything clean       — No exceptions. Commit BEFORE starting. 4. Verify the build passes       — Don't start from a broken state. 5. State your scope explicitly   — "I am changing X, Y, Z. Nothing else."

title: Safeguards
text: phrases appear agent’s reasoning, must stop commit proceeding: “’m …” “also noticed…” “cleaner …” “good opportunity …” “Let also fix…” “really …” phrases signal mode switch happening. correct response : commit current work, discuss new scope user.

title: Safeguards
text: Re-read file immediately modifying . edit memory prior read. Memory degrades across long sessions — especially context compaction. haven’t read target section last 5 minutes, read now. applies code, documents, configuration files, artifact.

title: Safeguards
text: user may modify files outside session — another editor, sessions, manual intervention. Never overwrite user-modified content without confirmation. Check git blame system-reminders determine file modified user. doubt, ask regenerating.

title: Safeguards
text: Every project “build equivalent” — command confirms deliverable broken: Identify project’s build equivalent setup run every substantive change. deliverable doesn’t pass build equivalent broken, regardless correct looks source. documentation projects, rendering also verifies cross-references, citations, figure generation — failures indicate broken content must fixed committing.

title: Safeguards
text: Build success asset-use success. render can succeed silently using different assets configured: font family resolves Regular face missing Italic / Bold faces fall back default; CSL file resolves wrong style version; LaTeX template resolves missing class option silently ignored; figure-generation script imports library version different one specified. output looks valid build exits cleanly. defect invisible unless something checks . build produces rendered output depends external assets (fonts, citation styles, templates, figure libraries), build-equivalent check necessary sufficient. Two additional checks apply: deliverable compiles, renders, embeds right assets correct. deliverable compiles renders embeds fallback assets broken way build tell — find reader . See domain workstreams (e.g., docs/methodology/workstreams/RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION_WORKSTREAM.md research papers) toolchain-specific verification commands.

title: Safeguards
text: session crash, model upgrade, fresh start, follow checklist ORDER taking action:

title: Safeguards
code: 1. Read SESSION_NOTES.md          — What was the last session doing? 2. Check GitHub Issues (`gh issue list`) — What are the current priorities? (Fall back to BACKLOG.md if no repo. BACKLOG.md should contain only open work items — for history see `CHANGELOG.md`, for feature inventory see `ROADMAP.md`.) 3. Read SAFEGUARDS.md             — Refresh the rules (this file) 4. git status                     — What's committed? What's not? 5. git log --oneline -10          — What were the recent commits? 6. git diff --stat                — What's changed but uncommitted?

what: Step 2: Report (Tell the User What You See)
title: Safeguards
code: - "Here's the state I found: [summary]" - "These files are uncommitted: [list]" - "The last session was working on: [X]" - "Build status: [passing/failing/unknown]"

title: Safeguards
text: NEVER resume work previous session without completing three steps. new session inherit judgment context previous one. must verify everything scratch.
code: - "Should I commit the uncommitted changes?" - "Should I continue where the last session left off?" - "Should I start fresh on something else?"

title: Safeguards
text: Feature work: feat: [description] Bug fixes: fix: [description] Checkpoint commits: checkpoint: [done far] WIP commits: [WIP] [middle ] Checkpoint WIP commits optional luxuries. safety nets.

title: Safeguards
text: agent works best : Give narrow, specific tasks. “Add login form dashboard” perfect. “Clean frontend” disaster waiting happen. Demand commits scope expansion. agent says “also noticed X needs fixing,” response : “Commit first.” Every time. Don’t leave agent unsupervised broad tasks. ’re stepping away, give bounded task clear definition done. Watch scope creep language. “’m ” “cleaner ” red flags. Interrupt redirect. Use plan mode hard gate. agent wants change approach redesign something, make go plan mode. Don’t let plan execute breath. Set explicit session boundaries. “session, work X. Nothing else.” gives agent permission ignore things “notices” stay focused. session crash model upgrade, expect orientation step. agent report sees anything. jumps straight action, say “Read SAFEGUARDS.md orient first.” Trust verify. agent says committed, check git log. agent says tests pass, check output. Trust rebuilt verification, promises.

title: Safeguards
text: agent made mess (failed refactoring, deleted files, broken state):

title: Safeguards
text: let agent try fix mess. Compounding errors #1 cause data loss.

title: Safeguards
code: git status git log --oneline -20 git stash list git reflog (for recovering lost commits)

title: Safeguards
text: Can git checkout known good state? need cherry-pick specific commits? uncommitted work worth saving?

what: Step 4: Never Let It Happen Again
title: Safeguards
text: safeguard skipped? Add document ’s already .

title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: Across 11 functional areas audited, confirmed findings cluster around handful recurring themes: () Two coexisting, diverging Shiny applications — legacy monolith inst/application/ (server.R, ui.R) alongside newer modular Shiny modules R/. Logic maintained places, creating duplicate-maintenance tax standing risk two implementations drift apart. (b) Dead-code duplicate-variant accumulation — near-identical helper variants copy-pasted loop/aggregation bodies recur across kinship, genetic-value, group-formation cross-cutting clusters, inflating surface area review cost. (c) Hardcoded domain constants central schema / species profile — sex codes (M/F/U/H), minParentAge = 2, column-name lists embedded inline many call sites instead sourced one schema species-configuration object, blocks reuse species makes validation rules hard evolve. (d) Shiny concerns leaking core compute functions — UI / reactive / notification logic appears inside functions pure compute, coupling analytical core presentation layer hurting testability script-mode reuse. (e) Inconsistent error / return conventions — mix stop()/warning()/silent-NULL/return-value styles across QC pedigree code makes caller-side handling unpredictable. 60 confirmed findings across 11 functional areas (13 complexity, 19 duplication, 28 extensibility); 44 quick wins, 16 architectural overhauls.

what: LOOP-2 — getFocalAnimalPed mixes I/O dispatch, logging, error construction, and data munging in one 51-line function
title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: Location R/getFocalAnimalPed.R:32-82 · Severity low · Category quick-win · Regression risk low Cluster LOOP (Loops & Offspring Traversal) Evidence: Single function carries >=5 responsibilities: (1) excel-vs-csv format detection branched reading (lines 36-56), (2) interleaved flog.debug logging 4 points (33-35, 38-42, 51-55, 60-63, 69-75), (3) coercing first column character (57), (4) DB-failure error-list construction early return (59-68), (5) column renaming + NA filtering + date reformatting (76-81). two read branches (36-43 vs 43-56) differ reader call otherwise near-identical flog.debug message, branching adds length without adding distinct logic. Recommendation: Extract readFocalAnimalFile(fileName, sep) helper returns focalAnimals data frame (handling xls/xlsx vs csv branch logging internally), keep getFocalAnimalPed focused orchestration: read -> getLkDirectRelatives -> error-handling -> normalize. shortens top-level body isolates /O branch testing. Verifier: Confirmed R/getFocalAnimalPed.R (function body lines 32-82, exact). function combines xls/xlsx-vs-csv format detection branched reading (36-56), four interleaved flog.debug blocks, first-column coercion (57), DB-failure errorLst construction early return (59-68), column-rename/NA-filter/date-reformat normalization (76-80); two read branches differ reader call near-identical debug message, branching adds length without distinct logic. text: Location R/hasGenotype.R:27-47 · Severity medium · Category overhaul · Regression risk medium Cluster GENO (Genotype & Excel /O) Evidence: required-column contract (fuzzy ‘id’ column + ‘first’/‘second’ ‘first_name’/‘second_name’) independently encoded least five files. hasGenotype.R (27-47): length(cols) < 3, (stri_detect_fixed(tolower(cols), "id")), (tolower(cols) == "first"), (tolower(cols) == "second"). checkGenotypeFile.R (40-46): length(cols) < 3L, stri_detect_fixed(tolower(cols[1L]), "id"), (tolower(cols) %% c("first", "second")) (note: opposite intent — ‘first’/‘second’ columns FORBIDDEN, whereas hasGenotype REQUIRES ). getGVGenotype.R line 42 hardcodes ped[, c("id", "first", "second")]. fixGenotypeCols.R (14-19) hardcodes firstname/secondname -> first_name/second_name rename. addGenotype.R (24,32-33) hardcodes positional columns 2/3 emits columns literally named first/second. domain concept duplicated five times subtly different even contradictory rules. Recommendation: Introduce single source truth genotype schema (e.g. constant GENOTYPE_KEY <- "id" plus accessor functions genotypeAlleleCols(genotype) validateGenotypeColumns(genotype, mode = c("raw","encoded")) helper). checkGenotypeFile, hasGenotype, getGVGenotype, addGenotype call ‘id/first/second’ contract raw-vs-encoded distinction live one place drift. Verifier: Verified five citations source: hasGenotype.R 29/31/33/35, checkGenotypeFile.R 40/42/44, getGVGenotype.R 42 (hardcoded id/first/second), fixGenotypeCols.R 14-19, addGenotype.R 24/32-33 match exactly. genotype column contract genuinely restated across files, “opposite intent” claim holds: checkGenotypeFile FORBIDS first/second (raw input) hasGenotype REQUIRES (encoded), intentional raw-vs-encoded distinction nonetheless duplicates domain vocabulary. Severity lowered medium: real maintainability debt contained, stable, well-tested (test_hasGenotype 93 lines, test_checkGenotypeFile 79 lines, plus addGenotype/fixGenotypeCols tests getGVGenotype via test_geneDrop), correctness broad-coupling impact. Overhaul correct since schema source--truth plus accessor/validator helpers touches five functions contracts; existing coverage keeps regression risk medium.

what: XARCH-5 — Rigid pipeline threads fat data frames keyed by string column names, no validated seam
title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: Location R/qcStudbook.R:R/qcStudbook.R:24-56 (qcStudbook fn) 168-191 (col helpers); getRequiredCols.R:15-17; getPossibleCols.R:15-22; getIncludeColumns.R:5-9; reportGV.R:70-77 (mergeReportColumns) · Severity medium · Category overhaul · Regression risk medium Cluster XARCH (Cross-Cutting Extensibility & Architecture) (auditor cited 163-276; verifier corrected R/qcStudbook.R:24-56 (qcStudbook fn) 168-191 (col helpers); getRequiredCols.R:15-17; getPossibleCols.R:15-22; getIncludeColumns.R:5-9; reportGV.R:70-77 (mergeReportColumns)) Evidence: qcStudbook (276 lines) -> setPopulation/trimPedigree -> createPedTree.R:35-43 -> kinship.R + calcA.R -> reportGV.R -> groupAddAssign.R assume prior stage’s exact columns (id/sire/dam/sex/birth/gen/population/pedNum). Coupling purely column-name string convention; getRequiredCols()/getPossibleCols()/getIncludeColumns() return three different hardcoded name lists (getRequiredCols.R:24, getPossibleCols.R:48-54, getIncludeColumns.R:14-18) consumers must keep sync hand. reportGV.R:119 intersects getIncludeColumns() names(ped) validation required columns survived. Inserting new analysis stage swapping kinship backend means touching every downstream caller. Recommendation: Define lightweight S3 ‘pedigree’/‘gvReport’ class carrying data plus columnMap validator; stage accepts returns validates boundary. Consolidate three column-name lists one schema definition. Gives clean, swappable seams without rewriting callers. Verifier: Structural claim CONFIRMED: getRequiredCols/getPossibleCols/getIncludeColumns three separate hand-maintained hardcoded lists, pipeline (qcStudbook->setPopulation/trimPedigree->createPedTree->kinship/calcA->reportGV->groupAddAssign) couples purely via string-keyed ped$id/sire/dam/sex/gen/birth/population accesses S3 class validated seam, reportGV intersects getIncludeColumns() names(ped) required-column validation. However cited line numbers wrong nearly every reference: qcStudbook.R 2580 lines (276) function 24-56 163-276; getRequiredCols 15-17 :24; getPossibleCols 15-22 :48-54; getIncludeColumns 5-9 :14-18; createPedTree 27 lines (:35-43); reportGV intersection lines 71-72 :119. Medium severity fair (real extensibility friction, acute bug). Overhaul correct: remediation touches every pipeline stage consumer, although stage tests, broad blast radius makes regression risk medium stated.

title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: Findings generated parallel per-cluster auditors (11 clusters: QC, KIN, PED, GV, LOOP, GRP, GENO, APP, MISC, XDRY, XARCH), reading actual source functional area. Every medium/high finding underwent adversarial per-finding verification real code; verifier-CONFIRMED findings appear main sections, verifier-ADJUSTED severity category. Auditor line numbers sometimes drifted; verifier found line range inaccurate, corrected location shown authoritative. audit brief contained non-existent filenames, produced phantom findings; verification caught rejected (see Appendix B). audit . Implementing refactoring target separate, strictly test-driven (RED→GREEN→REFACTOR), one-deliverable-per-session effort.

title: Technical-Debt & Refactoring-Viability Audit — nprcgenekeepr
text: limitations audit run, conclusions code: PED (Pedigree Construction & Recoding) GV (Genetic Value Analysis & Reporting) produced ZERO findings auditor agents returned result — two functional areas effectively audited. Absence findings means “examined,” “clean.” re-run treating audit complete. Core files left unaudited include R/createPed*.R, R/recodePed*/addParents.R/addBackSecondParents.R (PED) R/reportGV.R, R/calcGU.R, R/rankSubjects.R, R/makeGeneticSummaryTable.R (GV) — though reportGV.R partially covered via XARCH cross-cutting findings. Appendix-B rejections caused transient tool/environment failures, verifier agent literally read file (“every tool call returned empty output”) therefore defaulted confirmed per fail-safe rule. substantive refutations — unverified, several likely real. Notably APP-11 (two parallel app implementations) rejected tooling failure independently CONFIRMED XARCH-1; GENO-2 (magic 10000L allele offset), APP-2/4/5 (monolith server complexity/magic strings), GRP-1/2/6 fall category worth re-verifying clean run. Net: treat 60 confirmed findings high-confidence floor, ceiling. targeted re-run PED, GV, tooling-failed verifications likely add findings.

code: focalAnimals <- breederPed$id[!(is.na(breederPed$sire) &   is.na(breederPed$dam)) &   is.na(breederPed$exit)] print(stri_c(   "There are ", length(focalAnimals),   " animals in the vector _focalAnimals_." )) breederPed[breederPed$id %in%   focalAnimals, c("id", "sire", "dam", "exit")][1L:10L, ] ##          id   sire    dam exit ## 1669 01QRQ4 VDBGDP TH7HTY <NA> ## 1743 CLSVU6 ULV9M7 SUFWJI <NA> ## 1887 1SPLS8 U9APLW 142GKP <NA> ## 1934 5IAFMK U4YSS5 WVE6Y4 <NA> ## 2072 HLQ9SY UI3RFL VEWC1E <NA> ## 2234 XFWVVX U3MFSZ L4LM1F <NA> ## 2337 6X6BG9 ENI6HX IUF0HC <NA> ## 2377 B228Q6 UEUIRJ CBSIAA <NA> ## 2378 B2CKHA ENI6HX WBFBR5 <NA> ## 2383 BCJJKN UA379T JPVAT3 <NA> breederPed <- setPopulation(ped = breederPed, ids = focalAnimals) nrow(breederPed[breederPed$population, ]) ## [1] 327 trimmedPed <- trimPedigree(focalAnimals, breederPed) nrow(breederPed) ## [1] 3694 nrow(trimmedPed) ## [1] 704 trimmedPedInformative <- trimPedigree(focalAnimals, breederPed,   removeUninformative = TRUE ) nrow(trimmedPedInformative) ## [1] 509 nonfocalInTrimmedPed <- trimmedPed$id[!trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals] length(nonfocalInTrimmedPed) ## [1] 377 allFocalParents <- c(   breederPed$sire[breederPed$id %in% focalAnimals],   breederPed$dam[breederPed$id %in% focalAnimals] ) trimmedFocalParents <- c(   trimmedPed$sire[trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals],   trimmedPed$dam[trimmedPed$id %in% focalAnimals] ) all.equal(allFocalParents, trimmedFocalParents) # Are the IDs the same? ## [1] TRUE notFocalNotParent <-   trimmedPed$id[!trimmedPed$id %in% c(focalAnimals, allFocalParents)] length(notFocalNotParent) ## [1] 187 unknownBirth <- breederPed$id[is.na(breederPed$birth)] knownExit <- breederPed$id[!is.na(breederPed$exit)] unknownBirthKnownExit <-   breederPed$id[is.na(breederPed$birth) | !is.na(breederPed$exit)] knownPed <- breederPed[!breederPed$id %in% unknownBirthKnownExit, ] otherIds <- knownPed$id[!knownPed$id %in% trimmedPed$id[is.na(trimmedPed$exit)]] print(stri_c(   "The living animals in the pedigree that are not in the trimmed ",   "pedigree are ", get_and_or_list(otherIds), "." ))

code: ped <- setPopulation(breederPed, NULL) probands <- ped$id[ped$population] ped <- trimPedigree(probands, ped,   removeUninformative = FALSE,   addBackParents = FALSE ) geneticValue <- reportGV(ped,   guIter = 50L,   guThresh = 3L,   byID = TRUE,   updateProgress = NULL ) summary(geneticValue) ## The genetic value report  ## Individuals in Pedigree: 3694  ## Male Founders: 141 ## Female Founders: 122 ## Total Founders: 263  ## Founder Equivalents: 241.84  ## Founder Genome Equivalents: 164.01  ## Live Offspring: 4052  ## High Value Individuals: 1593  ## Low Value Individuals: 729 trimmedGeneticValue <- reportGV(trimmedPed,   guIter = 50L,   guThresh = 3L,   byID = TRUE,   updateProgress = NULL ) summary(trimmedGeneticValue) ## The genetic value report  ## Individuals in Pedigree: 327  ## Male Founders: 3 ## Female Founders: 17 ## Total Founders: 20  ## Founder Equivalents: 109.67  ## Founder Genome Equivalents: 47.49  ## Live Offspring: 321  ## High Value Individuals: 233  ## Low Value Individuals: 94

code: names(trimmedGeneticValue) ##  [1] "report"          "kinship"         "gu"              "fe"              ##  [5] "fg"              "maleFounders"    "femaleFounders"  "nMaleFounders"   ##  [9] "nFemaleFounders" "total" names(trimmedGeneticValue$report) ## column names ##  [1] "id"              "sex"             "age"             "birth"           ##  [5] "exit"            "population"      "origin"          "sire"            ##  [9] "dam"             "indivMeanKin"    "zScores"         "gu"              ## [13] "totalOffspring"  "livingOffspring" "parentage"       "value"           ## [17] "rank" nrow(trimmedGeneticValue$report) ## Number of rows ## [1] 327 rpt <- trimmedGeneticValue[["report"]] rpt$indivMeanKin <- round(rpt$indivMeanKin, 5L) rpt$zScores <- round(rpt$zScores, 2L) rpt$gu <- round(rpt$gu, 5L) rpt <- toCharacter(rpt) names(rpt) <- headerDisplayNames(names(rpt)) knitr::kable(rpt[1L:10L, ]) # needs more work for display purposes. rpt <- trimmedGeneticValue[["report"]] kmat <- trimmedGeneticValue[["kinship"]] f <- trimmedGeneticValue[["total"]] mf <- trimmedGeneticValue[["maleFounders"]] ff <- trimmedGeneticValue[["femaleFounders"]] nmf <- trimmedGeneticValue[["nMaleFounders"]] nff <- trimmedGeneticValue[["nFemaleFounders"]] fe <- trimmedGeneticValue[["fe"]] fg <- trimmedGeneticValue[["fg"]] gu <- rpt[, "gu"] guBox <- ggplot(data.frame(gu = gu), aes(x = "", y = gu)) +   geom_boxplot(     color = "darkblue",     fill = "lightblue",     notch = TRUE, #| fig.alt: >     #|   Histogram of time between eruptions for Old Faithful.     #|   It is a bimodal distribution with peaks at 50-55 and     #|   80-90 minutes.      outlier.color = "red",     outlier.shape = 1L   ) +   theme_classic() +   geom_jitter(width = 0.2) +   coord_flip() +   ylab("Score") +   ggtitle("Genetic Uniqueness") print(guBox) mk <- rpt[, "indivMeanKin"] zs <- rpt[, "zScores"]

text: stated earlier can see types errors detected qcStudbook looking names returned getEmptyErrorLst() shown . defined . going use small imaginary pedigree listed multiple errors discuss pedigree error detection reporting. First note birth dates ego_id o4 (2006-04-13) purported sire s2 (2006-06-19). Note also purported birth date d2 birth dates offspring. Obviously dates IDs may incorrect. pedigree. (discuss column names shortly.) try convert pedigree file standardized studbook format, going get error message creation file R sessions temporary directory lists records generated errors. contents lowParentAge.csv shown . Examination ages parents reveals issues reported. can remove errors changing birth dates o4 2006-04-13 2015-09-16 d2 2015-09-16 2006-04-13. Note changes made column names original pedOne pedigree pedigree (ped) get qcStudbook. chosen limit displayed pedigree selecting ego_id’s id’s pedOne ped respectively. However, preferred method creating standardized studbook format qcStudbook examine errors found correcting proceeding. done explicitly requesting aspects inconsistent studbook format identified setting reportChanges reportErrors . discuss newly identified errors moment, however, let’s look shortening report, often interested trivial changes column names made qcStudbook cases simply choose report changes column names shown setting reportChanges . illustration, going bring back original copy pedOne see suspicious parents reported summary function. first two errors mentioned particular interest. Currently qcStudbook automatically changes sex dams F (female) sires M (male) reportErrors set .
code: names(getEmptyErrorLst()) ##  [1] "failedDatabaseConnection" "missingColumns"           ##  [3] "invalidDateRows"          "suspiciousParents"        ##  [5] "femaleSires"              "maleDams"                 ##  [7] "sireAndDam"               "duplicateIds"             ##  [9] "invalidIdChars"           "changedCols" knitr::kable(nprcgenekeepr::pedOne) pedOne <- nprcgenekeepr::pedOne # put it in the local environment ped <- qcStudbook(pedOne, minParentAge = 0.0) ## Error in `qcStudbook()`: ## ! Parents with low age at birth of offspring are listed in /tmp/RtmpBGSTrK/lowParentAge.csv. pedOne$birth_date[pedOne$ego_id == "o4"] <- as.Date("2015-09-16") pedOne$birth_date[pedOne$ego_id == "d2"] <- as.Date("2006-04-13") ped <- qcStudbook(pedOne, minParentAge = 0.0) ped[ped$id %in% c("s2", "d2", "o3", "o4"), ] ##   id sire  dam sex gen      birth exit  age recordStatus ## 2 d2 <NA> <NA>   F   0 2006-04-13 <NA> 20.2     original ## 4 s2 <NA> <NA>   M   0 2006-06-19 <NA> 20.0     original ## 7 o3   s2   d2   F   1 2012-04-11 <NA> 14.2     original ## 8 o4   s2   d2   M   1 2015-09-16 <NA> 10.8     original errorList <- qcStudbook(pedOne,   minParentAge = 0.0, reportChanges = TRUE,   reportErrors = TRUE ) summary(errorList) ## Error: The animal listed as a sire and also listed as a female is: s1. ## Error: The animal listed as a dam and also listed as a male is: d1. ## Change: The column where period was removed is: sire.id to sireid. ## Change: The columns where underscore was removed are: ego_id, dam_id, and birth_date to egoid, damid, and birthdate. ## Change: The column changed from: egoid to id. ## Change: The column changed from: sireid to sire. ## Change: The column changed from: damid to dam. ## Change: The column changed from: birthdate to birth. ##  ## Please check and correct the pedigree file. ## pedOne <- nprcgenekeepr::pedOne errorList <- qcStudbook(pedOne,   minParentAge = 0L, reportChanges = FALSE,   reportErrors = TRUE ) options(width = 90L) summary(errorList) ## Error: The animal listed as a sire and also listed as a female is: s1. ## Error: The animal listed as a dam and also listed as a male is: d1. ##  ## Please check and correct the pedigree file. ##   ## Animal records where parent records are suspicous because of dates. ## One or more parents appear too young at time of birth. ##   dam sire id sex      birth recordStatus exit  sireBirth   damBirth sireAge damAge ## 2  d2   s1 o2   F 2009-03-17     original <NA> 2000-07-18 2015-09-16    8.66   -6.5 ## 3  d2   s2 o3   F 2012-04-11     original <NA> 2006-06-19 2015-09-16    5.81   -3.4 ## 4  d2   s2 o4   M 2006-04-13     original <NA> 2006-06-19 2015-09-16   -0.18   -9.4

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: goal nprcgenekeepr implement Genetic Tools Colony Management. initially conceived developed Shiny web application Oregon National Primate Research Center (ONPRC) facilitate analyses perform regularly. enhanced capability Shiny application expose functions can used either interactively R scripts. work supported part NIH grants P51 RR13986 Southwest National Primate Research Center P51 OD011092 Oregon National Primate Research Center. present, application supports 5 functions: Quality control studbooks contained text files Excel workbooks pedigrees within LabKey Electronic Health Records (EHR) Creation pedigrees lists animals using LabKey EHR integration Creation display age sex pyramid plot living animals within designated pedigree Generation Genetic Value Analysis Reports Creation potential breeding groups without proscribed sex ratios defined maximum kinships. information see: Practical Approach Designing Breeding Groups Maximize Genetic Diversity Large Colony Captive Rhesus Macaques (Macaca mulatta) Vinson, ; Raboin, MJ Journal American Association Laboratory Animal Science, 2015 Nov, Vol.54(6), pp.700-707 [Peer Reviewed Journal]

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: Studbooks maintained breeding colonies generally contain information varying quality. quality control functions toolkit check ensure animals listed parents line entries, parents appropriate sex listed, animals listed sire dam, duplicate entries removed, pedigree generation numbers added, dates valid dates. addition, exit dates added possible consistent information departure dates death dates. Current ages animals still alive added database connection provided via configuration file user read permission LabKey server demographic data EHR (Electronic Health Record) module. See LabKey documentation. Parents ages user selected threshold identified. minimum parent age years set user used ensure parent least age birth date offspring. minimum parent age defaults 2 years. check performed animals missing birth dates.

what: Creation of Pedigree From a List of Potential Breeders and LabKey

what: How to Read the Age-Sex Graph
title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: age-sex pyramid breaks population male female genders age ranges. Usually, ’ll find left side pyramid graphing male population right side pyramid displaying female population. Along horizontal axis (x-axis) population pyramid, graph displays population either total population age percentage population age. center pyramid starts zero population extends left males right females increasing size, proportion population. Along vertical axis (y-axis), age-sex pyramids display two-year age increments, birth bottom old age top.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: interface organized three panels: Left Panel: Displays guidance documentation explaining use pedigree browser interpret data columns. Middle Panel - Focal Animals: Provides controls specifying subset animals focus analysis : Text Area: Enter animal IDs manually (one per line comma-separated). IDs can pasted directly Excel. Right Panel - Display Options: Display Unknown IDs: Toggle display unknown IDs (beginning “U”) created animals one known parent. Trim Pedigree: checked, trims pedigree include focal animals relatives, removing unrelated lineages. Export Button: Download current pedigree view CSV file.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: left panel contains controls configuring analysis: Gene Drop Iterations: Number iterations gene-drop simulation (default: 5000, range: 100-10,000). iterations provide accurate genome uniqueness estimates take longer compute. Calculate Genome Uniqueness: Toggle whether run gene-drop simulation estimate genome uniqueness values. Calculate Mean Kinship: Toggle whether compute pairwise kinship coefficients individual mean kinship values. Minimum Breeding Age: Slider set minimum age (years) animals considered breeding population (default: 2 years). Run Analysis: Click start genetic value computation.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: information panel explains key metrics: Mean Kinship: average kinship coefficient individual members population. Lower values better indicate animal less related population. Genome Uniqueness: proportion individual’s genome unique population based gene-drop simulation. Higher values better indicate animal carries rare genetic material.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: group formation process accomplished using algorithm determining maximal independent set (MIS). graph theory, maximal independent set largest set vertices graph two share edge. breeding group formation, vertices animals, edges kinships need considered. given group animals pairwise kinships, potentially many maximal independent sets, depending animals included excluded final group. order effectively sample set MISs, use random selection animals repeat MIS generation numerous times. allows us sample number MISs choose one best fits selection criteria. purposes, want largest group can formed set animals, none concerning relatedness . algorithm requires several pieces information: candidate animals matrix pairwise kinships candidate animals number groups desired list candidate animals number simulations run. * equivalent number random MISs generate compare. Information inter-animal relationships () ignored.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual

Since iteration gene-drop independent, process can vectorized element vector represents 1 iteration gene-drop simulation. vectorized version, population iterated , regardless number simulations desired. drastically reduces amount time necessary program run.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: basic steps gene-drop : founder assigned two unique alleles animal, find genotypes parents, select one allele parent randomly. every animal assigned genotype mendelian inheritance tally number unique alleles possessed member focal population. case algorithm, allow ‘uniqueness’ threshold adjusted allele can considered unique possessed N fewer members focal population.

title: Genetic Management Tools Manual
text: vectorized gene-drop simulation follows basic process described . difference instead dropping one allele time, repeating simulation N times, vectorized version drops N independent alleles one time. vectorized version, animal vector paternally inherited alleles vector maternally inherited alleles. offspring, random combination alleles produced dropped offspring process shown following figure: start simulation, founder assigned two unique founding alleles. N-element vectors created alleles, N desired number simulations. example , founder assigned unique founder alleles 1 & 2 5 simulations desired. time alleles need dropped parent offspring, unique transmission vector created representing whether allele passed offspring. vector generated contain random combination 0’s 1’s. animal’s paternally inherited alleles multiplied transmission vector, maternally inherited alleles multiplied compliment transmission vector. generate final set alleles received offspring, maternal paternal allele vectors added together. result vector alleles offspring received parent. allele vectors generated every animal pedigree, focal population can subset . Within population allele vectors, unique alleles can determined: position allele vectors (1:N) - Gather animal’s two alleles - number animals possessing allele equal , threshold, score allele unique (1) - Otherwise, score allele non-unique (0) every position animal’s two allele vector’s scored, sum scores animal divide total number alleles considered (2 * number simulations).
